Coast, Aug., 1891; ag. dist. commr. and sheriff, Accra, 1892; ag. col. treas., 1893; rec-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1894; represented Harbour Isld. in House of Assem., 1895-6; ag. col. sec., July to Oct., 1897, Apr. to Nov., 1898; admnr. of Dominica, Aug., 1899; ag. gov., Leeward Is., Aug., 1904, and from June, 1905; commr. and commr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., 31st Jan., 1906; gov., ditto, 18th Oct., 1907; gov., N. Nigeria, 30th Oct., 1909; gov. Leeward Is., 1912; gov., Mauritius, 7th Jan., 1916; assumed govt., 18th May, 1916; author of "Geography of the Gold Coast," "Obeah," etc.
BELL, JOHN FREDERICK, M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1883; bldg. inspr., F.M.S. Rlys., Dec., 1913; asst. engrn., Jan., 1917; dist. engrn., II, Jan., 1922; ditto I, Jan., 1926.
BELL, P. S., M.B., B.S., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926.

BELLAMY, WILLIAM EDWARD.—Ag. asst. comsry. of taxation, B. Guiana, 12th Sept., 1893; dist. comsry. and crown land offr., Pomeroon River, 26th July, 1895; 3rd grade comsry., I.R.B., treasy., 24th Mar., 1896; 2nd grade ditto, 22nd May, 1901; asst. inspr. of dista., L.G.B., 5th Sept., 1906; ag. sec., L.G.B., and inspr. of dista., 14th Dec., 1907 to 12th June, 1909, 1st Oct., 1910 to 23rd Nov., 1911, 24th Oct., 1912 to 14th Feb., 1914, and from 15th Mar. to 12th Oct., 1914.

BELLANTLI, J. J., LL.D., Malta Univ., 1910.—B. 1885; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric. (3rd place in hons. list), Malta Univ., 1902; olk., civil serv., Malta, 1907; mem. of coun. of govt., 1911; asst. crown advocate, Gozo, 1914; assessor of taxes, Malta treasy., Apr., 1923.

BELLETH, BENJAMIN, I.S.O. (1926).—B. 1876; ag. office asst., Kalutara Kach., Ceylon, July, 1918; apptd. to cls. V. of civ. serv., July, 1919; extra office asst. to the col. sec., Aug., 1920; off. asst. to col. sec., Apr., 1925; cls. IV., June, 1927; asst. col. sec., Sept., 1929; asst. contr., estab. lishments, July, 1931.
BEMINSTER, FREDERICK ARBOR.—B. 1886; tech. instr., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1914; supt., arts and crafts schls., 1920; inspr., 1930.

BENHAM, JOHN ELLIOTT.—B. 1886; major, 3rd E. Lance. Regt.; war service, German and Portuguese E. Africa, Mar., 1917 to Dec., 1918; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1914; dist. commr., Aug., 1922; asst.-col. sec., Jan., 1925; J.P., 1926; ag. senr. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun. on many occasions; ag. col. sec. in Mar. and June, 1928 and Sept. and Oct., 1930; gov.'s dep. on two occasions.

BENNETT, HON. SIR JOHN ROBERT, K.B.E. (1916).—M.H.A., St. John's West, Newf'dld., 1904; M.E.C., 1913; col. sec., 1913; mem., defence comtes., 1915; deputy chief censor, telegraphs and cables, 1914; press censor, 1916; min. of militia, 1917.

BENNETT, Rt. Hon. RICHARD BEDFORD, P.C. (1930). P.C. (Can.), LL.B., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. pub. and high schls., N.B., Dalhousie Univ., N.S.; hon. degree LL.D., Dalhousie Univ., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Alberta; admitted to bar of N.B., 1903; mem., legis. assem., Northwest Territories, 1898-1905; legis. assem. of Alta., 1909; rep. Calgary in H. of C., Canada, 1911-17; min. of just. and atty.-gen., 1921; min. of finance in Meighen adminstr., 1926; leader of conserv. party, Oct., 1927; prime min. of Canada after g.e., June, 1930.

BENNETT, SYDNEY CHRISTMAS, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.Inst. Mun. and Cty. Engrns.—Ed. Sherborne Schl.; asst. engrn., P.W.D., R.A.P., 1914-15; pioneer, E. A. Pioneers, 1915-16; 2nd lieut., E.A. Rly. Corps., 1916-17; lt., 1917-18; ment. in deeps., "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory med.; exec. engrn., Kenya, 1921.

BENNETTS, ALBERT THOMAS.—B. 1890; ed. Camborne Schl. of Mines, 1908-11; medallist of the Royal Cornwall Polytechnic Soc'y., 1910; 1st cls. hons. and silver medallist, City and Guilds of London Inst., 1911; 1st cls. diploma, Camborne Schl. of Mines, 1911; mines dept., F.M.S., 1912-25; asst. ward., mines, Perak, Nov., 1929.

BENSON, SIR J. HAWTREY, KT. B.A.C.H. (1912), A.B., M.D., F.R.C.P. Irel.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; 1st hons. maths., graduated, 1864; consulting physician to Royal City of Dublin Hosp., and to the Monkstown Hosp. Fellow of Acad. of Med., Ireland; late censor and examr., Royal Coll. of Phys., Ireland, and late external examr. in med., Dub. Univ.; is now med. adver. for Ireland to C.O.; author of numerous papers and articles on technical med. subjects; ret., 1926.

BENTINCK, LILLIE JOSEPHINE, M.B., B.Ch., certif. L.S.T.M.—Lady med. offr., Ratu Gajah, May, 1921; Ipoh, July, 1921; K. Palu, July, 1923; Talping, Apr., 1925; K. Kangsar, June, 1925; lady med. offr., infant welfare centre, Taiping, Aug., 1926; Seremban, July, 1926; K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; Ipoh, Nov., 1927.

BENTINCK, MAJOR WALTER GUY, BARON, C.M.G. (1912), D.S.O. (1902).—Ed. at Marlborough Coll., Jena Univ., Germany, and Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; joined Rifle Brig., 1885; capt., 1894; major, 1902; ret., 1905; intelligence dept., W.O., 1899; served throughout S. African war, 1899-1902; (ment. in deeps., Queen's medal with six clasps, King's medal with two clasps); dist. commr., Vereeniging, 1900; res. mag., Wakkerstroom dist., Transvaal, 1901-1907; Transvaal mem. of Natal-Transvaal boundary commr., 1902; sec. in office of high commr. for S. Africa, 1907; ag. Imperial sec., Aug., 1907, to Mar., 1908; asst. Imperial sec., Apr., 1908; chmn. Swaziland water commr., 1909.
BENZIES, DOUGLAS, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1889; ed. Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh; Edin. Univ., 1919-25; M.A., B.Sc.; France, June-Dec., 1918; demob., Jan., 1919; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.

BERENDSEN, CARL AUGUST, LL.M.—B. 1890; ed. Australian pub. schls., Gore Dist. High Schl. (N.Z.) and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. educ. dept., 1906; lab. dept., 1916; chf. clk., 1917; dep. registr., industrial unions, 1919; Imperial affrs. offr., prime min.'s dept., 1926; sec., external affrs., 1928.

BEKELEY, HENRY SGRAVE.—B. 1879; served in S. African War with Kitchener's Horse (med. and five clasps), 1900-1901; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1904; polit. offr., N Nigeria, 29th April, 1906; statn. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; pol. mag., July, 1921; ag. puisne judge, Apr. to May, 1923 and on various occasions since; puisne judge, Nov., 1928; ag. ch. just., July to Nov., 1930.

BERLYN, BERNARD HENRY ALFRED FORBES.—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and privately; Army chaplain (C. of E.), 1914; 2nd lieut., 4th Batt., Royal Irish Rifles, 1915; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; mem. of S.E. circuit; pol. mag., sup. ct., Gold Coast, 1923; ag. circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, 1925; 2nd puisne judge, sup. ct., Leeward Is., 1927; served on W. Indian ct. of appl., 1928; ag. ch. just., 1928; 1st puisne judge, 1931.

BERNE, CAPT. JAMES LEO, O.B.E. 1918.—B. 1885; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd Lieut., Royal Irish Regt., 1904; capt., 1910; served with Sierra Leone Batt. W.A.F.F., 1907-1912; served with Franco-Liberian bdray. coman., 1908 (recd. thanks of French govt.); served with Anglo-French bdray. coman., 1911; capt., R. of O.; asst. dist. comsnr., Somaliland Prot., Sept., 1912; dist. comsnr., March, 1914; served with Admiralty war staff, intell. div., Apr. to Aug., 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on the occasion of coronation of Empress Zauditu, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia, 1917; sen. asst. sec. to govt. of Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1921; ag. asst. ch. sec., Sept., 1922 to May, 1923, Dec., 1923 to March, 1924; ag. prov. comsnr., Apr., 1930 to May, 1931 and Feb., 1932; dep. prov. comsnr., Feb., 1931; mem. leg. coun., 1931.

BERTEAU, F. C., I.S.O. (1914).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1865-72; ent. Newfoundland civ. ser. as clk. H.M. Customs, 1878; collr. of cust., Labrador, 1881; clk. in col. sec.'s off., 1889; ch. clk., 1890; J.P. for col., 1893; mission to Ottawa to inquire into Canadian fin system, with view of applying same to Newfdld., May, 1898; audr.-gen. Newfdld., July, 1898; ag. cashier, Newfoundland savings bank, 1911.

BERTRAM, SIR ANTON, KT. BACH (1916); K.C. (1913)—Ed. at City of London sch., and Camb.; scholar of Caius Coll., 1887; Fellow, 1891; 1st cl. class. trip., part I, 1890; ditto, part II., 1891; Powis medal for Latin verse; Chancellor's medal for classics, 1891; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1892; president, Union Soc., 1891; Tandred law studentship, Lincoln's Inn, 1890; called, 1893; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1902; elected to H. of Assem., June, 1903; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1903; admtd. govt., Sept. to Oct., 1906; ag. ch. justice, Sep.-Nov., 1906; chrmn. of comsn. on the Out Is., 1906; puisne judge, Cyprus, 1906; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 19th May, 1911; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. ch. justice, Apr., 1918; ch. justice, July, 1918; services lent to govt. of Palestine, Dec., 1920 to May, 1921; ret., 1925; joint author, "Handbook to Workmen's Compensation Acts, 1897 and 1900."

BESSBOROUGH, 9TH EARL OF (creat. 1739), Rt. Hon. VERA BRABAZON PONSONBY, P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G. (1919), B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1880; 2nd lieut., Bucks. I.Y., 1905-8; temp. capt., Suffolk yeo. and adjutant, 1915; served in Great War, 1914-18; L.C.C., 1907-10; M.P., Cheltenham, 1910 and Dover, 1913-20; J.P., Co. Kilkenny; gov.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1931.

BEST, HON. SIR ROBERT WALLACE, K.C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1866; vice-pres. of the exec. com., Australian Commonwealth; leader of the Deakin govt. in the Senate, Feb., 1907; min. for trade and customs, C. of A., 1909; now retd.

BEST, SIR THOMAS ALEXANDER VANS, K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1870; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; R.A., 1894; Taylorian Univ. scholar; gold medallist of French Ministry of pub. instruction; astt., B. Cent. Africa admnstr., 1896; judicial offr., 1898; ag. ch. judicial offr., Nov., 1899, to Mar., 1900; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, Aug., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. dep. comanr., Apr. to Dec., 1903, and June, 1905, to Jan., 1906; dist. res., 1st cls., Apr., 1906; col. sec., Falkland Is., Oct., 1909; ag. gov., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; attended Impl. Educn. Conf., 1911; col. sec., Leeward Is., Jan., 1913; ag. gov., May to Sept., 1914, Apr. to Aug., 1915, and from Jan., 1916, to Mar., 1919; recd. thanks of H.M.'s Govt. for services during disturbances in Antigua in Mar., 1918; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. gov., June-Aug., 1920, June, 1921 to Jan., 1922, June to Oct., 1923 and from Aug., 1924; lieut.-gov. Malta, 1925; O.A.G. for various periods in 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1930.
BETANCOURT, NELSON.—B. 1887; sworn survr.; 2nd asst. inspr. of mines, Trinidad, 1st April, 1909; 1st inspr., mines, 17th Apr., 1921; ag. inspr., mines, May to Sept., 1927.

BETHEL, CHARLES PERCIVAL, I.S.O. (1929).—B. 1891; ag. 4th clk., post office, Bahamas, Apr. and Nov., 1909; cadet, Outislands, Feb., 1910; 3rd clk., C.S.O., and clk. to pub. bd. of wks., New Providence, Sept., 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Feb., 1914; ag. clk., C.S.O., May to Aug., 1919; ch. clk., C.S.O., Aug., 1919; enlisted for active serv., 1915, 2nd lieut., Mar., 1919; served in France; twice wounded; discharged from Army, Apr., 1919 resumed duties, May, 1919; ag. clk. in charge, col. sec.'s office, Aug. to Sept., 1923; ag. col. sec. and temp. mem., exec. coun., on various occasions, 1923-30 and 1926-30; ag. A.D.O. to admtr., Sept. to Nov., 1925; asst. col. sec., July, 1925; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1929-32; chmn., water and sewerage bd.

BETHELL, DONALD LESLIE.—B. 1895; ed. King Edward VI. Sch., Stourbridge; Canadian Exp. Force, 1914-19; asst. treas., Tanganyika Terr., Sept., 1919; senr. asst. treas., Somaliland, Apr., 1925; ag. treas., Somaliland in 1925, 1927 and 1928; treas., Apr., 1929; treas., Gibraltar, 1931.

BETHELL, WILLIS H.—B. 1875; pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1897; customs offr., Mar., 1901; ag. chief clk., audit dept., June, 1912; chief clk., audit dept., Apr., 1913; ag. auditor of pub. accts. for various periods between 1916 and 1923; audr., Dec., 1925; audr., pub. accts., 1st Dec., 1925.

BETTINGTON, DIGBY ROWLAND ALBEMARLE.—Ed. at United Serv. Coll., Westward Ho!; served with Natal Mounted Pol., 1898-1901; with 2nd Kitchener's Fighting Scouts, 1901-1902 (dangerously wounded, ment. in despatches, Queen's med. and 5 clasps); inspr., mil. pol., Cyprus, 1903; inspr., pol., Sierra Leone, 1906; ag. comanr., pol. and sheriff on several occasions; dep.-commdr. of pol. and dep.-supt., prisons, Gold Coast, 1910; base commdt., Togoland Field Force, 1914; "1914-15" Star, War and Victory med.; title changed to dep. inspr-gen. of pol. and prisons, 1916; inspr-gen., pol. and prisons, Gold Coast, 1917; inspr-gen., pol., Gold Coast on separation of depts. of pol. and pri-ons, 1920; King's Police med., 1920; ret., 1924; commdt., pol., Bahamas, 1924; resigned, 1926; chief of pol., Bermuda, 1927.

BEVIR, ANTHONY, M.A.—B. 1895; scholar of Eton, 1909, and of Hertford Coll., Oxford, 1914; M.A., 1923; served with 7th King's Liverpool Regt., July, 1915 to Apr., 1919; twice ment. in despatches; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 9th Feb., 1921; accompanied Mr. Ormsby-Gore on his visit to the West African Colonies, Jan.-May, 1926; pvt.-sec. to Mr. Ormsby-Gore, Feb., 1926; prin., 1st Apr., 1929.
BEWES, GEORGE PRIOR HOPE.—B. 1892; ed., Clifton Coll. and Royal Naval Coll., Osborne; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 26th Aug., 1920.

BEWLEY, HAROLD DE BEAUVOIR, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1886; ed., Osweyche Schr. and Trinity Coll., Dublin (jurur. mod., silver med.); asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1910; junr. asst. sec., S. Provs., 1914; A.D.C. to gov.-gen., 1915; pvt. sec. to gov.-gen., 1916 and in 1918-19; attd., Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; 2nd asst. sec., S. Provs., 1918; local commr., Br. Empire Exhibn., 1925; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1926.

BHARUCHA, NAVROJI MANEKJI.—B. 1890; B.A., Cantab. cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1914; office asst. to gov. agt., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1914; attd. Kurunegala Kach., June, 1914; extra office asst. to gov. agt., N.W. Prov., in addn. to his own duties, Aug., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, Apr., 1916; office asst., govt. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1917; Balapitiya, Nov., 1918; dist. judge, Tangalla, Mar., 1922; ag. dist. judge, Chilaw, May, 1922; asst. attm't. offr., May, 1925; dist. judge, Kalutara, Jan., 1927; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Apr., 1932.
BICKLE, W. H.—B. 1885; apptd. after compet. exam. olk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th Mar., 1906; cler. offr. higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., 4th Sept., 1929.

BICKMORE, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1911; attached to Colombo kach., Dec., 1911; col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1912; Galle kach., Nov., 1912; ag. office asst. to gov. agt., N.C. Prov., Feb., 1913; offr. asst. to gov. agt., W. Prov., Dec., 1913; pol. mag., Jaffna and Kayta, June, 1914; office asst. to gov. agt., Cent. Prov., Nov., 1914; pol. mag., Panadure, Aug., 1915; seconded for serv. as ag. finan. asst. to chmn., mun. coun., Colombo, Mar., 1916; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1917; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1917; on mil. duty, 1918; ag. dist. judge, Ke-galla, June, 1919; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1919; ditto, Matare, May, 1921; asst. col. treas., May, 1922; ag. col. treas., Sept. to Oct., 1926 and Jan. to June, 1930; dep. finan. sec., July, 1931; offr. cls. I, grade II, 1932.

BIDEN, CLAUDE ERIC DE BERK.—B. 1887; ed., Radley Coll.; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, 1911; on active serv., attd. Nigerian Overseas Contgt., German E. Africa, and W. African Serv. Brig., 1917-19; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, 1926; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1930; mem., rly. retrenchment comtees., 1931; ag. audr., May, 1932.

BIENEMAN, G. A. J., B.A.—B. 1889; ed., Dean Close, Cheltenham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; junr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, July, 1913; head of training coll., Oct., 1921; ch. inspr., educn., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1929.

BIGELOW, HENRY VEEDER, K.C., B.A., LL.B., M.A.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls., Truro, N.S., and Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1896; M.A., 1897; admitted to bar of N.S. 1896, practised law at Truro, N.S., and Regina, Sask.; K.C., Sask., 1913; judge, King's Bench, Sask., Feb., 1918.

BIGG, WILFRED JOSEPH.—B. 1897; boy clk., G.P.O., 22nd Nov., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. olk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to C.O., 1st Dec., 1914; on mil. ser. from 31st Aug., 1914, to 8th May, 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1922; asst. prin., 1st Aug., 1925; pvtv.-ec. to perm. under sec., D.O. (Sir Ed. Harding), 3rd Feb., 1930; ag. prin., 22nd June, 1931.

BIRCH, HUGH MASSEY, M.I.C.E.—Ed. privately and King's Coll., Ldn.; trooper, M.M. Pol., 1897; exec. engr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1907; local capt., E.A. Forces, 1916-18; exec. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1918; B.S.A. Coy.'s, Br. War and Victory med.
BIRCH, W. C.—Asst. analyst, E.A.P., July, 1914; senr. chemical offr., Apr., 1923.

BIRD, EDGAR CLARENCE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed., Xaverian Coll.; asst. ch., cust., Zanzibar Prot., Oct., 1914; ag. ch., cust., Jan. to Aug., 1915, May, 1916 to Feb., 1917, and Dec., 1917; marshal, H.B.M. Court for Zanzibar (in Prize), Jan. to Aug., 1916 and from Dec., 1917; immigr. offr., Sept., 1915; dep. comptr., cust., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1920; ag. comptr., cust., on various occasions, 1921-29; dep. comptr., cust., Nigeria, 1930; comptr., 1932.

BIRD, REGINALD.—B. 1893; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1919; 12th and 6th Batt., York & Lancaster Regt., 1914-19; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, June, 1920; asst. dist. Selandor, July, 1924; dist. offr., K. Pilah, July, 1925; asst. advr., Batu Pahat, Nov., 1929; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1929; ag. asst. treas., Penang, Dec., 1931.

BIRKETT, FRANK.—B. 1885; Imp. postal serv. (els.), 1898-1902, mails, 1903-07; admstve. (cler. and acctg.) 1908; staff record offr., 1909; postmr., Nyassaland Prot. (various offices) 1912; (active serv., 1914-15, N.V.R., Nthn. Rhod. Forces), ag. asst. P.M.G. various occasions; 1st cl. clk., 1920; ch. acct. and stkpr., 1923; P.M.G., Br. Guiana, 1927.

BIRKINSHAW, FRANK.—B. 1887; asst. agrl. supt. and so. mast., gram. schl., St. Vincent, Aug., 1912; agrl. instr., Mauritius, Jan., 1915; inspr., co-op. credit socs. in addn., Oct., 1915; agrl. supt., Sept., 1919; asst. agrl. inspr., Perak, F.M.S., June, 1920; agrl. field offr., Perak North, Dec., 1924; ditto P. Wellesley and Penang in addn., Apr., 1926; sr. agrl. field offr., Perak, Sept., 1931.

BIRSE, ARTHUR LOUIS.—B. 1893; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; colr., income tax, Singapore, Sept., 1921; treas., exec. cmtee., Br. Malaya, Br. Emp. Exhibn., in addn., Jan., 1923; recvr., Alsagoff Concession, Kubuh, Mar., 1926; offr., cl. III, June, 1930; ag. asst. treas., Penang, Nov., 1930; ag. asst. adviser, Kemaman, Trengganu, July, 1930.

BIRT, DUNKY THOMAS.—B. 1881; ed., Uppingham and Univ. of Durham; M.B., B.S., Durham, 1905; certif. L.S.T.M.; house surg. and supt., Grey Hosp., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1908; med. offr., Nigeria, West African med. staff, July, 1913; senr. med. offr., Mar. 1927, and transfd. to Sierra Leone; senr. med. offr., Gambia, July, 1929; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

BIRTWISTLE, WILLIAM.—B. 1890; asst. naturalist (fisheries), min. of agr. and fisheries; directed research in dept. of oceanography, univ., Liverpool; fisheries economist, S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1924; offr.-in-ch., fisheries dept., Apr., 1928.
BISCOE, CECIL JULIAN TYNDALE, M.C., B.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1895; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, Trent Coll., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

BISCOE, VINCENT FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. at Cheltenham Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., June, 1908; 2nd lieut. London Rifle Brig. (Territorial Force), June, 1909; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1910; ass't res., N. Nigeria, 2nd Aug., 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 5th Dec., 1919; ag. station mag., 29th Dec., 1924.

BISHOP, W. W.—B. 1878; ed. Glasgow Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; asst. engnr., Cape govt. rly., 1903; dist. engnr., Baro-Kano rly., 1908; dist. engnr., 2nd grade, open lines, Nigerian rly., 1916; dist. engnr., 1st grade, 1919; ch. engnr., 1928.

BLACK, ARCHIBALD MAULE.—Ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; ent. civ. serv., Cape Colony, 1898; addnl. asst. regnr., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1903; asst. regnr., 1904; regnr. and taxing mast., sup. ct., Grahamstown, 1921; regnr. and taxing mast., sup. ct., Cape Town and high sheriff for prov. of Cape of Good Hope, 1928; on active service, 1914-19; ment. in despatches; awarded V.D., 1921; major, Union R. of O. (arty.).

BLACK, CAPT. THE HON. GEO.—B. 1873; ed. at pub. schls., N.B.; el., mem. of Yukon coun. three times, 1905-11; comantr., Yukon Territory, 1912-16; capt., C.E.F., 1916-19; el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1921; re-el., 1925, 1926 and 1930; el. speaker of H. of C., 1930.

BLACK, JOHN ALAN.—B. 1891; cadet F.M.S., 1914; offr., cls. V., 1917; offr., cls. IV., 1921; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; asst. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Dec., 1926; ag. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1929; do., Dec., 1930.

BLACK, JOHN GRAHAM.—B. 1896; cadet, S.S., May, 1920; pol. mag., asst dist. judge and dep. regnr., sup. ot., Malacca, 1920; passed cadet, June, 1921; coroner, Malacca, Mar., 1923; collr., land rev., May, 1923; offr., cls. V., May, 1923; priv. sec. to O.A.G., S.S., May, 1925; asst. dist. offr., Tapah, June, 1926; ditto, Kroh, Feb., 1927; offr., cls. IV., May, 1927; asst. advr., Trengganu, Nov., 1927; res., Labuan, Feb., 1930; offr., cls. III., May, 1932.

BLACK, MAJOR KENNETH, F.R.C.S. (Eng.)—B. 1879; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Apr., 1921; surg., native hosps., Aug., 1921; ophthalmologist, gen. hosp., in addn., Sept., 1921; prof., surgery, med. coll., S'pore, Apr., 1922; ag. snr. surgn., S'pore, Aug., 1923, Feb., 1926, June, 1928 and Apr., 1931.

BLACK, RONALD LESLIE.—Cik., deeds, Cape, 1891; cik., master's office, 1892; cik., deeds, 1894; dept. audr., rev., 1897; cik., master's office and deeds office, 1898; examr., deeds, 1901; dep. asst. regnr., deeds, 1919; asst. regnr., deeds, 1920; regnr., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1927; ditto, Pretoria, Oct., 1928.
BLACK, THOMAS.—B. 1894; supt., accts. and corresp., work development dept., Hong Kong, 1925; acctnt., treas'y., 1926.

BLACK, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. pub. and high schls., Ont.; grad. in agr., Toronto Univ.; editor, "Farmers' Advocate," Winnipeg, 1902-04; dep. min., agr., Manitoba, 1904-06; pres., Manitoba Agr. Coll., 1906-15; sec., economic and development comsn. of Canada, 1915-16; commr. of agr., Canada, 1916-18; chmn., soldier stintm. bd., 1918-20; dep. min., immigr. and coloni- zation, 1921; man., colonisation dept., Canadian National Rly., London, 1923; ch. colonisation dept., C.N. rlys., Montreal, 1924.

BLACKABY, ERNEST JAMES, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1902; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; house surg., R. Infirmary, Sunderland, Feb., 1924; res. surg. offr., dist. infirmary, Ashton-under-Lyne, 1924; house surg., London Lock Hosp., 1925; asst. med. offr., Tonbridge Infirmary, 1926; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, July, 1928; ag. surg. specialist, June to Oct., 1929 and July, 1930; med. offr., E.A.M.S., Nyasaland, 1931.

BLACKALL, HENRY WILLIAM BUTLER, B.A., L.L.B. (1st place).—B. 1889; ed. Stonyhurst and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Victoria prizeman, jurisprudence and internat. law; 1st of 1st cls. hon., mod. history; John Brooke scholar, King's Inns; called to Irish Bar, 1912; crown counsel, E.A.P., Oct., 1919; M.L.C., Apr., 1920; crown counsel, Nigeria, May, 1923; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1923-31; mem. coman. on riots, S.E. Provs., 1930; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Jan., 1932.

BLACKMORE, HARRY, O.B.E. (1933), V.D.—Ed. at St. Bartholomew's Schl., Wednesbury; Science and Art Schl., Walsall; St. John's Coll., York, and Victoria Univ., Leeds; senior asst. St. Bartholomew's Schl., Wednesbury, 1900; English and music mast. and pupil teacher, Centre and Higher Grade Schls., Walsall, 1904; head mast., Riccall Schl., York, 1905; normal mast., B. Guiana, July, 1908; headmast., primary schl., G. Coast, 1912; inspr. of schls., 1914; seconded to G. Coast Regt., 1917; rtnd. to civ. duty, 1918; ag. dir. educn., Mar., 1924; ch. inspr., schls., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; ag. dir. educn., Aug., 1924; dir., 1931.
BLACKWELL, BERNARD FLETCHER.—B. 1906; ed. Woodford Acad., N.S.W.; r. Australian Navy, 1924-30 (paymr.-lieut.); ret., 1930; corres. clk., New Hebrides, Sept., 1931.

BLACKWELL, CAPT. FRANCES.—B. 1885; Impl. P.O., 1901-13; asst. supt., posts and tels., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; on mily. serv., 1914-21; personal asst. to dir., posts and tels., F.M.S., Feb., 1921; supt., posts and tels., S.S. and F.M.S., Oct., 1926; contrlr., ditto, Jan., 1929; contrlr., Perak and Dindings, Nov., 1929; ag. contrlr., posts and tels., S'pore., Nov., 1930.
BLACKWOOD, A. McA., M.B., Ch. B. (Glasgow).—B. 1885; lieut., R.A.M.C., May, 1917; capt., 1918; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Jan., 1930.

BLACKWOOD, JOHN ALEXANDER.—B. 1874; ed. Dollar Academy, and at Heriot-Watt Coll., Edinburgh; articled to Scottish solr., 1892; Impl. mil. rlys. (now I.S.A.R.), 1901; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1902; asst. regnr. of inscribed stock, 1914; head of insurance and checking dept., 1919; asst. sec. to W. African Currency Bd., 1912-1914; dep. head, stores dept., 14th Nov., 1921; head, shipping dept., July, 1927.

BLAIR, HON. ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—B. 1876; ed. Wellington Coll. (N.Z.), and Canterbury and Victoria Univ. Colls.; judges associate, 1933-36; admitted barrister and solr., 1899; experience in legal offices, 1896-1910; in practice, 1910-28; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1928.
BLAIR, DOREEN JOSEPHINE BERNARD.—B. 1898; ed. Ladies' Coll., Eastbourne, Ladies' Coll., Cheltenham, Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Oct., 1927.

BLAIR, HON. SIR JAMES WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1930)—B. 1871; called to bar, 1894; M.L.A., Queensland, for Ipswich, 1902-15; atty.-gen. in Morgan and Kidston ministries, 1903-7; also sec. for mines for most of that period; sec., pub. instr., Denham ministry, 1912-15; puisne judge, Apr., 1922; ch. just., Queensland, 1925.

BLAIR, ROBERT WESTRUP, A.R.C.S.I., F.I.C.—B. 1885; ed., George Watson's Coll., Edin.; R. Coll. Sc., Ireland; asst. chem., Imp. Inst., 1905-6; asst. chem., Metro. Water Bd., Lond., 1906-11; 1st asst. chem., Wellcome Trop. Research Lab., Khartoum, Sudan, 1911-12; asst. chem., Inst. for Med. Research, Kuala Lumpur, F.M.S., 1912; chem., Inst. for Med. Research, Kuala Lumpur, F.M.S., 1913; 2nd lieut., East Lancs. Regt., 1915; tempy. lieut., 1916; sp. brigade R.E., 1916; ment. in desp., 1918; ag. capt., R.E., 1918; chem., Inst. for Med. Research, Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1919; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Dec., 1922; 1st lieut., July, 1924; extra A.D.C. to high commnr., Nov., 1924; ch. chem., Inst. for Med. Research, Mar., 1927; capt., M.S.V.R., May, 1927; hon. A.D.C. to high commnr., Mar., 1930.

BLAKELEY, DONALD QUENTIN.—B. 1875; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apr., 1898; ag. regiar.-gen.'s off., May, 1906, to Mar., 1907; 1st cls. clk., regiar.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1907; ag. regiar.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; commnr. of sup. ct., B. Hond., Mar., 1908; ag. regiar.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909, and Aug., 1912.

BLAND, EDWARD MALTEY, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1878; ed. Trin. Coll. Sch., Port Hope and Royal Mill Coll., Kingston, Canada; employed on Can. Pac. and Can. Northern rly., 1897-1904; served in S. African war in Strathcona's Horse (medal and three clasps); asst. engrnr. Imp. mil. rly., S. Africa, 1900-1902; ditto, C.S.A.R., 1902-1908; Baro Kano rly., 1918, and acted as dir. of rly., N. Nigeria, Feb. to Oct., 1912; asst. engrnr. in chief, Nigerian rly., 1st Jan., 1913; special mission to B. Guiana to report on proposed rly. development, 1913; engrnr. in chief, Nigerian Eastern rly. construction, Feb., 1914; dep. gen. man., Nigerian rly., eastern div., 1916; gen. man., Gold Coast rly., 1917; gen. man., Nigerian rly., Nov., 1918.

BLANDFORD, HON. SYDNEY D.—B. 1868; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Bonavista, 1904, 1908 and 1909; M.E.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1909; M.L.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1914; sheriff of Newfoundland, 1917.

BLANDY, CAPT. R. D.—B. 1891; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; ent. Indian pol., 1911; Indian Army, R. of O., Apr., 1915; active serv., N.W. Frontier and Mesopotamia; returned Indian pol., Jan., 1919; dist. supt., pol., 1920-24; ch. of international pol., Tangier, July, 1928; asst. to res. commnr., New Hebrides, Sept., 1929; ag. res. commnr., New Hebrides, Aug., 1930, to Oct., 1931.

BLANKENBERG, SIR R. A., K.B.E. (1920), O.B.E. (1918).—Rhodesian service, 1895 to 1902; conf. clk. to lieut. gov., Transvaal, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Aug., 1902; ch. clk. to agt.-gen., in London, 1st June, 1907; ch. clk. to high commnr. for Union of South Africa, London, 1910; asst. sec. to high commnr., 1912; sec. to high commnr., 1919; ag. high commnr., 1920; ret., 31st Mar., 1926.

BLAXTER, KENNETH WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Malvern and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); mily. serv., France and Italy, 1915-19; asst. prin., miny. of transport, 1920; sec., royal comm. on compensation for suffering and damage by enemy action, 1923; asst. prin., C.O., 1924; pvtc. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state for the cols., 10th Dec., 1928, pvtc. sec. to parly. under sec. of state for cols., 17th Sept., 1929; ag. prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

BLEASDELL, ERNEST HAMILTON.—B. 1887; ed. Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1912; ag. dist. commnr., Dec., 1912 to Feb., 1913, June to Dec., 1913, July, 1914 to June, 1915, Nov., 1915 to 1917, and from July, 1917; dist. commnr., 19th Aug., 1918.

BLEDSLOE, 1ST BARON (cr. 1918) RT. HON. SIR CHARLES BATHURST, P.C. (1926); K.H.E., (1917).—B. 1867; ed. Sherborne, Eton, Univ. Coll., Oxford and Royal Agrl. Coll., Cirencester (gold medallist and late chmn. of govt.); chancery barrister and conveyancer, 1894-1910; M.P. South West Divn., 1910-18; mem. of coun. of Duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec. to miny. of food, 1916-17; chmn., R. comm., on sugar supply, and dir., sugar distribution, 1917-19; parly. sec. to miny. of agr., 1924-28; chmn., R. comm., land drainage, England and Wales, 1927; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 1930.

BLONDIN, LIEUT. COL. THE HON. PIERRE EDOUARD.—B. 1874; ed. Seminaire de Nicolet and Laval Univ., Montreal; 1st to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1908; re-el., g.e., 1911; el. dep. speaker, 1911; min. of inland rev., 1914; sec. of state, 1915; P.M.G., Canada, 1917; overseas, 1917-18; apptd. to senate, 1918; re-apptd. P.M.G., 1920; speaker of senate, 1930.

BLOOD, HILARY RUDOLPH ROBERT, M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914-1919; tempy. employed at C.O., Mar.-Dec., 1919, Aug., 1924 to May, 1925 and Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; cadet, Ceylon, Jan., 1920; attd. to Columbo Kach., Feb., 1920; Anuradahapura Kach., Mar., 1920; ag. office asst., Anuradahapura Kach., Nov., 1920; attd., Kandy Kach., Apr., 1921; office asst., govt. agt., Cent. Prov., June, 1921; 4th ast. col. sec., Apr., 1922; 3rd ast. col. sec., June, 1925; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1926; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1930; col. sec. and regiar. gen., Grenada, 1930; O.A.G., Windward Is., June to Aug., 1930; admtd. govt., Grenada, on several occasions.

BLOXHAM, CAPT. HUGH FITZHERBERT.—B. 1893; ed. Bradfield Coll.; 4th batt., Wiltshire Regt., 1914-15; 102nd K.E.O. Grenadiers, I.A., 1915-24; served, Mesopotamia, 1915-19 and Afghan War, 1919-20; twice ment. in desps.; ast. supt., pol., Hong Kong, Feb., 1924; ast. supt., pri-ons, Apr., 1925; ast. supt., prisons, Apr., 1925; ast. supt., pol., Mar., 1926; ast. supt., prisons, Mar., 1929; dep. dir., prisons, Gold Coast, Oct., 1930; prison supt., S.S., 1932.

BLUMER, REV. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A., B.Sc., Dipl. Ed.—B. 1889; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; Caird schol. in chem.; B.A., 1910, 1st cls. hons., Latin, 2nd cls. hons., matha.; B.Sc., 1910; M.A., 1912; dipl. ed., 1921; deacon, 1912; priest, 1913; curate, St. John's, Parramatta, 1912-17; curate, Holy Trinity, Dulwich Hill, 1918; astt. mast., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1921-26; vice-prin., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.
BOAST, HENRY WALTER.—B. 1887; clk. Swazi-land, 1909; regiar. and mast., sup. ct. and sheriff, 1928; dep. ast. commnr., 1930.

BODENSTEIN, HELGARD DEWALD JOHANNES, LL.D. (Leyden), barrister-at-law, Middle Temple.—Cllk., mag.'s offices, Krugersdorp, Lydenburg, Pietersburg, 1909; Piet Retief, 1910; sec., judge pres., J. de Villiers, 1910-12; ch. clk. and professional astt. to atty.-gen., Bloemfontein, Apr., 1912; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Amsterdam, Aug., 1912; astt. edr., "Die Burger," 1920; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1922-1927; sec. to prime min. and for external affrs., 1927.

BODILLY, COMMDR. RALPH BURLAND, R.N.—B. 1884; ed. Bradfield Coll. and H.M.S. "Britannia," 1899; midshipman, China station, 1901; sub. lieut., Atlantic Fleet, 1905; lieut., Home Fleet, Jan., 1907; invalided, 1909; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1913; on war serv., Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; lieut. commdr., Jan., 1915; promoted commdr. for war services, Nov., 1918; res. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston et al. on various occasions, 1927-31; acted in appellate ct. on various occasions, 1928-31; pres., marine and water cts.; ch. mag., Palestine, Oct., 1932; author of "Interaction between Vessels."

BODKIN, GILBERT EDWIN, F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1886; ed. at Repton Schl., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1908; Cambridge Univ. diploma of agric., 1910; govt. economic biologist, dept. of science and agric., Br. Guiana, 1911; Carnegie scholar in economic entomology, and as such toured the prin. agrl. stations of the United States, 1912-1913; rep. Br. Guiana at W. Indian Agrl. Conf., 1913; astt. dir. of sci. and agr., and dep. chmn., bd. of agr., Br. Guiana, 1919; editor, jnl. of the bd. of agr., 1918; joint editor, Br. Guiana Handbook, 1921; govt. entomologist, Palestine, June, 1922; offr.-in-ch., locust campaigns, 1928-30; pres., Internat. Locust Bureau, Damascus, 1930-31; dir., agr. prin., coll. of agr. and regist., co-op. credit socs., Mauritius, 1932; author of numerous papers on the insects of Br. Guiana and Palestine, scientific rept. to govt., etc.

BOILEAU, LEWIS HAROLD.—B. 1888; ed. Monkton Combe Schl., Bath and Garrick Chambers (Schoones); astt. dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. prin. astt. sec., Apr., 1923; ag. prin. astt. sec., S. Prov., in 1925, 1926 and 1927; admstve. offr., cls. I, Aug., 1930.

BOK, DR. WILLEMS EDUARD, JUNR.—Priv. sec. to prime min., Transvaal, June, 1907; sec. to prime min. and clk. to exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; law advdr., Feb., 1916; sec. for just., Aug., 1918; judge, high ct., S.W. Africa, Feb., 1930.
BOLAND, FREDERICK MALCOLM, LL.B.—Called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; mag., County of St. Patrick, Trinidad, Mar., 1926; mag., eastern dist., Jan., 1927.

BOLTON, ARTHUR, B.A., Dipl.—B. 1897; ed. Rugby and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class. exhibir.); B.A. (hons. chem.), 1924; dipl., educn., 1921; lab. astt., H.M. facty., Sandycroft, 1916; research, Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1921; served in France, 1917-18; wounded; lieut., O.T.C., 1923; astt. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.
BOLUS, PERCY REGINALD, M.B. (Lond.).—Astt. col. surgeon, Falkland Is., 1904.

BONAVIA, WALTER JOSEPH.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-1916; clk., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1917; astt. polit. offr. (cadet), Oct., 1920; admstve. offr., 2nd grade, Oct., 1923; ag. ditto, 1st grade, Feb. and Nov., 1923 to Aug., 1925; ag. dist. offr., in 1926, 1928, 1929-31; dist. offr., Oct., 1931.
BOND, MAJOR B. W., M.C.—Astt. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

BOND, RALPH NORMAN, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1900; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; addt. Puttalam kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, Hambantota kach., Oct., 1924; office astt., Badulla kach., Jan., 1926; pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1930; astt. govt. ast., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1931.

BONE, MAJOR CHANDOS ERIC, M.C., F.S.I., L.S. (Vic.).—B. 1889; survr., 1st grade, rev. surveys bch., Perak, Oct., 1913; lieut., R.F.A., May, 1916; capt., Apr., 1918; major, Aug., 1918; astt. supt., rev. surveys, Nov., 1920; supt., surveys, Trengganu, May, 1926; astt. supt., surveys, S'pore., Sept., 1927; ag. supt., surveys, Malacca, June, 1929.

BONYUN, E. C. B.—B. 1885; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados and privately; copyist, pol. mag.'s courts, Dist. "A," Barbados, Jan., 1903; 2nd clk., registn. off., Barbados, Sept., 1905; ag. dep. registr., from Jan. to Feb., and Sept. to Nov., 1910; ah. clk., registn. off., Nov., 1913; supervisor of customs, Nigeria, July, 1914; astt. recvr., sup. ct., Nigeria, for the purpose of winding up affairs of German and Austrian firms, Nov., 1914; hon. astt. sec. to West Indian Contingent Comtee. (whilst on vacation leave of absence, and with permission of the S. of S.), Sept. to Dec., 1915; ag. supt. of customs, 1st grade, Feb., 1916; astt. censor, Bonny, Feb., 1916; censor, Bonny, May, 1916; ag. recvr. of enemy estates, Nigeria, June to Dec., 1916, and Jan., 1917; collr., 2nd grade, headmst., Jan.-Mar., 1917 and Sept., 1917, to June, 1918; hon. sec., comtee, Nigerian comforts for overseas forces, Sept., 1917 to June, 1918; in charge of Port Harcourt cust., June-Nov., 1918; ag. supt., 1st grade, Degema, July, 1919 to Jan., 1920; ag. collr., Lagos, Feb.-May, 1920; ag. senr. collr., Lagos, May-July, 1920; collr., Victoria, July, 1921.

BOOCOCK, JOHN JAMES.—Enlisted in Cape Mounted Rifles, 1897; forester, Transkeian conservancy, Cape, 1903; supt. of plantations, 1904; astt. dist. forest offr., Umtata, Feb., 1905; Butterworth, Dec., 1908; Kambi, Oct., 1909; Umtata, Apr., 1912; dist. forest offr., Kologha, Oct., 1914; conservator of forests, Natal Conservancy, May, 1920; served with Cape Mounted Rifles during Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; served with S. African Heavy Art'y., Apr., 1917 to June, 1919; conservator, Eastern conservancy, 1926; do., Western conservancy, 1931.

BOON, HERBERT.—Copyist, treasy. dept., St. Kitts, May, 1908 to Mar., 1910; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Apr., 1913; ch. clk., post office, St. Kitts, May, 1915; clk. to admstr. and clk. exec. and leg. couns., 1st Deor., 1924; has held various ag. appts.
BOOTH, LEONARD HORACE VERTE.—B. 1896; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Dec., 1919; astt. supt., pol., Feb., 1922; ag. astt. harbmr., Pol., 1922 and Feb., 1926; astt. dir., criminal intelligence, 1931.
BOOTH, PERCY, N.D.A., U.D.A.—Senr. supervisor, agrl. dept., Kenya, May, 1923.
BOOTH, R. W.—B. 1879; stock. inspr., Basuto-land, 1911; sub.-inspr., Basuto-land Mounted Pol., 1915; inspr., 1920; staff offr., 1929.

BORDEN, RT. HON. SIR ROBERT LAIRD, P.C. (1912), G.C.M.G. (1914), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1854; ed. Acadia Villa Acad., Horton, N.S.; called to the bar, 1878; head of firm Borden. Ritchie and Chisholm, Halifax; pres. Nova Scotia Barristers' Society, ten years; Q.C., 1900; LL.D., Queen's and St. Francis-Xavier Univs.; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g. e. 1896; re-elec., 1900; defeated g. e. 1904; re-elec., bye-elec., 1905, and g. e. 1908, 1911 and 1917; leader opposition in H. of C. 1901-1911; president of the King's privy council for Canada and prime minister, Oct., 1911; prime minister and S. of S. for external affairs; mem. of the Peace Conference, Versailles, 1919, as representative of the Dominion of Canada; resigned premiership, 10th July, 1920; Canadian rep. to Armaments Conf., Washington, 1921; chan., Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont., 1924.

BORG, ANTHONY J., M.D., Malta.—B. 1889; medical offr., Fiji, 1916; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Ra., 1917; ag. R.M.O., med. supt., pub. lunatic asylum and visiting med. offr., Suva gaol, 1919; D.M.O., Nadroga, 1920; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Nadi, 1921; ditto, Levuka, 1923; ditto, Labasa, 1924; D.M.O. and dist. comntr., Ra (conjoint), 1929.

BOSHOFF, STEPHANUS PETRUS ERASMUS, M.A., D.Litt.—B. 1891; ed. Paarl Boys' High Sch., Grey Univ. Coll. and Univ. of Amsterdam; teacher, 1912; prof. of Afrikaans and comparative philology, Potchefstroom Univ. Coll., 1915; ditto, Univ. of Cape Town, 1930; dir., educ., Transvaal Prov., 1932.

BOSKER, WALTER LONSDALE, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1913; asst. engr., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1913; asst. engr., irrign., May, 1915; ag. exec. engr., Kuala Langat, Oct., 1916; ditto, Seremban, Apr., 1921; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1921; ditto, head office, Selangor, Oct., 1921; exec. engr., wks. and bldgs., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1924; ag. senr. exec. engr., Seremban, Apr., 1927; senr. exec. engr., F.M.S., July, 1928; ditto, Klang, Jan., 1929; ag. state engr., Trengganu, Jan., 1930.
BOSTOCK, J. K.—B. 1892; ed. Repton; L. & N.W. Rly., Crewe, 1909; on mil'y serv., 1914-19; divnl. supt., Nigerian rly., 1928.

BOSTOCK, MAJOR H. E., O.B.E. (Mily.), R. of O.(R.E.), M.I.C.E., B. 1878; ed. Marlborough and Univ. Coll., London; asst. engr., Gold Coast harbrs. and Sierra Leone harbr., 1909; Br. Army, France, R.E. (ment in desps.), 1915-19; res. engr., Lagos harbr. wks., 1921; port engr., harbr. dept., Lagos, 1923.

BOSTOCK, WILFRED CRAMER.—B. 1879; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate; served, S. African War, Imp. Yeomanry; asst. engr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1908; transf'd., Baro-Kano rly., Jan., 1909; res. engr., constr., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1914 to Dec., 1915; asst. ch. engr., Nigerian rly., Jan., 1918; gen. man., Sierra Leone rly., Nov., 1919; ch. engr., Nigerian rly., Jan., 1922; dep. gen. man., Feb., 1924.

BOSTON, HUGH MERRICK.—B. 1893; ed. Wallasey H.E. Sch., New Brighton, Liverpool Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Liv.), 1925; served with 2nd W. Lancs. (98th County Palatine) Field Ambulance (T.F.), 1914-19; med. offr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Apr., 1928.

BOTHA, CHRISTIAN LAURENS.—B. 1870; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein and at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Amsterdam and K.C., 1919; puisne judge, Orange Free State divn. of sup. ct., S. Africa, 1926.

BOTHA, COLIN GRAHAM, V.D., M.A.—B. 1883; ent. atty.-gen.'s dept., Cape Colony, 1901; serv. in offices of civ. comntr., Cape, and mast. of sup. ct.; incharge, archives of Cape Col., 1912; ch. archivist Union of S. Africa, 1919; accompanied Sir Reginald Blankenburg as pvt. sec. to first assembly of League of Nations, Geneva; hon. or corresponding mem. of several hist., lit. and genealogical socs. of Gt. Britain and Holland; contributed articles to various jnl., mags. and newspapers; author of "Place Names in the Cape District," 1917; "A Brief Guide to the Documents in the Cape Archives, 1652-1806," 1918; "The French Refugees at the Cape," 1919; rept. of tour to various archives in Europe, Canada and U.S.A., 1921; "Social Life in the Cape Colony in the 18th Century," 1927; "Place Names in the Cape Province," 1927; "Public Archives of South Africa, 1652-1910," 1928.

BOTTOMLEY, SIR WILLIAM CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.K. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Owens Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., entrance maj. schlr., 1896; schlr., 1898; B.A. and 3rd wrangler, 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam.as 2nd cl. clk., 22nd Oct., 1901; sec. to col. surv. comtee., 1905; sec. to interdeptl. pensions comtee., 1907; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 16th Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to Sir F. Hopwood, 1st Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Lucas, 24th Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Emmott, 26th Oct., 1911; 1st cls. clk., 1913; principal clk., 8th Dec., 1917; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., E. African Currency Bd., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 1927.

BOUCHER, Cyril Haslewood.—Headqrs. offr., armed nat. constab., B.N. Guinea, Apr., 1906; ast. res. mag., central div., Jan., 1906; mag. for native affairs, Jan., 1906; J.P., Jan., 1906; visiting justice, Port Moresby gaol, Mar., 1906.
BOULDERSON, G. H. C.—Ast. dist. comntr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; 1st grade adm'tive. offr., Jan., 1924.
BOUR, EDOUARD FRANCOIS.—M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P. Lond.; L.S.A. Lond.; govt. med. offr., Plaines Wilhelms dist., Mauritius, 1907; medical officer in charge H.M. troops, Mauritius, 1916-19.

BOURDILLON, SIR BERNARD HENRY, K.B.E. (1931), C.M.G. (1924).—Ed. Tonbridge and St. John's Coll., Oxford; ast. mag., United Provs., India, Nov., 1908; under-sec. to govt., Mar., 1913; reg'ar., high ct. Allahabad, May, 1915; apptd. to I.A.R.O., Sept., 1917; jt. mag. (seconded), Mar., 1918; judl. ast., Persian Gulf, June, 1918; settlement offr., Baghdad, Mar., 1919; polit. offr., Baquba, Oct., 1920; polit. sec. to high comntr., Jan., 1921; dep. sec. to high comntr., Dec., 1921; sec. to high comntr., Jan., 1922; col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1929; O.A.G., Ap. to Sept., 1930; ch. sec., July, 1931; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Uganda, 1932.

BOURNE, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1884; joined S.S. pol., Oct., 1906; inspr., pol. in admn., June, 1911; passed sp. law exam., June, 1913; ch. ct. inspr., July, 1913; coroner, Sept., 1920; J.P., Feb., 1922.

BOURNE, JOHN DUNBAR MACDOWALL.—B. 1886; examiner, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1906; ass't auditor, N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; ass't audr., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1920; audr., Somaliland Prot., July, 1921; audr., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1923; senr. ass't audr., Nigeria, July, 1927; audr., Sierra Leone, 1930.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLEYN, KNT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1854; Gilchrist scholar (London Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with 2nd class honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Linc. Inn, Nov., 1876; solr.-gen., and escheator-gen. of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of comm. to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1896; ag. legal asst., C.O., Dec., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902; ret., 1912.

BOVELL-JONES, LIONEL CLIFFORD.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp.; enlisted 1915; served in France, Feb., 1916-Aug., 1918; demob., 1919; sub-insp., pol., British Guiana, July, 1920; dist. insp., 1924; offr.-in-ch., punitive expdn. to Venezuelan bdry., 1926; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1927; county insp., 1931.

BOWEN, F. C. P.—B. 1896; copyist, audit dept., B. Honduras, 1912, also depts. of col. sec. and regisr.-gen.; 3rd cls. clk., atty.-gen's off., 1915; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1918; clk. to regisr.-gen., 1919; sec'y, riot inquiry commn., 1919; ag. regisr.-gen. and cust. enemy ppty. and contrl., L.C.O., 1920; sec'y, yellow fever inquiry commn., 1921; ag. regisr.-gen., 1921, 1923 and 1925; regisr.-gen., regisr., lands titles, supt., vice-adm'y. and bankcy. cts., companies, patents, births, marriages and deaths, 1927; J.P.; stamp commr., dir., pub. offr. guarantee fund; commr., income tax, 1927; sec. in arbitr. between govts. of U.S.A. and Guatemala, 1930.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1848; ret. comdr., R.N.; entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean; E. coast of Africa, E. Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was apptd. priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, gov. Cape of Good Hope, and high commr. for S. Africa; imperial sec. to the high commr., 1884-97; colonial sec. Mauritius, 1898; adminstr. govt. on various occasions, 1900-09; ret. 1910.

BOWERLEY, WALTER, C.B.E., (1931), B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb. (class. tripus, 1899); apptd. clk., col. audit branch of E. and A. Dept., Nov., 1901; ass't audr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1902; ag. ass't treas., Oct., 1902; ag. local audr. in 1903, 1904, 1905 and of amalgamated territories, Lagos—S. Nigeria, 1906-7; ag. local audr., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1907, to Mar., 1908; local audr., Gambi, Apr., 1908; local audr., G. Coast, Nov., 1909; title changed to audr., 1910.

BOWMAN, HUMPHREY ERNEST, C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. mod. hist. 1902); B.A., 1902; M.A., 1906; ent. Egyptian miny. of educn., 1903; seconded to Sudan govt. (dept. of educn.), 1911-13; dir. Egyptian students in U.K., 1913-14; serv. with Br. Army, 1914-20; B.E.F., France, 1915-16; staff capt., H.Q., N. Command, 1916-18; India, 1918; Mesopotamia, 1918-20; ret. with rank of major, 1920; seconded from M.E.F. as dir. of educn., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; seconded to govt. of Palestine, 1920-23; dir. of educn., Palestine, 1920; mem., advisory coun. since 1920; chmn., comm. on crafts and industries, 1922; mem., transiliteration commn., 1923; chmn., comm. on transiliteration of geographical names, 1924.

BOWRING, ARCHIBALD GEORGE.—Ed. Nottingham High Schl. and Nottingham Univ. Coll.; capt., Sherwood Foresters, 1914-19; miny. of lab., Nov., 1919 to Nov., 1920; col. audit dept., Nov., 1920; ass't audr., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; do., Sierra Leone, Mar., 1930; ag. audr., May to June, 1930.

BOWRING, SIR CHARLES CALVERT, K.C.M.G. (1925), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1908).—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; col. audit branch of exchequer and audit dept., 1890; Hong Kong, Dec., 1892; loc. aud., B. C. Africa, Sept., 1895; loc. aud., East Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., June, 1899; treas., E. A. P., Oct., 1901; mem., exec. and leg. couns., E. A. P., 1907; ch. sec. to gov., E. A. P., Apr., 1911; ag. gov'r., Feb. to Oct., 1912, Aug. to Oct., 1913, Apr., 1917 to Jan., 1919, and from Nov., 1919; gov., Nyasaland, 1923-1924.
BOWRING, HON. SIR EDGAR RENNIE, KT. BACH. (1916).—B. 1858; mem. legis. coun., Newfoundland, 1897; high commr. for Newfoundland in London, 1919.

BOWRING, WALTER ANDREW, C.B.E. (1928).—Ed. at Eton; clk., col. audit branch, 1st Dec., 1894; served in Cyprus, 1896; ass't. auditor, E.A.P. and Uganda Rly., 13th Feb., 1899; ag. local auditor, Uganda Prot., 1899; ag. local auditor, Somaliland Prot., 1900, and in 1902; local auditor, Uganda Prot., 31st May, 1902; treas., Cyprus, 11th Nov., 1909; mem. exec. and legis. couns., Cyprus, 1909; chief refugee commr., Cyprus, 1915 (during war); passed exam. in Turkish, Feb., 1919; treas., Gibraltar, 1924; admstr., Dominica, July, 1931; mem., fedl. exec. coun., Leeward Is., July, 1931.

BOWYER, ERIC BLACKLOCK.—B. 1902; ed. Whitehill Schl. and Glasgow Univ.; open scholarship, 1920; B.Sc. (with distinction), 1923; G. A. Clarke school, 1923; M.A. (1st cls. hons. in maths. and nat. philosophy), 1924; ass't inspnr., taxes, 1926; appt. to C.O. as ass't prin. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; joint sec., col. survey commt., 1927; seconded for serv. with colonial development commt., 1st Oct., 1930; pvt. sec. to parl'y. under-sec. of State for Dominion affrs. (Mr. Lunn), 22nd June, 1931; do. (Mr. Malcolm MacDonald), 3rd Sept., 1931.

BOYCE, E. A., B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1898; ass't engrnr., P.W.D., Uganda Prot., 1925; ass't engrnr. in charge Mbale dist., 1926; ditto, N. Prov., 1927; dep. D.P.W., Bahamas, Aug., 1928; ag. D.P.W. in 1930 and 1932.

BOYD, EDMUND B.—B. 1894; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen; M.A. 1916; 1st cls. hons. classics; on military service, 1916; with 60th siege batt., R.G.A., France and Belgium, Nov., 1917, to June, 1919; 2nd lieut., Oct., 1917; lieut., Apr., 1919; temporary clk. in C.O., Sept., 1919; apptd. by civ. ser. commn. under reconstrn. scheme 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; ass't prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm't. under-sec., May to Sept., 1921; sec. inter-deptl. commt., income tax in cols., 1922; priv. sec. to parl'y. under-sec. (Mr. Ed. Wood), 4th Apr., 1922; (Mr. Ormsby Gore), 31st Oct., 1922; sec., Imp. Inst. commt. of enquiry, 1923; priv. sec. to Lord Arnold, 26th Jan., 1924; ag. prin., 12th Dec., 1924; prin., 2nd Nov., 1925; sec., C.O. confere., 1927; sec., col. development advisory commt., 1929; pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the Col. (Lord Passfield), 1st Apr., 1930; pvt. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas as S. of S. for Dom. Affrs. and for the Cols., 26th Aug., 1931; do., to S. of S. for the Cols. (Sir P. Cunliffe-Lister), 9th Nov., 1931.

BOYD, PETER FERGUSON.—B. 1893; telegraphist, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1906 to Dec., 1911; postal astt., N. Rhodesia, June, 1912; ag. ch. astt., Nov., 1916 to Apr., 1917; astt., post office, Apr., 1920; ag. sec., Jan., 1925 to Apr., 1926; ag. dep. P.M.G. Jan.-Sept., 1927; ag. P.M.G., Sept.-Dec., 1927; col. postmr. and supr., tels., Fiji, Jan., 1928; P.M.G., 1930.

BOYD, ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. (1912), B.A. (1913); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; on war serv., 1915-19; astt. contrlr. of labr., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; astt. dist. offr., Mar., 1923; registr., co-op. societies, May, 1923; astt. offr., in charge, co-op. societies, S.S., Nov., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; astt. dir., co-opern., F.M.S., and S.S., Dec., 1927; on sp. deputatiori to India, Nov., 1928; ag. dir., co-opern., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1930; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930.

BOYD, WILLIAM RYDER.—B. 1887; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast and Dublin Univ.; B.A., Dublin; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1911; passed cadet, June, 1914; astt. dist. offr., May, 1915; dist. offr., July, 1920; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1923; ag. astt. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, May, 1928; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1928; 1st astt. sec. (B), S.S., Mar., 1929; sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1930; ag. Br. res., Selangor in addn., Apr.-May, 1931; registr.-gen., statistics, S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1932.

BOYD-SHANNON, GODFREY ECCLESTON.— B. 1907; ed. Wellington and Cambridge; schol., St. John's Coll., 1926; 1st cls. class. tripos, pt. I, 1928, pt. II, 1929; Browne med., 1928; B.A., 1929; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. prin., D.O., 27th Oct., 1930.

ROYDELL, HON. THOMAS.—Served apprenticeship as engnr. at R. & W. Hawthorns & Leslie, St. Peters, nr. Newcastle-on-Tyne; came to S. Africa, 1903; worked as a fitter in the Natal harbr. wks. and also in the S.A. rly. workshops, Durban; stood unsuccessfully as offl. cand. for S. African Labour Party for the Greyville divn. of Durban at the first Union parly. elections, 1910; contested by-elect. for same const., 1912; re-elec., 1915-20, 21 and 24; dep. leader of the S. African Labour party in parlt.; chmn., parly. labour caucus; national organiser of party; min. of posts and tels. and pub. wks. in Hertzog cabinet, June, 1924; min. of lab., 1925-29; nominated senator, Sept., 1929.
BOYES, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Prince Albert Coll., Auckland; joined N.Z. pensions dept., 1903; registr., pensions, 1910; astt. comsrr., 1919; comsrr., 1929.

BOYLE, SIR ALEXANDER GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1908), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1872; ed. at Charterhouse, Clifton Coll, and R.I.E.C., Cooper's Hill; priv. sec. to senior puisne judge, S. Sttltns., 1893; resig., 1895; astt. treas., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1895; ag. sec. to H.M. Comsrr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apr., 1898; ag. treas. Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admtn., May, 1901, to Jan., 1902; appt. collr. with seniority of 18th Sept., 1895, 1st Apr., 1902; astt. sec., Apr., 1902; ag. sub-comsrr., E.Prov., 1st Nov., 1903; sub-comsrr., Jan., 1905; ag. dep. comsrr., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. ch. sec., Apr., 1909; admtngr., offr., Aug., 1909; col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ret., 1920.

BOYSE, V. H., O.B.E. (1933), M.B.E. (1929).— B. 1883; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902; on military serv. from 26th July, 1917 to 5th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; astt. acctnt., June, 1922; sent on sp. mission to Iraq to advise on accounting methods, Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ag. as finan. sec. to high comsrr., Iraq., Mar.-Dec., 1924; astt., 1st Sept., 1930; astt. to exec. coun., Imp. Agril. Bureau and Oversea Mech. Transp. Coun.; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa,

BRADBURN, COL. WILLIAM ERNEST HORATIO. —B. 1884; served, Imp. Yeomanry, 1901; S. African Constab., Jan., 1902 to Oct., 1907; 8th King's Royal Hussars, Sept., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 1st King Edward's Horse, King's Oversea Dominions Regt. (adjutant), Apr., 1917 to July, 1919; local commdt., Cyprus mil. pol., Oct., 1919; mem., prison bd., Mar., 1921; passed exams. in mod. Greek, 1921 and 1923; gov., Athalassa juvenile prison, Dec., 1922 to June, 1924 in admn.; ag. ch. commdt., pol., July to Oct., 1923 and June to Oct., 1926; twice ag. A.D.C. to high comsrr., 1924; dep. ch. commdt., pol., Aug., 1924; ag. dist. comsrr., Aug., 1924; hon. A.D.C. to high comsrr., Oct., 1924; nom. offl. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1926; inspr.-gen., pol. and commdt., local forces, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1927; ag. inspr. prisons in admn., May, 1927 to Nov., 1927; nom. offl. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1928 to June, 1930; offl. mem., exec. coun., May to Dec., 1929; King's pol. med., 1932.

BRADFORD, SIR JOHN ROSE, Bart. (1931), K.C.M.G. (1911); M.D., D.Sc., F.R.C.P., F.R.S. —B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll. Schl., Coll. and Hosp. George Henry Lewes student and Grocer research schlr.; late mem. of Senate, Univ. of London; physician to Univ. Coll. Hosp.; Holme lecturer on clinical medicine to Univ. Coll. hosp. med. schl.; sec. of the Royal Society; sen. med. adviser to C.O., 1912; resigned, 1924.
BRADLEY, CHARLES HUGH GRANVILLE.— B. 1901; ed. Wellington Coll.; elk., col. audit dept., June, 1925; astt. audr., Hongkong, Sept.,

BRADLEY, JOHN THOMAS, O.B.E. (1933), L.R.C.P.E., L.R.C.S.E., L.F.P. and S.G.; M.D. (Bruz.).—B. 1870; ed. at Queen's Coll., Belfast, and Lond. Schl. Trop. Med. (1901); undergrad. in arts, Royal Univ., Ireland; prizeman in med., midwifery, and diseases of women and children, Queen's Coll., Belfast, 1897-8; gold medallist in practical midwifery, Clifton st. Mater. Hosp.; 1st prizeman in med. and surg. diseases of children, Children's Hosp., Queen st., Belfast; late astt. med. offr., Glamorgan Co. Asylum, 1901; astt. med. offr., Seychelles, Aug., 1901; J.P. and chmn. of loc. bd. of health, 1st and 2nd divs., South Mahé; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Seychelles, Mar., 1906; ch. med. offr.,
BRADLEY, KENNETH GRANVILLE, B.A.— B. 1904; ed. Wellington Coll., Berkshire, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; prob., N. Rhodesia, 1926; astt. native comsrr., Jan., 1928; dist. offr., grade
III, Apr., 1929; seconded as ag. astt. prin., C.O., Nov. 1928 to May, 1929; astt. sec., N. Rhodesia, June, 1930.

BRADNEY, GEORGE PRESTON.—B. 1877; ed. at Oratory Schl., Edgbaston, and Trinity Hall, Camb.; B.A., Cantab.; hons. in hist., 1899; col. audt off., June, 1901; astt. aud., B. C. Africa, Feb. 1902; astt. auditor, East Africa Prot., Sept., 1904; auditor, Fiji, and Western Pacific Protectorates, 1909; dir. of external audit, S. Sttltns. and F.M.S., Oct., 1914; ag. aud.-gen., S. Stiltns., in addition, Jan. to Feb., 1916; ag. aud.-gen., S. Stiltns., in addition, June, 1916 and Dec., 1918; audir.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1919; pres., coman. to enquire into finan. position, Singapore municipality, Sept., 1921; mem., coman. to enquire into organization and expend. of gov't. dept., F.M.S., etc., Feb., 1922; ag. finan. adviser and treas., F.M.S., Mar., 1930; auditor, S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

BRADSTREET, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1884; ent. C.A.'s office, June, 1902; asst. head, checking and insurance dept., May, 1919; do., gen. dept., Jan., 1924; sec., Zambesi Bdg. inter-deptl. comtee., 1928-31; dep. head, finance dept., Mar., 1931.

BRADY, LIEUT.-COL. JOHN BANKS, D.S.O.—Head mast., Grey Coll. Sch., Dec., 1901; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., Feb., 1904; inspr. of schls., Rhodesia, Aug., 1909; on active serv., 1914-20; commanded 4th and 18th batts., K.R.R.C.; ment in desps.; croix de guerre with palm leaves; ag. dir. of educn., S. Rhodesia, 1920-21.

BRAGG, ALGERNON.—B. 1890; col. audit dept., Apr., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915; mily. audit, K. African Expedy. Force, 1916-19; asst. audr., Uganda, 9th May, 1919; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Sept., 1924; ag. audr., Uganda, for various periods, 1926-27; prin. asst. audr., Nov., 1928; ag. audr., May to Nov., 1929.
BRAILSFORD, H. E.—Aast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Jan., 1917.

BRANCH, SIR CHARLES ERNEST ST. J., KT. BACH. (1924), B.A., B.C.L., Durham Univ.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1892; ag. chief mag., St. Vincent, 1895 to Jan., 1898; asst. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., May, 1898; ag. atty.-gen., July to Nov., 1899; mem. legis. coun. and exec. coun., Dominica; mem. general legis. coun., Leeward Islds., Dec., 1899; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 2 Sept., 1902; chancellor of the Diocese of Antigua, 1903-1909; pres., Antigua defence reserve force, 1903; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1902; apptd. to combined offices of col. sec. and atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., Jan., 1903; deleg. to agric. confere., Jamaica, 1907; Barbados, 1908; Canadian reciprocity confere., Barbados, 1908; cotton confere., Liverpool, 1908; comsnr. to prepare a new and revised edition of laws of Leeward Islds., Feb., 1909; admstr. govt. of Antigua on various occasions in the years 1902 to 1909; ag. gov., Leeward Islds., Nov.-Dec., 1907, and Apr. to Oct., 1909; atty.-gen., Jamaica, Oct., 1909; deleg. to Canada on bananas tax question, 1915; deleg. to Indent. Emigr. Confere., Trinidad, 1916; ag. ch. just., Jamaica, Apr., 1919; rep. of Jamaica at Canada-W. Indies Trade Confere., May-June, 1920; puiane judge, sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1921; judl. comsnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1922; ch. just., Jamaica, 1923; ch. just., Ceylon, 3rd July, 1925; ret., 1926.

BRANCH, EDMUND RALPH.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin.; dist. med. offr., Dominica, 1902; ditto, Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1902; med. supttd., Cunningham hosp., and surgeon of the gaol, St. Kitts, Feb., 1903; med. offr., dist. 2, St. Kitts, Nov., 1910; served with R.A.M.C. during the war.

BRANCH, STANLEY, M.B.—Ch.B. (hons.), Edin., 1898; D.T.M., Liverpool, 1907; D.M.O., St. Lucia, 1899; Uganda, 1910; St. Vincent, 1903; house surg., St. Lucia, 1904; D.M.O., St. Lucia, 1912; seconded as dir. under Internat. Health Bd. campaign for eradication of hookworm disease, St. Lucia, 1914-24; ch. med. and health offr., St. Vincent, 1926.
BRANDON, KENNETH REVIL.—B. 1888; clk. of cts., Jamaica, 1919; ag. registr., sup. ct., Apr. to Dec., 1921; ag. res. mag., Oct. and Nov., 1923 and Nov., 1924 to Aug., 1925; res. mag., Sept., 1925.

BRASNETT, NORMAN VINCENT, M.A., Dipl. For. (Cantab.)—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick Schol. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; R.F.A., 1914-18 (capt., desps.); asst. cons., forests, Kenya Col., 1920; senr. A.C.F., Kenya, 1928; cons., forests, Uganda, 1929.

BRASSEY-EDWARDS, H.—Veterinary offr., E.A.P., Feb., 1910; ag. dir. of agric., 1910; senr. vet. offr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; ag. dep. ch. vet. offr., 1924; dep. ch. vet. offr., 1924; ag. ch. vet. offr., 1927; ch. vety. offr., 1929; dep. dir., agr. (animal industry), 1931.

BRAWN, ALAN C.—B. 1878; ed. "Queen Mary's" gram. sch., Wallasey, and St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea (1st cl. Queen's scholarship); teacher's certif. (Ed. of Educo., England); matric., Lond. Univ., 1898; asst. mast., diocesean sch., Hong Kong, 1901-1909; jun. asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1910; lecturer, Tech. Inst., Hong Kong, 1910-1912; headmaster, Wantsai dist. schl., 1913; headmaster, Yaumati schl., 1914; headmaster, Wantsai schl., 1915; seconded for mil. ser., 1917; 2nd lieut., 1917; 1st lieut., 1918; served in France, 1917 to 1919; demobilised 1919; dep. regler., Hong Kong Univ., 1919; headmaster, Wantsai schl., 1919, headmaster, Yaumati schl., 1919; asst. mast., cls. II., 1920; headmnr., Victoria Br. schl., 1920; asst. mast., cls. I., 1921; headmnr., Wantsai schl., 1921; ditto, Indian schl., 1921; asst. mast., Queen's Coll. and Wantsai schl., 1922; headmnr., Wantsai schl., 1923; headmnr., Yaumati schl., 1924-28; ag. inspr., schls., 1929-31; dir., tech. inst., 1930.

BRAYLEY, ALEXANDER JOHN, B.A.—B. 1907; ed. Dulwich and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; asst. sec., col. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Apr., 1930; passed exam., mod. Greek, May, 1931; ag. ch. ast., sec., July, 1932.

BRAYNE, CHARLES VALENTINE.—B. 1877; ed. Monkton Combe schl., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Oeylon civ. serv., Nov., 1901; off. ast. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Apr., 1903; ast. collr. cust., Trincomalee, May, 1905; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Feb., 1906; dist. judge, Negombo, Jan., 1909; Badulla, Feb., 1909; ast. land settltn. offr., Oct., 1909; ag. ast. govt. agt., Colombo dist., June, 1912; ditto, Prov. of Uva, July, 1912; ditto, Colombo dist., Aug., 1912; offr., Cls. II., Jan., 1913; ast. dist. judge, Jaffna, June, 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kaluthara, Sept., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1916; govt. agt., E. Prov., Feb., 1920; ag. contr., rev., May-Aug., 1927; addnl. ditto, Aug., 1927; ag. contr., rev., Feb., 1929 and Apr. to Sept., 1930; comsnr. lands, July, 1931.

BRAYSHAY, SIDNEY, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1886; tech. instr., tech. inst., P.W.D., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1911; ast. engrnr., Oct., 1914; lieut., R.E., Sept., 1918; exec. engrnr., Apr., 1920; exec., engrnr.,/i/c, tech. schl., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1922; ag. ar. exec. engrnr., hydraulic, Selangor, June, 1930.

BRAZEL, MAJOR CLAUDE HAMILTON, M.C., R.E. (T.), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1894; temp. 2nd lieut., R.E., 1914; S.M.E., Chatham, Apr.-June, 1915; lieut., 1915; adjt., R.E., 1916-17; capt., 1917; major, 1917-19; ment in desps., 1917; Belgian Croix de Guerre, 1917; M.C., 1918; "1914-15" Star, G.S. and Victory med.; France, 1915-19; major, R.E. (T.), 1920; Nat. Defence Force, Apr.-July, 1921; dir., elec. dept., Bahamas, 1926.

BREAKLEY, L. J.—B. 1882; passed Lond. matric. and Oxford sen. local exams., June, 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., olk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to ld. chamberlain's office, Feb., 1901; transferred to C.O., 18th Feb., 1901; conf. olk., 1st Jan., 1918.

BREKBEN, GEORGE GUSTAV RADLOFF, M.A. (Edin.). LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1883; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; prte. sec. to Gen. Botha, 1916-19, and attended Paris Peace Conf., 1919; prte. sec. to Gen. Smuts, 1919-21; asst. crown pros., Johannesburg, 1922; 1st prof. asst. to atty-gen., Pretoria, 1924-27; law adviser to crown, Jan., 1927.

BREEN, MICHAEL JAMES.—B. 1884; ed. at Clongowes Wood Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; B.A., math., science (honours) in Royal Univ. of Ireland, 1905; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1907; passed cadet, 1911; 2nd ast. regt. gen., 1911; ag. ast. P.M.G., 1911; ag. supt., impts. and expts., 1913; ast. P.M.G., 1913; ast. dist. offr., Jan., 1914; ag. ast. col. sec., 1914 to 1915; on mil. serv., 1916-19; ag. ast. P.M.G., 1920; ag. P.M.G., 1920 to 1921; ast. treas., 1921; P.M.G., 1924; ag. col. treas., 1929; chmn., retrenchment comittee., July, 1930.

BRENT, REGINALD WALTER.—B. 1883; trooper, S.A.C., Boer War, 1900-02 (Queen's Med. 4 clasps); ast. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; 2nd lieut., M.M.G.S., 1915; served in France, 1915-17 (M.C.); Mesopotamia, 1919-20 (Iraq med.); resumed duty as ast. conserv., forests, 1920; dep. conserv., forests, Dec., 1930; ag. conserv., forests, 1930-32.
BRITT, E. A.—B. 1880; apptd. to emigrants' information office, 2nd July, 1906; overseas settlmt. office, 1919.

BRETT, F. W.—B. 1885; Cape Mounted Rifles, Nov., 1903-06; 3rd battn., Royal Warwickshire Regt., Jan., 1906-08; ast. dist. commr., R.A.P., Sept., 1908; priv. sec. to gov., Oct., 1912-15, and sec. to high commr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1914-15; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, 1915-16; dist. commr., Dec., 1915; dist. polit. offr., German E. Africa, provisional adminstr., Nov., 1916; admstr. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; prov. commr., Jan., 1926.
BREWIS, HELEN ELIZABETH STUART.—B. 1904; ed. Sherborne Sch. for Girls, Dorset, Froebel Educln. Inst. Training Coll. for Teachers, Roehampton; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1927.

BRICE-SMITH, HUGH MIDDLETON, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. Pocklington and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1909; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1917; ag. res. in ch., Niger Prov., 1927; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1929.

BRIDGE, BLACKER FRANK, B.A. (Dublin), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1930.—B. 1891; ed. St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; ast. contr. of lab., June, 1918; ast. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Feb., 1922; dist. offr., Bentong, Apr., 1922; dep. contr., lab., Mar., 1924; offr. els. III., Nov., 1926; dep. pub. pros., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, June, 1929; mag., Ipoh, Aug., 1930; offr. els. II., 1931; ast. to atty-gen., S.S., Sept., 1932.

BRIDGES, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR TOM, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), D.S.O. (1904), F.R.G.S.—B. 1872; Grenadier Guards; served S. African War, 1899-1901; Somaliland, 1902-4; European War, 1914-15; mil. attache at the Hague, Brussels, Copenhagen and Christiania, 1910-14; lieut.-col., 4th Hussars, 1914-16; brev. col., 1916; maj.-gen., temp. lieut.-col., 1917; col., Dragoon Gds., 1920; commdr., Br. mly. mission to Smyrna, 1920; gov. S. Australia, 1922-27.

BRIDGES, WILLIAM FRANCIS NOEL, D.S.O.—B. 1890; ed. Melbourne C. of E. Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.; survr.-on-agreement, F.M.S., Sept., 1912; served A.I.F., Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; ast. supt., surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supt., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1927; astd. survr.-genl's office, Jan., 1928; ag. ast. survr. gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1929 and Aug., 1930 to Feb., 1931.
BRIERCLIFFE, RUPERT, O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., B.Sc., D.P.H. (Man.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.).—B. 1889; dep. dir., health, Palestine, 1920; dir., med. and sanry. services, Ceylon, July, 1930.

BRINK, MAJOR-GEN., ANDRIES JACOB EESTERN, D.T.D., D.S.O.—Joined civ. serv., S. African Repub., 1896; served Mpufu (native) campaign, 1898; Zoutpansberg and Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1902; elected veldt cornet, S. African Repub. forces, 1901; capt., permanent force (staff), Union defence forces, 1912; promoted major, and served as dist. staff offr., No. 6 and later as dist. staff offr., No. 7 mil. dist., 1914; brig-major, 1st mounted brig., German S.W.A. campaign, 1916; temp. lieut.-col., Mar., 1915; dist. staff offr., No. 6 mil. dist., with rank of temp. lieut-col., whilst so emp., Aug., 1915; brev. lieut.-col., Sept., 1915; brig-major, 2nd mounted brig., E. Africa, Mar., 1916; gen. staff offr. with rank of col. headqrs., 3rd E. African divn., Apr., 1916; resumed duty as dist. staff offr., No. 6 mil. dist., Dec., 1916; transf. to defence headqrs., and assumed duty as ast. adjt.-gen., defence headqrs., Sept., 1917; ag. ch. of gen. staff and adjt.-gen., with temp. rank of col., Nov., 1919; lieut.-col., 1st grade staff offr., Nov., 1919; brig.-gen., and ch. of gen. staff, Sept., 1920; combined post of ch. of gen. staff and sec. for defence, 1st Oct., 1922; 1914-15 Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med., D.S.O. (1917), French Croix de Guerre; men. in desps., for serv. in G.S.W.A. campaign (1918), medal, Anglo-Boer oorlog.

BRINK, COLONEL CAROLUS, D.T.D., D.S.O.—B. 1879; served Jameson Raid, S. African Republic, 1896; Mpufu campaign, Zoutpansberg, S. African Republic, 1896; Anglo-Boer War, Heidelberg commando, staff General Spruyt, 1899-1901; German S.W. Africa campaign, as brig.-major, 2nd Mounted Brig., Northern Force, 1914-15; German E. African campaign as G.S.O.I., 2nd E. African divn., 1916-17; Europe, Western Front, 1917-18; qualified, staff course, Bloemfontein, 1914; qualified, senr. staff course, Cambridge, 1919; qtrmr.-gen. Union Defence Forces; ment. in desps.; Croix de Guerre (French); med., Anglo-Boer War.

BRINK, DANIEL.—Cik., C.C., Kimberley, 1895; cik., deeds, Vryburg, 1897; cik., C.C. Kimberley, 1898; cik., deeds, Vryburg, 1899; cik., res. mag., Simonstown, 1899; cik., deeds, Cape Town, 1900; ditto, Vryburg, 1902; ditto, Kimberley, 1906; ch. cik., C.C. and R.M., Pietermaritzburg, 1908; ch. cik., deeds, Vryburg, 1908; 1st grade Union cik., deeds, Vryburg, 1912; examr. (ast. regir.), Vryburg, 1913; ast. regir., deeds, Windhoek, 1921; ditto, Pietermaritzburg, 1922; ditto, Pretoria, 1925; ditto, Cape Town

BRISTOW, CHARLES FRANCIS.—B. 1882; 2nd Middlesex, R.G.A., 1899-1901; S. African constab., 1902-07; served in S. African war (Queen's medal and clasp); Moulinein Artillery, 1908-10; asst. inspr. of police, E.A.P., Jan., 1911; passed pol. promotion exam., Aug., 1911; passed Kiswahili exam., lower standard; inspr. of police, Mar., 1913; asst. supt. of police, 1st Apr., 1914; during war period 1914, attached to Moulinein Rifles as lisut.; on active service, E.A.P., 1915-17; awarded "1914-15" Star, War and Victory meda.; resumed civil duties as asst. supt. of police, 1918; ag. qrtmr., pol. and prison depts., Oct., 1920 to Mar., 1921; immigr. offr., Apr., 1921 to June, 1922; supt. of pol., 1st Jan., 1923; headqr. supt., Jan., 1926.

BRITTEN, HENRY.—Apptd. probationer, Dee., 1895; 3rd ol. olk., magistrate's office, Ixopo, June, 1896; elk. and Zulu interpreter and sub-distributor of stamps, Ixopo, Apr., 1897; ag. clk. of the court on several occasions; served through the Boer War, 1899-1900, with the Border mounted rifles, including the siege of Ladysmith; 2nd cls. elk., off. of sec. to nat. affaire dept., Pietermaritzburg, May, 1901; transf. to Transvaal civ. ser. as asst. res. mag., May, 1902; A.R.M., Christiana, Nov., 1904; ag. R.M., Lichtenburg, Apr., 1906; ag. R.M., Wolmaranstad, Oct., 1906; A.R.M., Belfast, Feb., 1907; ag. R.M., Lichtenburg, Aug., 1908; A.R.M., Boksburg, Dec., 1908; R.M., Boksburg, July, 1909; mag., Potchefstroom, Jan., 1918; mag., King William's Town, Jan., 1925; Pretoria, Jan., 1929; Johannesburg, Jan., 1930.
BRITTER, EDWIN HAROLD.—B. 1886; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1909; admitted bar, Mauritius, 1909; advoc., sup. ct., Natal, 1911; judge, native high ct. of Natal, 1930.
BRIZZELL, ANNIE ISABEL, M.B.E. (1926).—B. 1877; staff nurse, Basutoland, 1906; matron, 1907; war serv., 1915-19; ment. in despatches, 1918.

BROCKLEBANK, S. H., M.B.E.—B. 1877; ed. Calday Sch., served Boer War, 1900-02; asst. supt., repatriation dept., 1902; acct. gen.'s dept., 1904; inland rev. dept., 1905; defence dept., 1914; survr., inland rev., 1921; recr., rev., Krugerdorp, 1928; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1929; Durban, Feb., 1930.

BROCKMAN, SIR EDWARD, LEWIS, K.C.M.G. (1913), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1865; cadet, Straits Stitlmts., 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; comntr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sept., 1903; ag. res. gen., May, 1907; confirmed as fed. sec., continuing to act also as res. gen., July, 1907 to Feb., 1908; ag. res., Perak, Apr., 1908; res., Pahang, Nov., 1908; res., Negri Sembilan, 1st Jan., 1910; col. sec., S. Stitlmts., 1910; chief sec., F.M.S., 1911; ret., Sept., 1920; apptd. agt., Malay States Infr. Agency, Nov., 1920.

BROCKWELL, MONTAGU BUTLER.—B. 1878; asst mast., Raffles Inst., Singapore, July, 1904; European mast., grade II, Victoria Bridge schl., Oct., 1907; inspr., schls., N. Sembilan, Aug., 1913; headmast., Maxwell Rd. schl., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1922; headmast., Geylang Eng. schl., Singapore, July, 1930.

BROMLEY, REAR-ADML. ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1876; ed. Farnborough Sch.; H.M.S. Britannia; joined navy, 1892; lieut., 1898; commodr., 1908; capt., 1915; served European War, 1914-18; H.M.S. Courageous, 1916-19; flag capt. to Vice-Adml. Napier commg. light cruiser force; ret. list, 1922; rear admr., ret., 1926; a gentlemen usher to the King since 1927; pvt. sec. (ceremonial and reception) to the S. of S. (Lord Passfield), 1931.
BROOK, THOMAS.—B. 1873; ed. Lawrence dist. high schl., N.Z.; various positions N.Z. land and survey dept., 1919-25; valuer-gen., 1926.
BROOK, CAPT. W. B.—Aust. dist. comntr., E.A.P., 1911; dist. comntr., Kenya, Jan., 1919.

BROOKES, VICTOR S.—B. 1888; passed senr. Cambridge local exam., 1906; appd. copyist, col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1906; ag. elk. to regis. gen. of births, etc., Jan. to Dec., 1908; cashier, St. John's savings bank, Sept., 1908; acctnt., ditto, June, 1909; ag. acctnt., treasy. dept., June to Sept., 1909; elk. to comntr. and exec. coun., Virgin Is., Apr., 1911; rev. offr., St. Kitts, Nov., 1914; acctnt., treasy., St. Kitts; ag. audit elk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

BROOKS, ARCHIBALD JOSEPH, F.L.S., F.C.S., F.R.H.S.—B. 1881; entd. Royal Gardens, Kew, 1902; offr. in charge, agric. schl., Dominica, 1903; awarded R.H.S. teacher's certificate, 1904; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 2nd June to Dec., 1904, and July to Dec., 1908; mem. of coun. of agric. soc'y., Dominica, 1908; deleg. to agric. confere., Trinidad, 1905; asst. sec., gov't. permanent exhibtn. comtee., 1906; mem. of Dominica reserve force, 1904-11; winner of Lady Sweet-Escott challenge shield; asst. agric. supt., St. Lucia, 1911; offr. in charge, Reunion Estate, 1913; land offr., 1914; agricultural supt., Dec., 1914; dir. of agr., Gambia, 1922; del. to Imp. Agr. Research Confere., London, 1927; del. to confere. of Empire Meteorologists, London, 1929; del. to Imp. Mycological Confere., Lond., 1929; author of "The West Indian Lime," and other works.

BROOKS, MAJOR LAWRENCE ABBOTT WALLACE, F.R.G.S., etc.—Ed. Woolwich and abroad; ent. Royal Arty., 1900; served, S. African War, 1901-2 (Queen's medal with clasp, ment. in despatches); Royal Canadian Field Arty., 1902-5; special serv., Russo-Japanese War, 1906-6; served, Natal Native Rebellion, 1906; (medal with clasp); road-mapping, Natal and Zululand, 1908; special serv. in Canada, 1909-10; Chinese Revolution, defence of Shameen, Canton, 1911; in Far East and Australia, 1911-14; served, Togoland, 1914; France and Flanders, 1915; anti-aircraft home defence, 1916; Egypt and Palestine, 1917-18 ("1914-15" Star, "General Serv." and "Victory" medals, ment. in despatches); Woolwich, 1919; Australia and New Zealand, 1919-20; asst. travelling comntr., Gambia, Jan., 1921; ag. travelling comntr., S. Bank Prov., Nov., 1924; travelling comntr., July, 1926; senr. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1932; ag. col. sec., May, Sept., 1932.

BROOKS, R. L.—B. 1900; ed. Charterhouse; Artists Rifles; comntr., Guards Machine Gun Regt., 1920; passed inter. exam., Surrv.'s Inst.; dipl., forestry, Edinburgh, 1922; asst. conserv. forests, Uganda, 1922; seconded to adminstr., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Trinidad, 1925.

BROWN, CHARLES CUTHBERT.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schlr., 1909; Bell schlr., 1908; 1st cls. class. tripes, 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; asst. dist. offr., Bruas, 1912-13; asst. sec. to res., Perak,

BROWN, FRANK LESLIE, M.C.—B.1896; ed. Wilson Gram. Sch.; served in France, 1916-19; lieut., M.C., bar (ment.in despe.); prob., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1919; ast. nat. commnr., Feb., 1922; nat. commnr., Oct., 1926; dist. offr., grade III, Apr., 1929; do., grade II, Apr., 1931; sec. as ast. sec., Aug., 1931.

BROWN, GAVIN ROBERT LOCKHART.—B. 1882; govt. travelling schl. master, Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; ast. govt. schl. mast., Jan., 1915; ag. local audr., Apr., 1915; 2nd clk., treas. and cust., Feb., 1918; ch. clk., treas. and cust. and ship's mast., Mar., 1917; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office and clk., exec. and leg. count., Oct., 1920; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1928 to Feb., 1931; harbrmr., Sept., 1929; ag. col. sec., May, 1929 to Feb., 1930; mag., registr., sup. ct., col. postmtr. and harbrmr., Feb., 1931; pvt. sec. to gov., June, 1931.

BROWN, GEORGE Pritchard.—B. 1886; served with E. Africa pol. service batt., 1914-17; capt., Aug., 1915; Turkana expdn., 1915 (A.G.S. med.); ast. supt. of pol., Kenya, Apr., 1916; supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923; staff offr. to commnr., pol., 1926; ag. dep. commnr., pol., July to Oct., 1931; order (serving brother) of St. John of Jerusalem; ast. commnr., pol., 1932.

BROWN, HARRY WHIDDEN, B.A., L.L.B.—B. 1868; ed.
