Rhodesia, Sept., 1910; ch. astt. survr. and examiner of diagrams, Nov., 1917; ch. survr. on various occasions, 1916 to 1920; ch. survr., 1920; dir., surveys, Oct., 1924; mem., leg. coun., 1929-32.

FALK, EDWARD MORRIS, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Bradford Grammar Schl., on the Continent and at Victoria Univ.; astt. dist. comanr., S. Nigeria, 1907; West African G.S. Medal, 1909; dist. comanr., 2nd grade, 1913; interned as prisoner of war in Germany, 1914; escaped and retld. to England, 1915; resumed duty, Nigeria, 1915; res., 1923; staff grade, 1929; ag. admstr. of col., 1931.

FALLOWFIELD, J. B., M.I.C.E., etc.—Astt. engr., P.W. D., Gold Coast, 1915; exec. engr., grade II, 1919; dep. prov. engr., 1921; exec. engr., 1924; res. constrnl. engr., Cape Coast water works, 1925; transfd., Nigeria, P.W.D., as engr. exec. engr., 1928.
FALLS, NORMAN.—B. 1881; sec. to ch. just., S.S., May, 1905; stltm. offr., F.M.S., June, 1907; supervisor, cust., Feb., 1912; supt., cust., F.M.S., Oct., 1923.

FARFAN, JOSE MANUEL.—B. 1876; 6th elk., secretariat, Trinidad, Apr., 1895; 5th elk., Aug., 1897; 4th elk., Feb., 1902; 3rd elk. and sec. to cen. road., Feb., 1906; 2nd and conf. clk., secretariat, Aug., 1915; 2nd astt. col. sec. and supt. of govt. printing, Apr., 1920; ag. lst. astt. col. sec. on several occasions since 1920; 1st astt. col. sec., Jan., 1930; ag. dep. col. sec., in 1931 and 1932.

FARMER, FRANK.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Dec., 1914; astt. elk., Apr., 1917; on mily. serv., Mar., 1917 to Feb., 1919; olfr. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted, under order in council of 1916, elk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Dec., 1924; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.

FARMER, NEIL JOHN CONSTABLE, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; ed. Radley Coll. and King's Coll. London; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1913-14; war serv., 1914-20; ment. in desps.; major R. of O.; exec. engrn., Nyasaland, 1920; ag. asst. D.P.W., Feb. to July, 1928 and Nov., 1928 to Feb., 1929; exec. engrn., Uganda, 1930.
FARQUHAR, EDWARD ALLAN.—Comsnr., S. Aust. harbours bd., 1914; chmn., 1924.
FARQUHAR, FREDERICK COLLIN.—B. 1884; asst. acct., P.W.D., S. Nigeria, 1910; acot., 1911; ch. acct., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1923; transf. treasy., as senr. asst. treas., 1924; ag. dep. treas. in 1928.

FARQUHAR, GEORGE NEIL, M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to secretariat, Apr., 1920; ditto, Puttalam Kach., June, 1921; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1922; ditto to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., 1925; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1926; sec., land coman., Aug., 1928; asst. ool. sec., Jan., 1930; clk. to leg. coun., Mar., 1930; clk. to state coun., July, 1931; contr. establishments, July, 1932.

FARQUHARSON, ROBERT ALEXANDER, M.A. (Oxon), M.Sc., F.G.S., A.O.S.M.—B. 1883; ed. Boys' High Sch., Otago Univ., N.Z. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; senr. schol., N.Z. Univ., 1905; dipl. in mining engrg., 1905; 1st cls. hons., geology and 2nd cls., physical science, N.Z., 1906; Rhodes schol., N.Z., 1906; B.A., Oxford, hons. in geology and mineralogy, 1908; M.A., 1912; govt. petrologist, mines dept., W. Australia, 1911-22; lent at request of C.O. to Somaliland govt. to furnish rept. on geological and mineral resources of Prot., 1923; govt. geologist, Somaliland, 1923-26; recd. thanks of S. of S. for geol. map of Somaliland; dir., agr. and geologist, Somaliland, 1926.

FARRELL, CAPT. WILFRID JEROME, M.C., M.A., R. of O.—B. 1882; ed. Ushaw; Hymers Jesus Coll., Cambridge; fellow of Jesus Coll., 1906; student, Br. Schl. of Archaeology, Athens, 1906; Munich Univ., 1907; on staff of Rugby Schl. and Haileybury Coll., 1911-19; served in European war, 1914-19; civ. educn. offr., Iraq, Sept., 1919; officiating dir. and adviser, miny. of educn., Iraq, 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1923; asst. dir. educn., Apr., 1927; dep. dir., Oct., 1929; ag. dir., June to Aug., 1927 and July, 1928 to Jan., 1929; Br. mag. for riot cases, Sept. to Oct., 1929.

FARRELL, WILLIAM JOSEPH HENRY.—Ent. post office, C. of G.H., 1889; tel. dept., S. African Repub., 1895-1902; priv. sec. to min. of just., Union of South Africa, 1910; priv. sec. to min. of posts and tels., 1912; priv. sec. to min. of lands, 1913; dept. of lands, 1916; asst. sec. to prime min. and asst. clk. of exec. coun., 1924; under sec. to prime min., under sec. for external affrs., and clk. exec. coun., 1927.

FAULKNER, ODIN TOM, C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1890; ed. St. Albans and Calius Coll., Cambridge (Schol.), B.A. (1st cls., nat. sci. tripos, 11); dipl., agr., 1912; mycologist and agr. expl. to Rubber Growers' Asso., Malaya, 1912-14; dep. dir., agr. to govt. of Punjab 1914-21; dir., agr., Nigeria, 1921.
FAULKNER, S.N., O.B.E. (1918)—Asst. auditor E.A.P., Jan., 1909; senr. asst. auditor, Apr., 1915; dep. ch. acctnt., Uganda Rly., 1917.
FAWCETT, J. F. ST. A.—Deputy regir., high ct., F.A.P., 1910 regir., high court, Feb., 1919; mast. and regir., sup. ct., Sierra Leone, July, 1921; ch. regir. and sheriff, Gold Coast, May, 1924.
FAZAN, S. H., O.B.E. (1930)—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. commr., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

FEARON, FRANK ST. CLAIR.—Ed. Charterhouse (schol.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhibr.), B.A. (litt. hum.), 1902; clk. law dept., Cape Colony, 1903; asst. regir., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; 1st asst. regir. and taxing offr., 1923; regir. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Johannesburg, 1927; ditto, Grahamstown, 1930.

FEETHAM, RICHARD, C.M.G.—B. 1874; ed. Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; mem., Inner Temple; called to bar, 1899; deputy town clk., Johannesburg, 1902-3; town clk., Johannesburg, 1903-5; admitted advoc., Transvaal, 1903; legal adviser to high commr. for S. Africa, 1907-10 and 1912-13; K.C., 1919; mem., leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907-10; mem., H. of A., Union of S. Africa, 1916-23; lieut., 1st battn. Cape Corps, 1916-19; mem., Southborough comtee. on Indian Reforms and chmn. functions comtee., 1918-19; mem., India Office comtee. on Govt. of India Bill, 1919; ag. pres., sup. ct. of Swaziland, 1922-23; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal Prov. Divn., Aug., 1923; judge pres., sup. ct., Natal prov. divn., May, 1930.

FEHILY, JOSEPH PATRICK, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. N.U.I., F.A.C.S., D.P.H., Queen's Univ., Belfast.—B. 1892; ed. Blackrock Coll. and Univ. Colls., Cork and Dublin; late temp. surg., R.N. and capt., R.A.M.C.; res. med. offr., Br. Hosp., Buenos Aires, 1919; Hong Kong govt. med. serv., 1924; health offr. of port and inspr., immigrts., 1932.
FELIX-JONES, IVOR, B.A.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; sup't., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Mar., 1922.
FENN, EGERTON DUDLEY.—B. 1893; clk., staff of gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1912-15; sub.-inspr. Swaziland pol., 1928; inspr., 1931.

FENTON, HON. JAMES EDWARD.—M.H.R. C'wealth of Australia since 1910; mem., standing comtee., of pub. wks., 1914-17 and of joint comtee., pub. accts., 1917-25; opposition whip, 1922-29; min. for trade and cust., 1929; Austral. del. to Disarmament Conf., London, 1930; P.M.G., Lyons' Ministry, 1932; resigned, Oct., 1932.

FENTON, JAMES STEVENSON.—B. 1891 ed.; Dundee High Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. (Edin.); war service, Mesopotamia 1917-19; passed progress exam. in Mandi; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1915; dist. commr., Apr., 1925.
FERGUSON, DAVID GILBERT.—B. 1861; called to the bar, 1890; judge of sup. ct., New S. Wales, Mar., 1912.

FERGUSON, HON. GEORGE HOWARD, P.C. (Can.), 1931, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1870; ed. Kemptville, Ont., High Sch., Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall; called to bar, 1894; el. to Ont. legis., g.e., 1905; re-el., g.e., 1908, 1911, 1914, 1919 and 1923; leader of conservative party since 1920; premier of Ont., 1923; high commr. for Canada in London, Nov., 1930.

FERGUSSON, GEN. SIR CHARLES, BART., G.C.B. (1932); G.C.M.G. (1924); K.C.B. (1915); K.C.M.G. (1918); C.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1906); D.S.O. (1898), D.L., J.M.—B. 1865; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; joined Grenadier Guards, 1883; adjutant, 1890-94; joined Egyptian Army, 1895; Sudanese campaign, 1896-98; severely wounded, Rosaires (med. with eight clasps, ment. five times in desps.); commanded 15th Sudanese, 1899 (2nd cls. Medjidie); garrison and dist., Omdurman, 1900; adjt.-gen., Egyptian Army, 1901-3; commanded 3rd Batt. Grenadier Guards, 1904-7; brig.-gen., gen. staff, Irish commd., 1907-8; inspr., infy., 1909-13; served European War, 1914-18; in commd., 5th divn. and subsequently of 2nd and 17th Army Corps; mily. gov., occupied German territory, 1918-19 (seven times ment. in desps.); gov. and commdr.-in-ch., New Zealand, Oct., 1924; assumed govt., Dec., 1924; ret., 1930; chmn., W. Indian Closer Union Comm., 1932.

FERNANDO, SIR HILARIAN MARCUS, KT. BACH. (1923), M.D., B.S. (Lond.), Fellow of Univ. Coll. (London); J.P.—B. 1864; ed. at the Royal Coll., Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. Scholar and Gilchrist Scholar for the East, 1882-83; 2nd medical entrance scholar, Univ. Coll., 1884; Atchison scholar, 1888, 1st cls. hons. and gold medal, physiology, medicine, and forensic medicine (Univ. of London); asst. col. surg. and regiar., Ceylon med. coll., 1890; 1st physician, general hosp., and lecturer in medicine, Ceylon med. coll., 1898; reported on outbreak of malaria in Galle, 1894, and plague in Bombay, 1897, (rec. thanks of govt.); ret. from govt. serv., 1909; served on Colombo lake development, railway traffic, education, opium, prevention of tuberculosis, and several other commissions and comtees. of enquiry (1909-1918); nominated mem. of legis. coun. of Ceylon as the first low-country Sinhalese representative, Jan., 1917; nom. unoff. mem., leg. coun., 1921-1924; ditto, exec. coun. from

FERNANDO, PETER OLIVER.—B. 1898; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. to Kurunegalla kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Feb., 1922; ditto, Jaffna kach., Mar., 1923; pol. mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1924; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Nov., 1924; attd., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1925; office asst., ditto, Mar., 1926; pol. mag., Panadura, Dec., 1926; office asst., Mannar kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1929; asst. stimt. offr., Jan., 1930.
FERNANDO, SELLAPERUMAGE SHELTON
CLARENCE, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1931; attd., ch. sec.'s office, Dec., 1931; attd., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1932.

FIDDES, SIR GEORGE VANDERLUB, G.C.M.G. (1917), K.C.M.G. (1912), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888 to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and acctnt. to Sir A. Milner, high commr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.; accounting offr., 1907; asst. under-sec. of state, 20th June, 1909; chmn., West African currency board, 1912- 1916; perm. under-sec. of state for the colonies; 10th Mar., 1916; sec. to the Order of St. Michael and St. George; ret., 1921.

FIDDIAN, ALEXANDER.—B. 1875; ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke Coll., 1893; 1st cls. classical honours, 1896; Goldsmiths' co. exhib., 1895; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1897; B.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897; asst. priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1st Apr., 1905; ditto to Earl of Elgin, Dec., 1905; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1907; mem. of deptm't. comte. on W. African med. staff, 1909; principal clk., 1st Nov., 1917; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; estabm. offr. for C.O. and Dominions office, 15th July,

FIELD, EDGAR JOHN.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls. and Christian Brothers' Coll., Adelaide; ent. survey dept., S. Australia, Jan., 1891; olk., office of commr. of crown lands, 1892; ch. clk. and ent., 1899; ch. immigr. offr., 1911; sec. for lands, chmn. of advances to settlers bd. and recr. of lands, 2nd Apr., 1917; chmn., irrigtn. comen., S. Aust., 1923; dir., lands, 1931.

FIELD, HENRY TREVOR CROMWELL.—B. 1891; ed. St. John's Sch., Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.): on war serv., 1914-18; demob. (capt.) 1919; instr. and schoolmast., S.P., Nigeria, 1922; supl., educn., 1926; prin., govt. coll., Ibadan, 1932.

FIELDGATE, ALAN FREDERIC EDMOND, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; ed. Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, 28th Apr., 1915; ag. dist. commr. on various occasions, 1918-21; dist. commr., 6th Aug., 1922: ag. pol. mag., Mar. to June, 1925.
FIENNES, LIEUT.-COL. THE HON. SIR

EUSTACE EDWARD TWISLETON-WYKEHAM, BART. —B. 1864; Lieut.-Col., Oxford Imp. Yeo.; served Riel rebellion, 1886 (medal with clasp); Egypt, 1887 and 1888 (medal with clasp, bronze star); pioneer expdn. to Mashonaland, 1890; South Africa, 1900 (medal with three clasps; twice ment. in desps.); European war, 1914 (France, Belgium, Dardanelles and Home); M.P. (L.) Banbury div. of Oxfordshire, 1906-1910, and 1910-1918; gov. and commr.-in-chief, Seychelles, 6th July, 1918; assumed govt., 8th Oct., 1918; gov., Leeward Is., 29th Oct., 1921; assumed govt., 4th Dec., 1921; ret., 1929.
FILLEUL, P. R.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1910; dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1918.
FINCH, A.—Asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1922; supt., pol. 1929.

FINCH, FRANK GEORGE.—B. 1882; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1907; asst. state engnr., Kedah, 1910-15; exec. engnr., F.M.S., 1913; state engnr., Pahang, 1923; state engnr., Selangor, Mar., 1927; dep. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., July, 1928; dir., drainage and irrign., S.S. and advr. on do., F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

FINDLAY, ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1886; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Sch., Aberdeen Univ. and North of Scotland Coll. of Agr.; M.A., B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A., N.D.D.; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1912; senr. supt., 1922; asst. dir., 1927; dep. dir., 1930; dir., agr., Zanzibar, 1931.

FINDLAY, GEORGE HUGO, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; ed. Repton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; asst. sec., S. Provs., 1922; cls. I, grade I, admstr. serv., 1928; ag. admstr. of colony, Lagos, 1930.
FINLAYSON, D. A.—B. 1892; home cust. and excise serv., 1912-24; war serv. in R.N., 1918; collr., cust., Sierra Leone, 1924; dep. dir., dept. of cust., excise and trade, Palestine, 1928.

FINLAYSON, GEORGE DANIEL.—B. 1883; ed. Pitotou Acad. and Dalhousie Univ.; B.A., 1907; apptd. to dept. of insurance, Canada, 1907; associate of Inst. of Actuaries of Great Britain, 1911; supt. of insurance, Canada, 1914; dep. min. of insurance, 1924.

FINN, BASIL ARTHUR.—B. 1888; ed. at Alleyne's Sch., Dulwich and Wadham Coll., OXFORD; B.A., 1912, 2nd cls. mod. hist.; asst. dist. offr., N. Provinces, Nigeria, Apr., 1914; attached, C.O., Dec., 1915, to Mar., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., Gambia, May, 1919; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1919 to 1930; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar., 1931.
FIRRE, TOM FREEMAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Crystal Palace Engng. Sch.; 1st asst. survr., Nyasaland Prot., June, 1903; asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1909.

FIRTH, OWEN WATTS.—B. 1884; ed. Tonbridge Sch.; asst. dist. commr., Nigeria, 1911; polit. offr., Udi-Okiigwi Patrol, 1915; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; cls. I, grade I, adm. serv., 1927; ag. prin. asst. sec., 1928.
FISCHER, PERCY U., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1878; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, S.A. Coll., Cape Town, Trinity Hall, Camb.; judge, sup. ct., South Africa, 1929.

FISET, MAJOR-GEN. SIR EUGENE KT. BACH (1917), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O., G.H.G.S., B.A., M.D.—B. 1878; staff adjt. med. corps, Canada, 1902; dir.-gen., med. services, 1903; dep. min., militia and defence, vice-pres., the militia coun. since 1906; reed. D.S.O. for service in S. Africa; mentioned in despatches; ret., 1924; mem. for county of Rimouski.

FISHER, BERTIE ST. JULIAN.—B. 1885; Fiji serv., 1909; dist. commr., 1918; legal dept., 1920; ag. dep. regt.-genl. and registr., sup. ct., 1921; ag. registr., sup. ct., curator of intestate and vacant estates, pub. trustee and offl. recr. in bankruptcy, 1923-27; registr.-gen. and registr., titles, 1929; registr., sup. ct., pub. trustee, offl. recr. in addn., Jan., 1931.

FISHER, JOHN CAMPBELL, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1880; ed. at Oakham Schl. and Sid. Suss. Coll., Cambridge; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., July, 1902; asst. auditor, Lagos, Sept., 1903; detached for duty in N. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; asst. aud., N. Nigeria, Apr., 1905; local audr., July, 1909; examnr., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1910; 1st div. clk. (2nd cls.), central office, May, 1910; aud., Malta, Mech., 1913; mem. of control board and hd. of food and commerce control office, Malta, 1917; senr. clk., cent. office, May, 1918; asst. dir. col. audit, Apr., 1928; deputy dir., Oct. 1929.
FISHER, HAROLD MAYSTON, L.D.S., R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1893; mil. serv., 1914-19; dental surg., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. dental surg., 1930.

FISHER, REV. ROBERT, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. Cambridge; asst. mast., educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1921; house mast., 1922; mast., Achimota Coll., 1926; supt., educn., S. Prov., Nigeria, 1928; prin., govt. coll., Umuahia, 1928.

FISHER, SIR STANLEY, KT. B.A.OH. (1922).—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford, M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrenia, Cyprus, May, 1902; puisane judge, Cyprus, 1911; ch. just., Cyprus, 1919; on sp. mission to Cairo under F.O., 24th Jan. to 14th Mar., 1924; ch. just., Trinidad, 1924; ch. just., Ceylon, 1926; ret., Aug., 1930; passed exam. in modern Greek, Dec., 1903 and in Turkish, 1918; joint commr. for compiling revised edition of Statute Laws of Cyprus (published 1907), for which reed. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.; joint commr. under reprint of statutes law, 1913; edition published, Feb., 1914; commr. for laws, under reprint of statutes law, 1922; edition published, 1924; compiler of "The Mukhtar's Handbook," 1911; published "Ottoman Land Laws," 1919.
FISHER, V. M.—Medical offr., K.A.P., Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1926; prin. lab. inspr., Feb., 1928.

FITCHETT, WILLIAM HERBERT.—Clk., agric. dept., Cape Colony, Feb., 1895; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Wodehouse, Sept., 1898; Port Elizabeth, Oct., 1899; Colesberg, Mar., 1900; Britstown, Aug., 1901; acted as C.C. and R.M., Britstown, 1902; asst. registr., high ct., and chief clk. to the sheriff of the O.R.C., July, 1902; acted as registr. and sheriff in 1903 and 1904; registr. and sheriff, O.F.S., Aug., 1921.

FITZGERALD, D. R. DESMOND, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin), D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.), certif. (hons.) L.S.T.M., M.C.—B. 1885; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.O., Penang, July, 1913; do., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1912; health offr., Malacca, Sept., 1914; med. offr., gen. hosp., Singapore, Sept., 1914; served in European war, 1914-19 (ment. in desps., M.C.); med. offr., Tan Tock Seng hosp. and surg. native hosp., Singapore, Dec., 1919; M.O., gen. hosp., Singapore, Apr., 1920; med. offr. in ch., officials, pol. surg. and prison surg., Jan., 1921; prin. med. offr., Johore, June, 1923; off. del., Far Eastern Asso. Trop. Med., Calcutta, Dec., 1927.
FITZGERALD, THOMAS, O.B.E. (1927).—F.M.S. postal service; dep. P.M.G., Kenya, July, 1920; P.M.G., Jan., 1922.

FITZGERALD, WILLIAM JAMES.—ed. Blackrock Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.C. and Croix de Guerre (French); adm. serv., Nigeria, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1924; solr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932.

FITZGIBBON, HENRY ELLIOT.—B. 1889; ed. St. Stephen's Green Sch., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., B.A.I., Assoc. M. Inst. C.E., M. Inst. M. & Cy. E., M.T.P.I.; inspr., municipalities, Egypt, 1913; war serv. with E.E.F. (ment. in desps.); dir., irrig. and mech. dept., min. of wakfa., Egypt, 1919; Order of the Nile, 4th cls.; munici. and town planning engr., Kenya, 1928; Pickering prize and gold medal., Inst. M. and Cy. E., 1931.

FITZ-GIBBON, WILLIAM GUERRIN.—B.A., Royal Univ. of Ireland; B. 1886; rly. dept., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st clk., col. secretariat, Hong Kong, 1917; ag. ch. clk., col. secretariat, 1921 and 1924; J.P., 1924; sec., P.W.D., 1927.

FITZ-HENRY, WILLIAM COOPER, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Dulwich Coll.; asst. engr., Nigerian rlya., 1908; lieut.-E.R., 1915; capt., R.E., 1916; served with miny. of munitions and admy., 1918; in India on rly. work, 1920-27; road engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927; ch. road engr., N. Rhodesia, July, 1928; ag. D.P.W., Aug., 1930.
FITZMAURICE, ALEXANDER LINDSAY—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (1909); M.B., B.S. (London), 1913; late out-patient offr., asst. house surg. and house physician, Guy's Hosp.; med. offr. Somaliand Prot., 1914.

FITZPATRICK, RIGHT HON. SIR CHARLES, P.C. (1906); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.M.G. (1907).—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; elected to Quebec legislature, 1890; to Dominion parl., 1896, again in 1900 and 1904; solr.-gen., Canada, 1896; min. of just., 1902; ch. just. of Canada, 1906-1918; mem. of Hague tribunal of arbitration, 1908; lieut.-gov. of Quebec, 1918 to 1923.

FITZPATRICK, JAMES PATRICK, L.R.C.P. and S. (Ireland).—B.1885; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; ag. med. supt., Central Mental Hosp., Tanjong Rambutan, May-Dec., 1929; med. offr., Seremban, Jan., 1931.

FITZROY, HENRY SOMERSET, B.A., LL.M., R.N.—B., 1870 (schol. of Trinity Hall, Cambridge); law tripos, part II (1st cls.), 1893; served R.N., 1896-1905; called to bar, 1910; served in admry. and in New York, 1914-18; asst. to atty.-gen., Hong Kong, Apr., 1927; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1929; pub. pros. and asst. to atty.-gen., 1930.
FLANAGAN, MAURICE JOSEPH, B.A. (Cantab.)—Aast. audr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; audr., Cameroons Expdy. Force, 1916; audr., Windward Is., June, 1919; audr., Nyasaland, Oct., 1925.

FLAVIN, FRANK.—B. 1888; Imp. posts and tel., July, 1905; postmr., Nyasaland, 26th Sept., 1919; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Mar., 1922 to Feb., 1923; asst. postmr.-gen., 3rd Feb., 1923; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1923 to June, 1924, Feb., 1927 and June 1929 to Apr., 1930.

FLECK, FRANK.—B. 1875; clk., educ. dept., O.F.S., July, 1897; clk., orphan mast., O.F.S., Oct., 1899; clk. (first), educ. dept., O.F.S., Feb., 1901; clk., atty.-gen.'s office, O.F.S., Sept., 1901; clk., master's office, high st., Bloemfontein, 1902; asst. res. mag., Vrededorf dist. (Parys), June, 1902; asst. res. mag., Faureamith dist., Aug., 1903; asst. res. mag., Vierfontein, Aug., 1906; asst. res. mag., Bothaville, Feb., 1910; asst. res. mag., Reitz, Apr., 1912; detached asst. mag., Dewetsdorp, Nov., 1913; detached asst. mag., Zastron, Feb., 1919; mag., Zastron, Oct., 1919; pub. serv. inspr., Pretoria, May, 1928; dir., prisons, Pretoria, Nov., 1929.

FLECKER, ORIEL JOYCE, B.Sc.—Ed. Cheltenham Ladies' Coll.; assoc. of Cheltenham Ladies' Coll., 1908; gold med., Société Nationale des Professeurs de Français en Angleterre, 1909; B. Sc., Lond., 1912; Somerville Coll., Oxford, 1924-5; headmistress, girls' schl., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925; author of "A School Chemistry," 1923.

FLEMING, ERNEST.—B. 1891; 5th class clk., crown agts. office, Oct., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd division of the civ. serv. and temporarily employed in the Bd. of Trade, Jan., 1912; assigned to C.O., April, 1912; on military service from 25th May, 1915 to 9th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; 2nd asst. acctnt., 22nd Oct., 1930.

FLEMING, COL. JOHN GIBSON, C.B.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1916), late R.E.—B. 1880; ed. Haileybury Coll. and R. Milly Acad., Woolwich; joined R.E.'s 1898; served S. African War, 1901-2 (Queen's med. 5 clasps); European War, 1914-18 (desp. D.S.O., bt. lt.-col., C.B.E.); ret. with rank of col., 1924; apptd. fourth Crown Agent and engrn.-in-ch., 1st June, 1932.
FLEMING, W. P.—B. 1887; apptd., after compet. exam., S. and T. clk., G.P.O.; lent to C.O., 9th May, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Sept., 1921; on mil. serv., 4th Apr., 1915 to 4th May, 1919.

FLETCHER, SIR ARTHUR GEORGE MURCHISON, K.C.M.G. (1930), KT. BACH (1929), C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford (exhibt.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang St. May-June, 1903; ag. asst. regiar. gen., 1903; J.P., 1904; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1906-8; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offr. recvr., 1909; asst. to atty.-gen., 1909; regiar., trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 1911; ag. dep. regiar., 1911; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1912; offr. recvr., 1912; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1912; ag. treasr., 1913; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Aug., 1913; priv. sec. to O.A.G., and to gov., Aug.-Dec., 1913; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. and leg. couns. in 1916, 1917, 1918, 1919, 1920 and 1922; chmn., shipping control comte., 1917-19; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1923 and 1925; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1926; O.A.G., May-Aug., 1927; gov., Fiji and high comm., W. Pacific, 1929.
FLETCHER, REGINALD H.—Joined Jamaica civ. serv., post office dept., by compet. exam., 1890; 1st cls. clk., 1908; ch. clk., 1915; asst. postmr., 1921; postmr., Jamaica, 1925.

FLEURKT, ARTHUR ISADORE.—B. 1899; mesgr. O.S.O., Falkland Is., Dec., 1912; ag. jun.clk., Jan., 1914 to Sept., 1919; clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1919; clk., C.S.O., Jan., 1924; ch. clk. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1928; offr.-in-ch., secretariat, Oct., 1928 to Jan., 1929 and Feb. to June, 1931; 2nd lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, Jan., 1931.

FLEURY, CAPT. ARTHUR MARLEY, O.B.E. (1932).—Second lieut., 3rd Royal Irish Fusiliers, May, 1900; capt., May, 1906; lieut., W.A.F.F., July, 1900; Ashanti campaign (medal), 1900; dist. comsnr., N. Territories, G. Coast, Jan., 1907; local comdt., Cyprus mil. police, Feb., 1910; comsnr., Nov., 1919; ag. local commdt., pol., Apr., 1930.

FLINN, MAJOR WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1895; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Royal Irish Regt. and staff, 1914-19; adminstr., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Mar., 1921; A.D.O. and priv. sec. to H. Comsnr., 14th Apr., 1921; ag. clk., exec. coun., Feb.-Mar., 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Apr., 1922; ag. ch. asst. sec., July, 1922; ag. comsnr., Sept., 1922; ag. local commdt. police, Sept., 1922; ag. ch. asst. sec., June to July, 1923; ag. regist., co-op. societies, June, 1923; regist., co-op. societies, July, 1923; ag. regist., co-op. credit societies, June, 1923 to Apr., 1924 and from Oct., 1924; ag. ch. asst. sec. for various periods, 1924-27; ag. col. sec. in 1926 and 1927; trade comsnr. for Cyprus in London, July, 1927.

FLOOD, J. E. W., C.M.G. (1933)—B. 1886; ed. at Portora Royal Schl., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dublin, B.A. (scholar univ. student); apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Oct., 1910; sec., edible nuts comte., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 15th Oct., 1918; on military service from 16th Apr., 1917 to 7th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., Feb., 1926; vice-chmn., col. advisory med. and sany. comte., 1924-26; mem. W. Afr. Currency Board, 1928.
FLYNN, J. B., B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Drogheda Grammar Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, 1928.

FOLEY, GERALD ROBERT EDWARD, O.B.E., B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Tipperary Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. and senr. moderator in maths., Dublin Univ.; Royal Irish Constab., 1911-22; war serv. with R. Irish Regt. in France, Palestine and Salonica (rank major); twice ment. in desp. and awarded O.B.E. (mil.); asst. comdt., Br. gendarmerie, Palestine, 1922; comdt., Police Training Schl., 1926; dist. supt., pol., N. dist., 1927.

FOLLIT, HAROLD HARRY BAILEY.—B. 1883; ed. Bedford, Heidelberg and Cambridge; St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; European War, 1914-18; Egypt, Gallipoli and France; med offr., Nyasaland, 1921; senr. health offr., Tanganyika, 1928; senr. health offr., Nyasaland, 1931.

FOLLOWS, CHARLES GEOFFREY SHIELD.—B. 1896; ed. Wellington; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; served in France, 1915-18, latterly as adjt.; on staff, 1918 and 1919, attached to H.Q., Southern dist., Ireland, and H.Q., Aldershot command; priv. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and clk. to couns., 1920; attd. O.O., May-Oct., 1925; asst. sec., Gibraltar, 1925; ch. asst. sec., 1927; ag. col. sec. on various occasions.

FOORD, FRANCIS LATTON.—B. 1874; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, S. African War, 1899-1902; lieut., 1st S. Staffs. regt.; sub-instr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1902; instr., 1913; asst. comnr., 1914; accompanied Basuto deputations to England in 1909 and 1919; mem., poor white comnr., 1918; govt. sec., 1930; dep. res. comnr. and govt. sec., 1931; ag. res. comnr., Mar. to Oct., 1932.
FORAN, WILLIAM—B. 1871; entered civ. serv., Canada, 1890; 2nd cl. clk., 1894; sec., bd. of civ. ser. examns., 1895; 1st cl. clk., 1906; sec. of civ. ser. comnr., 1908.

FORBES, Rt. Hon. GEORGE WILLIAM, P.C. (1930).—B. 1869; ed. Boys' High Schl., Christchurch; mem., N.Z. parl., since 1908; senr. whip, Liberal party, 1912-22; leader of opposition, 1925; leader of National party, 1925-28; min., lands and agr., 1928-30; prime min., min. of finance, external affrs., cust., and stamp duties, 1930; prime min., min. of rlya. and min. of external affrs. in coalition min., 1931.

FORBES, HARRY.—B. 1873; cust. dept., Fiji, 1911; clk. of peace, Rewa, Fiji, 1914-18; J.P., 1916; cashier, treas., 1918; ag. acot., Gilbert & Ellioe Col., June, 1921; ag. treas., ch. postmr. and collr. of cust., June-Dec., 1922; acct., Br. residency, New Hebrides, Aug., 1923.
FORBES, J. H.—B. 1884; ed. Dulwich Coll.; lieut., Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; asst. engnr., Baro-Kano rly., 1911; divn. engnr., 1926.

FORBES, LESLIE.—B. 1889; B.A., Oxon. (lit. hum.), 1913; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; lent to Kedah govt., 1914; sec. to adviser, Kedah, 1916; dist. offr., Dec., 1920-22; offg. dist. offr., Bentong, Feb., 1925; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Feb., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; collr., land rev., Seremban, Feb., 1931.
FORBES, PAUL HENRY.—B. 1892; admiralty (war staff), Aug.-Oct., 1914; B.E.F., 1914-19; asst. ch. acot., Palestine rly., Aug., 1921; ch. acot., Aug., 1923; ch. acot., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1926.

FORRER, HENRY AUGUSTUS, M.A. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1886; cadet S.S. Oct., 1909; passed cadet, Aug., 1913; supernum. offr. of cls. V., Mar., 1914; att. to censor's office, May 1915; services lent to the F.M.S., Jan., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, July, 1921; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1921; 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, Dec., 1923; dep. regist., and asst. offl. assignee, Penang, Sept., 1924; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1926; regist., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1927; regist., sup. ct., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, July, 1930.

FORREST, ARTHUR ERIC.—B. 1892; asst. audr., E. Africa Prot., July, 1915; on active service, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1916; asst. audr. in charge, Somaliland, 1917; ag. senr. asst. audr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1917 to Apr., 1919, Nov., 1919 to Jan., 1920; asst. audr. in charge Zanzibar, Feb., 1920; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1922; senr. asst. treas., Nov., 1925; treas. acot., Jan., 1926; mem. and sec., Kenya cost of living comnr., 1926; ag. dep. treas., June, 1926 to Nov., 1927 and Aug., 1929 to Apr., 1930; title of subst. post changed to prim. asst. treas., 1928; dep. treas., Uganda, Apr., 1930; ag. treas., Apr., Dec., 1930 and May, 1932.

FORREST, ROBERT ANDREW DERMOD.—B. 1893; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1914; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct. 1919; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Jan., 1922; attd., educn. dept., Jan., 1922; ag. 2nd pol. mag., May, 1925; asst. head, sany. dept., Nov., 1925; ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1928; asst. treas. and dep. estate duty comnr., 1931.

FORSTER, BABON (cr. 1919), Rt. Hon. HENRY WILLIAM, P.C. (1917), G.C.M.G. (1920), D.L.—B. 1866; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford; M.P., Sevenoaks Kent, 1892-1919; a lord comnr. of the treas., 1902-05; finan. sec. to the W.O., 1915-19; mem. of army council; gov.-gen. and comdr-in-chief, Commonwealth of Austr., 1920-25.
FORSYTH, L. E. W.—B. 1877; ag. comnr., Mangrove Cay, Bahamas, 1922; Out Island comnr., 4th grade, 1927; 2nd grade, July, 1927.
FOSBROOKE, HENRY ALBERT, B.A.—B.1906; ed. Poolecking Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.
FOSDIKE, ALFRED ERNEST.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1907-1917; contrlr. of stores, 1917.

FOSTER, LIONEL HUBERT LISLE.—B. 1889; ed. Bradfield and Christ Ch. Oxford (B.A.1912); min. of educn., Egypt, Jan., 1913; asst. res., Nyassaland, Jan., 1915; res., 1st grade, Apr., 1925; ag. asst. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932.

FOSTER, R. S.—B. 1894, apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service; temp. employed at Nat. Hi'th. Insurance Comnr., Mar., 1913 to June, 1914; assigned to C.O., June, 1914; on military service from Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; asst. aud., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; do., Sierra Leone, Feb., 1923; do., Gold Coast, Nov., 1931; ag. dep. audr., Nov., 1931 and May, 1932.

FOSTER, ROBERT SPENCER, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1891; ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl. and St. Edmund's Hall, Oxford; 2nd lieut., K.S.L.I., 1915; K.A.R., 1917-18; head mast., educn. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. dir., educn., Aug. to Oct., 1924; ag. dep. dir., educn., Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; Swahili examr.; dep. dir., educn., Uganda, 1930.

FOSTER, HON. WALTER EDWARD.—B. 1874; ed. St. John Pub. and Grammar Schls.; chosen leader of opposition liberal party, New Brunswick, 1916; premier, N.B., Apr., 1917; sec. of state of Canada, Sept., 1925; senator, Dec., 1928.

FOSTER, WILLIAM, M.B.E.—B. 1880; served in S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., 3 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; Br. post office, 1897-1920; serv. in Gt. War, 1914-20; ment. in desps.; major, 1920; dep. postmnr.-gen., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. postmnr.-gen. on several occasions.

FOULGER, HARRY CECIL, M.A. (1928).—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; capt., R. Fusiliers; asst. res., Nyassaland Prot., Aug.1914; attd., K.A.R., ag. pvtc. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., 1919; res., 1st grade, Jan., 1924.

FOURIE, THE HON. A. P. J.—Formerly mem., Cape prov. coun. and subsequently mem., H. of A. for Somerset East; admstr., Prov. of the Cape of Good Hope, Jan., 1926; min. of mines and industries, Aug., 1929; del., Ottawa Econ. Confce., 1932.

FOURIE, LOUIS, M.B.E.—B. 1878; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edin. Univ.; Berlin; M.B., Ch.B. (Hons) (Conan Doyle Prizeman); D.P.H., F.Z.S., F.R.A.I., Fell. Soc. M.O.R. M.R. San. Inst., mem., Amer. Anthorp. Assoon., res. med. offr., R. Infirmary, 1906; city fever hosp., 1906; roy. hosp. sick. child., Edin., 1906-7; h.-surg., Paddington Green Child. hosp., Lond., 1907; act. serv., Boer Forces, 1899-1900; D.S. Taunga, 1913-15; capt., S.M.A.C., 1916-19; M.O. to admtn., S.W.A., 1920-29; asst. health offr., Union Health Dep., 1930.
FOWLIS, HENRY GEORGE.—Entd. judl. dept., Gambia, 1890; gov't. offr., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-06; ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

FOXLEE, RICHARD WILLIAM, M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1885; ed. Westminster Sch.; served in engnr's. dept., G.N.R., 1903-09; G.C.R., 1909-15; P.L.A., 1915-21; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1921; dep. ch. engnr. (civ.), 1928.

FRANCE, HENRY DUNLOP.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., treas., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904; 1st cls. supervisor of cust., Aug., 1905; ag. inspr. cust. preven. serv., Mar.-Oct., 1911; ag. inspr. maritime cust., Oct., 1916 to Jan., 1917; prov. collr. cust., Jan., 1921.

FRANCIS, BERTRAM ALEXANDER, O.B.E. (1928)—B. 1878; ed. St. Edmunds Sch., Canterbury, and St. Catherine's Coll., Camb.; B.A. (class. tripos), 1900; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1900; lieut., 1902; inspr. of immigrts, Mauritius, 1908; ag. auditor-gen., Jan. to June, 1909; prot. of immigrts., and poor law comsnr., 1912; temp. capt., B.G.A., Jan., 1915 to 1918; ag. reovr.-gen., Feb. to Nov., 1919, Dec., 1919 to Jan., 1922 and from Nov., 1922 to Aug., 1923; chmn., profiteering comte., May, 1920; collr. of cust. and harmr., Aug., 1924; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1927 to May, 1928 and Dec.-Aug., 1930; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1929 to Dec., 1930.

FRANCIS, CLIFFORD CLAUDE.—B. 1889; ed. at King's Sch., Rochester, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; ag. pay and qrtmr. and clk., Fiji constab., Feb., 1908; clk. of the peace, Taveuni, Aug., 1909; served in audit and reovr.-general's dept.; asst. matr., Queen Victoria schl., Nainiu, Fiji, Feb., 1910; ag. headmaster, ditto, Apl. to July, 1911; ag. ch. clk., native dept., Sept., 1911, to Feb., 1912; cadet, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., June, 1912; passed cadet, May, 1914; ag. adjt., local forces, Aug., 1914; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific, Dec., 1914; lieut. E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; capt., Apr., 1916; served in France and Flanders, 1916-17; dist. offr., Gizo, Oct., 1919; ag. res. comsnr., Br. Solomon Is. prot., Oct., 1922; rep. natives of Gizo dist. in claims before lands comsnr., 1923; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, May, 1923; judl. comsnr. for W. Pacific in New Hebrides, Aug., 1925; Br. judge, joint ct., New Hebrides condominium, Aug., 1925; pol. mag., Nigeria, 1930.

FRANCIS, CYRIL GERARD BROOKE.—Apptd. Fiji civ. serv., 1898; lieut., armed native constab., 1901; certif., musketry and machine gun, Hythe, 1905; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1907; inspr., Fiji constab., 1907; barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1909; A.D.C. to ag. gov., Dec., 1908 to 1909; ag. inspr.-genl. constab., and prisons, 1909 to 1910; A.D.C. to ag. gov., Aug., 1910 to Feb., 1911; ag. comsnr. and stip. mag., 1911-12; ag. chief pol. mag., July to Aug., 1911; ag. inspr.-genl., constab., and prisons, 1912 to 1913; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., May, 1912; ag. chief pol. mag., Mar. 1913 to Dec., 1914; 2nd in comd. local forces with rank of major, 5th Aug., 1914; mentioned by Army Council for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji; capt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; coy. comdr., K.A.R., E. Africa, 1916-19; 1st grade dist. comsnr., Fiji, Jan., 1916; regnr.-gen., regnr., sup. ct., comsnr. stamps, and pub. trustee, Fiji, Jan., 1920; cust., enemy property and contr. L.C.O., Fiji and W. Pacific, Feb., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1920 to May, 1921; comsnr. for the revision of the laws of Fiji, 1920 (not completed); atty.-gen., B. Honduras, July 1921; ag. ch. just., July, 1921 to May, 1922 and from July to Nov., 1923; chmn., comsn. to enquire into outbreak of yellow fever, 1921; chmn., retrenchment comte., 1922; chmn., franchise comsn., 1923; revd. and consol. laws, B. Hond., 1924; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.
FRANCIS, JOSIAH.—Served European war with A.I.F., 1916-19; M.H.R., Commonwealth of Australia since 1922; hon. min., Lyons' ministry, 1932.

FRANCIS, WILLIAM, F.I.O.—Ed. Glasgow Univ.; "Malcolm Kerr" bursar in chem. and asst. to prof. of chem., Glasgow Univ., 1890; asst. to Sir C. A. Cameron, C.B., govt. analyst, Ireland, 1892-4; govt. analyst, agrl. chemist and lect. in chem., to the govt. of Cyprus, 1900-20; ag. island postmr., asst censor, and pres. of the note currency bd., Cyprus, 1915-20; dep. govt. analyst and geologist, B. Guiana, 1920; ag. dir. of science and agr., Feb.-July, 1921 and in 1922; asst. dir., science and agr., B. Guiana, 1922; ag. dir., science and agr. and chmn., bd. of agr., Feb. to Nov., 1926; govt. analyst, 1927; senr. editor, B. Guiana Handbook, 1922.
FRANCKLIN, B. J. F.—Asst. jun. staff survr., E. Africa Prot., 1914; asst. dist. comsnr., May, 1915; 2nd grade res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade admstve.-offr., Apr., 1925.
FRANKS, JOHN WILLIAM.—B. 1879; prison coman., H.O., June, 1899; asst. supt., Victoria Gaol, Hong Kong, Nov., 1913; asst. supt. pol. res., Feb., 1919; supt. prisons, Dec., 1920.
FRASER, ALASTAIR GARDEN, B.A., B. 1902; ed. Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 3rd cls. hons., phil., polit., and econ.; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, REV. ALEXANDER GARDEN, C.B.E. (1930), M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Merchiston Castle, Edin., Edin. Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; Jesoon, 1913; priest, 1915; C.M.S., Uganda, 1900-03; prin., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1904; chaplain with 3rd divn., France, 1917-18; chaplain to the forces; prin., Prince of Wales Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.
FRASER, H.—Ed. Grammar Schl., Inveraray George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., 1925; asst. conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

FRASER, HUGH.—B. 1890; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., 1913; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., Apr., 1914-Feb., 1915; ag. supervisor of cust., Port Dickson, May, 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Jan., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cl. V., Dec., 1916; 3rd asst. sec., Fed. sect., Aug., 1917, priv. sec. to ch. sec. in addn., Sept., 1917; dist. offr., Jelebu, Feb., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Sept., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1921; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1924; offr., class III., Nov., 1925; ag. asst. advr., Kedah, Mar., 1927; ag. temp. legal adviser, Kedah in addn., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, June, 1929; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dist. offr., Larut and regir., titles, Perak, Nov., 1930.

FRASER, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.C., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1896; ed. Trinity Academy, Leith, and Edin. Univ.; on mil'y. serv., 1915-19; cadet, Hong Kong, 1919; attd. to sany. dept., 1921; 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; 2nd pol. mag., Apr., 1922; dist. offr. (North), June, 1922; passed in Cantonese, 1922; asst. head, sany. dept. and sec. to sany. bdr., 1922; ag. head, sany. dept., 1923; dist. offr., South, 1924; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1925; dist. offr., North, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., North and South Sept., 1928; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1931; pol. mag., Kowloon, July, 1931.

FRASER, SIR JOHN GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1864; cadet Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1887; ag. dist. judge, &c., Badulla, Feb., 1894; asst. govt. agt., Chilaw, Dec., 1899; land settm. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., 4th Jan., 1912; ag. controller of revenue, in 1911, 1912, 1913, 1915 and 1916; govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1914; M.L.C.; ret., 1924.

FRASER, MALCOLM, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1873; ed. at Inverness; entd. N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1893; acct., pub. serv. superannuation fund, 1908; govt. statistician 1911-32, also comsnr. of unemployment, 1930-32; admstr. enrolment and balloting, Expedy. Force Reserve, 1916-18; N.Z. rep. at British Empire Confce. of Statisticians, 1920; under sec., internal affrs., 1932.

FRASER, WALTER MORTIMER.—B. 1865; 3rd cls. clk., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1st Feb., 1886; acctnt. and clk., lands dept., Oct., 1890; ag. 1st cls. clk., record office, Aug., 1900 to May, 1904; 1st cls. clk., treasy., Nov., 1910; acctnt., govt. savings bank, Nov., 1918; ch. clk. and acctnt., treasy., July, 1920; dep. treas., Apr., 1921; treas., Apr., 1925; nom. mem., leg. coun.
FRASER, WINFORD BLACHE.—B. 1868; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; mag., St. Andrew, Trinidad, Jan., 1923; mag., Caroni, Nov., 1923; mag., St. George East, Dec., 1924.

FRAZER, HON. FRANCIS VERNON, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Nelson Coll., Otago Univ. Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; barrister and sol'r., stip. mag., 1911; chmn., N.Z. pub. serv. appeal bd., 1918; asst. pub. serv. comsnr., 1920; judge, st. of arbitn., 1921.
FREEMAN, B. J.—Asst. chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1918; dep. ch. acctnt., 1924; asst. P.M.G., Kenya, 1931.

FREEMAN, GEOFFREY JAMES.—B. 1889; ed. privately and in Clongoweswood Coll., Ireland; served in Royal Dublin Fusiliers and Royal Munster Fusiliers in the Great War, 1914-21; collr., cust., Gold Coast, 1922; senr. do., Feb., 1932.

FREESTON, LESLIE BRIAN, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. at Willaston Schl., Nantwich and Oxford; scholar New Coll., 1911-19; 2nd cls. hons. mod., 1913; B.A. (war), 1919; on mil. ser., 17th Sept., 1914 to 8th Mar., 1919; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Mar. to 15th July, 1919; appt. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th July, 1919; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-secr., 18th June, 1920; sent to Ceylon for temp. serv. as sec., salaries comsnr., 1921; act. prin., 6th Dec., 1922; prin., 1st April, 1924; visited Bahamas and Br. Hond. on duty, 1927; seconded for serv. with Col. Development Advisory Comte., 1st Feb., 1930.

FRENCH, LAWRENCE, I.C.S. (Ret.).—Ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; served as colonisation offr., Chenal Canal, Punjab, afterwards as dep. comsnr. and joint ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, 1896-1921; dir., development, Palestine, 1931.

FRERE, SIR BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse schl. and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres., dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transf. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902; atty.-gen., 1911; ch. just., Apl., 1914; judge, prize ot., Aug., 1914; cable censor during European War; ret., Aug., 1922.

FRERE, NOEL GRAY.—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Schl., N.Z., and Cranleigh Schl.; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone, July, 1909; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsnr., N. Prov., in 1921, 1923, 1926 and 1928; prov. comsnr., Jan., 1929.

FRETZ, WILMOT THEODORE STUART.—B. 1889; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; Rhodesian admistrn., B.S.A.P., N. and S. Rhodesia, 1913-19; Transvaal Border Patrols, 1918; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1921; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1923; chmn., agr. comsnr., 1924; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, July, 1925.

FREYBERG, CAPT. GEOFFREY HERBERT, R.N. (Retd.), O.B.E. (Mil.).—B. 1881; served in R. Navy, 1895-1926; China med., 1900; ment. in desps. for services at Battle of Jutland when comdr., H.M.S. "Valiant"; awarded O.B.E. (Mil.) for services afloat during the Great War; King's harbrmr., Plymouth, from 1919; elec. a younger brother, Trinity House Corp., 1921; served at adm'y., 1922; master attendant, S. Stiltns., Apr., 1926.

FRITH, GEOFFREY HAMMOND.—Served in the Great War; just. of peace, Turks and Caicos Is., 1919; ch. clk. to comsnr., clk. to leg. bd., sec. to bd. of educon., 1920; ag. audr. and inspr., pol., 1921; sub-inspr., pol., 1922; ast. treas., collr. of cust., postmr., gen. man. of savings bank, regist. of shipping, 1923; ag. ast. comsnr., mag., etc., 1927; ag. comsnr. and judge, grand ct., Cayman Islands, 1929; sub. treas., etc., Tobago, 1931.

FRODSHAM, FEARN HAMMERSLEY.—B. 1882; comptg. draftman, Tasmania govt., survey dept., 1900-04; field, 1904-09; field, Queensland govt., survey dept., 1910-12; senr. rev. survey br., F.M.S., June, 1912; survr., grade I, June, 1918; ast. supt., rev. surveys, Jan. 1919; ag. supt., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, Nov., 1928-Feb., 1930; ditto, Penang, Feb., 1931.

FUAD, MUSTAFA.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; ed. Turkish Schl., St. Paul's Schl., and English Schl., Nicosia, Cyprus; clk., treasy., Cyprus, 1905; inspr., pol. and dep. coroner, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Limassol, 1913; judge, dist. ct., Nicosia, 1916; mem., legal bd., 1916; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; pres., abbr. ct. of appeal, 1927.

FULLER, HON. SIR GEORGE WARBURTON, K.C.M.G. (1919).—Elected to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903 and 1906; min. of home affairs, C. of A., June, 1909 to Apr., 1910; col. sec., N. S. Wales, Nov., 1916-20; premier, 1922-26; agt.-gen., N. S.W., 1926-31.

FULTON, ADAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1897; lieut., May, 1915; capt., 1916; on active serv. with 1st, 6th and 2nd batts., Border Regt., Gallipoli, Sept. to Nov., 1915; Egypt and Suez defences, Jan. to July, 1916; France, July to Sept., 1916 and from Jan. to Aug., 1917; wounded, 26th Sept., 1916; Italy, Dec., 1918 to Jan., 1919; demob., Jan., 1919; vety. offr., Gold Coast, 18th May, 1926.

FURKERT, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.M.G (1926), M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1876; ed. Hokitika High Sch., Wellington and Dunedin Tech. Schls., and Otago Univ., N.Z.; joined N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1894; res. engrnr., 1903; inspecting engrnr., 1912; asst. engrnr.-in-chief, 1919; engrnr.-in-chief and under sec., 1920.

FURNISS, SIR ROBERT HOWARD, Kt. Bach. (1919).—B. 1880; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man; solr. (hons.), 1902; practised at bar of B. Honduras, 1906-13; regir.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; served with B. W. I. Regt., 1915-19; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 9th Jan., 1920; senr. mag., Apr., 1921; solr. gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; ch. just., Barbados, Oct., 1926.

FURNESS-SMITH, CECIL, Barrister-at-law.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; asst. dist. comnrr., G. Const., Apr., 1914; dist. comnrr., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1922; crown coun., Mar., 1925; ag. solr.-gen., May-Oct., 1927; ag. circuit judge, 1929; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1932.

FURSE, MAJOR R. D., D.S.O.—B. 1887; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Mr. Lewis (now Lord Harcourt), Dec., 1910 to Aug., 1914; on active service in 1st King Edward's Horse, K.O.D.R., 5th Aug., 1914 to 26th Nov., 1918; served in France and Italy, Apl., 1915 to 26th Nov., 1918; promoted capt., 1914; major, 1916; wounded, 1917; D.S.O., July, 1918; Bar to D.S.O., gazetted, Mar., 1919; twice ment. in desps., asst. priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state (Lord Milner), May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state, Mr. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, 8th June, 1929; visited Canada on C.O. business, 1922, and again in 1923 as rep. at Imp. Forestry Confce; mem., bd. of govs., Imp. Forestry Inst; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1930.

GALIA, ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1930)—B. 1872; ed. Malta Lyceum; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1891; 3rd cls. elk., 1st Jan., 1896; 2nd cls. elk., 1st July, 1901; asst. regir. ots. of judicial pol., 17th Dec., 1913; regir., 6th Jan., 1914; transfd., secretariat, 14th May, 1918; ch. elk., lieut.-gov.'s office, Sept., 1920; ch. elk. to legislature, 20th Dec., 1921; compt., charitable institutions, 4th Apr., 1923; off. sec. to head of ministry, 1st Mar., 1927.

GALIA, PROF. ROBERT V., L.S. & A.—B. 1882; matric. (with honrs.), Malta Univ., 1900; graduated as land survr. and architect, 1903; lect. on engrnr. and architecture, Malta Univ., 1905; prof., ditto, 1915; ag. rector of univ., 1923; al. for 1st divn. in leg. assem., June, 1924; r-v-el., Aug., 1927; min. for pub. health and charitable institutions, 16th Aug., 1927, also min. for treas., 7th Aug., 1929.

GALL, GEORGE LOUIS BROCKLES.—B. 1880; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, 1897; ent. gov't. serv., B. Guiana, 1897; J.P., 1902; 3rd grade comnry. of taxation, 1904; sworn weigher and gauger, 1905; qual. in colloquial Hindi, 1909; ag. imigrn. agt. in addn., 1911; sec. to rum duties comnry., 1912; ag. imigrn. agt. in addn., 1913; supervisor, pub. wks. in addn., 1915; 1st grade comnry. of taxation, Oct. 1915; sec., sugar comnctee., 1916; ag. prin. elk., corp. sec.'s office, June to Dec., 1924; ag. ch. comnry. in Jan. and Feb., 1925; dir. of co-op. socs., 1st Jan., 1926; ag. ch. comnry., 24th July, 1926; ag. col. treas. from June, 1927.

GALLAGHER, LIEUT.-COL. ALBERT ERNEST, C.B.E. (1927), D.S.O., (1907).—Reserve of offrs.; B. 1872; ed. privately; served in S. African war, 1900; served with N. Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 1901-1908; Argungu operations, 1902 (desps.); Sokoto expdn., 1903; Sokoto expdn., 1906 (wounded, desps. D.S.O.); asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; local comdnt. Cyprus, mil. police, 1909; chief comdnt. Cyprus mil. police, 1915; served with 7th Royal Munster Fus., 10th Divn., Dardanelles, 1915, and Serbia, 1915; major and 2nd in command, 11th Worcester Regt., Salonika Force, Sept., 1916; served with 7th Royal Fus. (63rd Div.) as 2nd in command, 1917, B.E.F., France; lieut.-col. comdng. 2nd Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers, Sept., 1918, B.E.F., France; wounded, Oct., 1918; resumed duties as chief comdnt., Cyprus mil. police, Aug., 1919; ag. regr. gen. in 1921 and 1922; Br. del. of Evcaf., Aug., 1920; mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1922; mem., leg. coun., 1925-31.

GALLIHER, HON. WM. ALFRED.—B. 1860; ed. pub. and high schls., Walkerton, and Collegiate Inst., Collingwood; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1887; N.W.T., 1889; B. Columbia, 1897; served in the Nile expdn. 1884-5; elected to H. of C., Canada, 1900 and 1904; judge of ct. of appeal, B. Columbia, 1909.

GALLOWAY, WILLIAM CHARLES GOMBLETON.—B. 1892; Lond. Institution of City and Guilds bronze medalist, telephony, A.I.E.E.; Br. P.O., engng. dept., Jan., 1912; on active serv., R.E. (Signals), Nov., 1915; asst. teleg. engrnr., P. and T. dept., F.M.S., June, 1920; engrnr., Oct., 1926; ag. sr. engrnr., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., May-July, 1928, and Feb-May, 1929; sr. engrnr., Dec., 1930.

GALT, HON. ALEXANDER CASIMIR.—B. 1853; ed. Hellmuth Coll., Toronto Univ.; practised law in Toronto, 1876-1896; Rossland, B.C., 1896-1906; Winnipeg, 1906-1912; judge of the ct. of king's bench, Manitoba, 1912; contrib. to Can. Law Journal and Can. Law Times.

GALWAY, LT.-COL. SIR HERBERT LIONEL, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1889), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. comnrr. and vice-consul, Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. comnrr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and divn. comnrr. Niger Coast prot. (now South. Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. high comnrr., South. Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass expdn., 1895 (desps., medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin expdn., 1897 (desps., clasp, brevet majority); Aro expdn. as chief political officer, 1901-2 (desps., medal with clasp); gov., St. Helena, 1902; gov., Gambia, 30th Sept., 1911; gov., S. Australia, 27th Feb., 1914; assumed gov't., 18th Apr., 1914, retired, 1920.

GALWAY, JOHN PATRICK FRANCIS.—Ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Liverpool; elk., vety. pathological lab., Kabete, Aug., 1910; seconded, mily. serv., 1915; corp., F. African Vty. Corps, 1st June, 1916; lieut., ditto, 17th Oct., 1917; lieut., 3rd K.A.R.; "1914-15" Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory medals; labour inspection officer, E. Africa Protectorate, April, 1919; office superintendent, P.W.D., December, 1922.

GAMBAL, Lawrence Irving Washington.—B. 1876; clerk, general manager, Riley's, Trinidad, May, 1893; clerk, engineering surveyor, March, 1900; second clerk, crown lands office, July, 1907; chief clerk, crown lands office, February, 1914; warden, St. David, April, 1920; warden, St. Patrick, June, 1922.
GAMBLE, F. C.—Assistant district commissioner, E. Africa Protectorate, February, 1915; reserve magistrate, July, 1923.

GAMBLE, Joseph Samuel.—Acting sub-inspector of police, B. Guiana, 1905; wounded in Georgetown riots, 1905; sub-inspector of police, 1906; Hythe musketry certificate, 1908; district inspector of police, 1912; inspector-general, police, Fiji, and commandant, Fiji defence force, October, 1927.

GAME, Frederick Harold Roberts, A.C.G.I. (1921), A.M.I.C.E. (1925).—B. 1900; educated Taunton School and Imperial College of Science and Technology, South Kensington; assistant engineer, P.W.D., Uganda, September, 1925.

GAME, Air Vice-Marshal Sir Philip Woolcott, G.B.E. (1929), K.C.B. (1924), C.B. (1919), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1876; educated Charterhouse; entered Royal Artillery, 1895; commissioned, 1901; adjutant, 1902; major, 1912; air vice-marshal, 1922; G.S.O., third and second grade, W.O., 1910-14; served in South Africa, 1901-02 (despatches, Queen's Medal, five clasps); European war, 1914-18; despatches, six times; director, training and organization, Air Ministry, 1919-22; commanded R.A.F., India, 1922-23; air member for personnel on air council, 1923-28; retired list, 1929; governor, New South Wales, 1930.

GAMMANS, Leonard David.—B. 1895; second lieutenant, Royal Field Artillery, 1915; B.E.F., France, 1916; lieutenant and captain, 1917; seconded for service as instructor in gunnery and equitation, R. Milly, Coll., Canada, May, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., November, 1920; passed cadet, January, 1922; assistant assistant district officer, K. Kubu, April, 1922; assistant supervisor, customs, P. Dickson, October, 1922; ditto, P. Buntar, March, 1923; served at Malayan pavilion, British Empire Exhibition, May, 1925; offr., class IV, June, 1927; attested, co-operative societies department, F.M.S., October, 1927; offr., class III, June, 1932.

GANADO, Roberto F., LL.D.—B. 1875; educated Malta Lyceum; matriculated, Malta University, 1891; LL.D., Malta, 1898; bar, Malta, 1899; advocate for poor, 1905; magistrate, judicial police, 1918; examiner, laws, Malta University, 1919; chairman, licensing board, 1920; visitor, notarial acts, November, 1921; advocate, crown advocate, June, 1922; advocate, public prosecutor and treasurer, council, July to October, 1922; chairman, board of prison visitors, 1924; advocate, supreme court, June to September, 1924; member, general council of University of Malta, 1924; judge, supreme court, September, 1925.

GARDINER, Frederick George.—B. 1874; educated Diocesan College, Rondebosch, and Keble College, Oxford; B.A., Cape Town and Oxford; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1896; admitted to Cape Town bar, 1897; advocate, puisne judge, Natal, 1907; chairman, Cape Province, 1910; puisne judge of supreme court of South Africa (Cape Province Division), 16th October, 1914; judge president, Cape Province division, 1926.

GARDNER, Albert Edward, M.M.—Enlisted, April, 1900; South Africa, 1901-02; India, 1902-08; European War, 1914-19; C.S.M., and R.Q.M.S.; Somaliland Camel Corps, October, 1919-June, 1929; mentioned in despatches, Somaliland operations, 1920; offr., Somaliland police, July, 1929.
GARDNER, H. M.—Assistant conservator of forests, F.A.P., May, 1914; senior assistant conservator, forests, Kenya, December, 1923; assistant conservator, 1925 and 1927; conservator, 1928.

GARDNER, John Hyslop, B.Sc.—B. 1883; educated Kelvinside Academy and University, Glasgow; assistant engineer, Basutoland, 1909; government engineer, Swaziland, 1919; served with R.E., Flanders, 1916-18.

GARDNER, Joseph Henry.—B. 1882; active service with Imperial Yeomanry, South African War; military service, East African Expeditionary Force attached to R.A.F., Corps, 1916-18; assistant locomotive superintendent, Tanganyika Railways, March, 1918.

GARLICK, George Herbert, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (London), D.M.R.E. (Cambridge).—B. 1886; medical officer, Johore, 1917; Malayan medical service, 1923; assistant principal medical officer, Johore, 1924; physician and radiologist, Johore, 1927.

GARNER, Joseph John Saville.—B. 1908; educated Highgate School, senior foundation school, 1923-26; Jesus College, Cambridge (modelling languages school, 1926-29); modern languages trip, Part I, classes 2 division 1; ditto, Part II, classes 1; B.A., 1930; assistant modern language master, Haileybury College, 1929-30; appointed after competitive examination, assistant principal, D.O., October, 1930.

GARNETT, W. J., B.Sc. (London).—B. 1889; appointed, after competitive examination, clerk of the second division of the civil service, and assigned to the Colonial Office, January, 1907; seconded for service in Overseas Settlement Office, January, 1919; promoted assistant principal under clause 51, 1924; assistant to the rep. in the Commonwealth of Australia of H.M.'s government in the United Kingdom, 1931.

GARRAN, Sir Robert Randolph, K.C.M.G. (1920), Kt., B.A. (1917), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1867; barrister-at-law; secretary to drafting committee of Australian Federal Convention, 1897-8; secretary to attorney-general's department, Commonwealth of Australia, 1901; parliamentary draftsman; solicitor-general, 1916-32; chairman, Canberra University; author of "The Coming Commonwealth," 1897, and joint author (with the Hon. Sir John Quick) of "The Annotated Constitution of the Australian Commonwealth," 1901.

GARRAWAY, Thomas William Savile.—Educated Denstone College, Staffordshire; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1906; admitted to Grenada bar, 1907; St. Vincent bar, 1909; magistrates' court, St. Vincent, July, 1916; assistant attorney-general on two occasions; chairman of police and superintendence of prisons, October to December, 1918; chairman, education commission, 1919; magistrate, West district, Grenada, September, 1920; magistrate, East district, June, 1922; assistant attorney-general, November, 1926 to October, 1927; magistrate, South district, October, 1927; assistant justice, on three occasions; chairman, just and magistrates' court, St. Lucia, October, 1930.

GARROOD, Harry St. Clair.—B. 1882; educated St. Polycarp's College, Farnham; articled architect; Sudan civil servant, 1906; second lieutenant, Royal Engineers, 1917; captain, 1918; served with Hedjaz Expeditionary Force, Br. agent, Jeddah, 1919 with rank of major; M.C., third class Order of Nishan from King Hussein; mentioned in despatches, demobilized, 1920; general headquarters, Egypt to 1928; crown surveyor and engineer, Gibraltar, 1929.

GARTON, Harry.—B. 1889; served in R.F.A., France, Salonica, Egypt and Palestine, 4th August, 1914 to April, 1919; clerk to court martial officers, R.F.A., May to September, 1919; Admiralty, 21st October, 1919 to October, 1922; served on Naval Inter-Allied Commission of Control in Germany, May, 1921 to October, 1922; clerk to officers, C.O., 21st October, 1922.

GARVEY, Francis Willoocks.—B. 1882; served in South Africa, first in the ranks and afterwards as transport officer; served Transvaal town police, 1904-08; assistant commissioner, police, S. Nigeria, 1909; commissioner, police, 1918; received thanks of government. for investigation and suppression of human sacrifices to Agbara Juju at Awka, 1921; comant., pol., Lagos, 1922; ag. dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Feb., 1925 to Mar., 1925; dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Mar., 1925.

GARVEY, RONALD HERBERT, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Trent Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji and Western Pacific services, Nov., 1926; ast. dist. offr., Mala, Apr., 1929; dist. offr., Oct., 1930; ast. sec., W. Pacific high comm., Aug., 1932.

GARVIN, THOMAS FORREST, K.C.—B. 1881; Crown counsel, Ceylon, Mar., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1913; ag. addtl. dist. judge, Colombo, Feb., 1914; solr.-gen., 1916; ag. puana just., Nov., 1921 to Jan., 1922; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1922 to Dec., 1922; ag. puana just., sup. ct., Jan., 1923; puana just., June, 1924; ag. ch. just., July to Sept., 1926.
GASH, VINCENT GORDON.—Served in Bechuansland Prot. pol., 1911-15; sub-inspr., 1915; passed 3rd grade Seawana exam., 1923; inspr., 1929.

GATER, BOSSERT ALAN REX, M.A., Dip. Agric. (Cantab.), D.I.C., F.R.M.S., F.E.S.—B. 1896; war serv., 1914-19; ast. entomologist, dept. of agric., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; malaria reseach offr., Nov., 1926; entomologist, Inst. Med. Resch., 1927; prof., biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Aug., 1930.

GATFIELD, WILLIAM HUKET, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Streatham Gram. Schl. and St. George's Coll., London; appd. to the 2nd divn. of the Imp. civ. serv., 1914; joined Queen's Westminster Rifles, 1915; 2nd lieut., 1916; served in France, and demob. with rank of lieut., 1919; cadet, S. Stltmtn., Nov., 1920; proceeded to China to study Chinese, July, 1921; ag. ast. prot., Chinese affrs., S'pore, Aug., 1920; do., S.S., Jan., 1931.
GAUNT, A. E.—B. 1897; entd. C.O., Aug., 1913; appd. ast. clk., Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from Dec., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 28th Dec., 1928.

GAUNTLETT, HUGH GAUNTLETT.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1919; ent. civ. serv., Jamaica by compet. exam. and apptd. ast. clk. of cts., Mar., 1894; dep. ditto, Apr., 1903; ag. clk. of cts., on various occasions between June, 1907 and June, 1912; clk. of cts., Feb., 1915; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, Aug., 1926 and from Sept., 1926; res. mag., 1929.

GEERING, SIDNEY.—Temp. ast. to irrig. engrnr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, 1st Aug., 1916 to Oct., 1918; ag. dist. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; 1st ols. overseer, July, 1919; clk. of works, Gambia, 20th Aug., 1920.

GELDENHUYS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; B.A., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Univs. of Cornell, Wisconsin, Illinois and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nature study and agrl. science, Grey Coll. Schl., 1918; edir., "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, S.A., 1919; first ch., divn. of agrl. economics and marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; under-sec. for agrl., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931.

GENEVE, FREDERIC PIERRE, K.O. (1929).—B. 1888; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, Nov., 1909; ag. dist. and stip. mag. for varying periods; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, July, 1921; addnl. substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1922; substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1923; ag. ditto, Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925 and Apr., 1926 to Jan., 1927; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1928, Mar.-Dec., 1929 and Apr.-Sept., 1930; ag.ch. just., Mar., 1931.

GENT, GERARD EDWARD JAMES, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1895; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1914-19; ast. prin., C.O., 1920; sec., rubber restriction comteee., 1922; act. prin., 1923; pvte. sec. to partly. under sec., 1924; prin., 1927; dep. sec., C.O. confce., 1927; sec., col. services comteee., 1929; seconded to India Office as a sec. of Indian Round Table Confce., 1930; accompd. Permt. Under-Sec. of State to Ceylon, Malaya and Hongkong, 1932.

GENTLE, GEORGE EDWIN PALMER, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1880; ed. Denstone Coll.; clk., gov.'s office, Grenada, 1898; warden, P.W.D., St. Vincent, 1901; road surv., Grenada, 1905; ag. supt., pub. wks., 1914, 1916, 1918, 1921, 1922; ast.supt., pub.wks., 1916; supt., pub.wks., 1922.
GEORGE, REGINALD R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1887; ed. H.M. Schl. Ship "Conway"; seconded for serv., Lake Nyasa, 1915; commodr., marine transport, Sept., 1918; ag. marine supt. in 1922, 1925, 1928 and 1931.
GEORGE, NANCY MARGARET FANNY.—B.1900; ed. St. Catherine's High Schl., Malta, Devonport Secondary Schl., Froebel Training Coll., Bedford; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928.

GERAHTY, CHARLES CYRIL, K.C. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Trent Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1909; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt. E. Lancs. Regt., 1910; lieut., 1911; capt., 1914; ast. res. and comntr., prov. cts., N. Nigeria, 1911-14; war serv. in France, Belgium and W. Africa, 1914-18; restored to civil establmt., N. Provs., Nigeria, Nov., 1918; legal ast., lands directorate, W.O., Sept., 1919 to Sept., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1920-26; ag. atty.-gen. and puana judge on various occasions; joint compiler 2nd vol. stat.law of Cyprus, 1923; atty.-gen., Dec., 1926; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1929; puana judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., Apr., 1932.

GERMAN, RALPH LIONEL.—B. 1892; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (class. sch.); B.A. 1914; served with Hampshire Regt., 1915-19; Tank Corps, 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1921; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Perak, Feb., 1922; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, Mar., 1924; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1924; dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1926; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1929; supt., marine and cust., chandu, posts and tels., audr. and pub. proc., Kelantan, Jan., 1931.
GERMAN, WALTER.—B. 1885; bldg. inspr., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1913; ag. ch. sany. inspr. in addn., Aug., 1919 to May, 1920.
GERRAND, JOHN KENNEDY.—B. 1899; ast. engrnr., Basutoland, 1929.

GETHIN, PERCY EDWARD LOVELL, A.F.C.—B. 1884; ed. Radley Coll.; R. Geographical Soc'y. dipl. in surveying, 1908; explorn. in S. Arabia, 1909; ast. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 1910; in charge, topographical surveys in the field, 1912; on milly. serv., 1914-20; major, R.F.C., 1916; ast. dir., surveys, Cyprus, 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, 1923; ag. reg-gen., 1923; dir., surveys, Tanganyika Territory, 1926; tech. adviser, air bd., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; dir., civ. aviation, 1930.

GETLEY, ALFRED CURPHEY.—B. 1897; ed. Roborough Schl., Eastbourne; acctnt. branch, Royal Navy, 1915-22; paymr. licut., Jan., 1920; ret. at own request, 1922; seconded as pilot to R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-20; ent. col. audit. dept., Apr., 1923; asst. audr., Uganda, June, 1923; ag. senr. asst. audr. on several occasions.
GIBB, JOHN.—B. 1881; supervisor of warehouses, govt. stores, Ceylon, Oct., 1914; col. storekeeper, Sept., 1916.

GIBBINGS, ALBERT VICTOR, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1913; Kedah, Apr., 1913; ag. exec. engrnr., Kedah, Jan., 1917; exec. engrnr., Kedah, Mar., 1922; exec. engrnr., Johore, Sept., 1925; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Apr. 1927; senr. exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1929; water wks. engrnr., Seremban, June, 1930; senr. exec. engrnr., Klang, Apr., 1931; ag. state engrnr., Pahang, June, 1931.
GIBBON, JOHN FRANK, M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—B. 1869; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1899; dist. med. offr., Jan., 1916.
GIBBS, GEORGE HOWARD.—B. 1889; aast. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920.
GIBSON, CLARENCE HYNES.—B. 1876; ag. dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Jan., 1914; confirmed in apptnt., Jan., 1916; a dep. comsrr. for the W. Pacific, with powers in Gilbert group, June, 1915.

GIBSON, H. D.—B. 1886; ed. Bedale and Lincoln Grm. Schls.; junr. traffic asst., Nigerian rly., 1910; aast. traffic supt., 1913; dist. ditto, 1920; traffic supt., 1922; divnl. supt., 1927; supt. of line, 1932.

GIBSON, LESLIE BERTRAM.—B. 1896; ed. King Henry VIII. Schl., Coventry; hon. LL.B. (War), London; flight sub-lieut., R.N.A.S., attd. Grand Fleet; lieut., R.A.F.; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; attd., atty-gen.'s office, Spore, Apr., 1920; attd., food control office, Penang, May, 1920; dep. dir., food prodn., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; dep. regisr. and asst. offl. assignee, Penang, June, 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; aast. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1925; asst. regisr., sup. ct., Ipoh, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Penang, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932.

GIBSON, ROY ALEXANDER.—B. 1885; ed. Brandon High Schl. and Brandon Coll.; ent. fed. govt. serv. at Regina, 1908; transf'd. to Ottawa, and apptd. ch. clk. to dep. min., 1908; aast. dep. min., June, 1919; comsrr., Yukon and N.W. Territories, 1923.

GIBSON, WILLIAM SUMNER, B.A., Oxon., Barrister-at-Law (Lincoln's Inn).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Keble Coll., Oxford (schol.), B.A. (1st cls. mod. hist.), 1899; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1899; services lent to Johore govt., July-Nov., 1908; aast. man., monopolies dept., Penang, Oct., 1909; called to the bar, June, 1913; legal adviser, Kedah, Jan., 1916; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1916; judge of high ct., Kedah (in addn.), July, 1916; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; pres., comm. to enquire into organisation and expend. of govt. depts., F.M.S., Feb., 1922; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1922; legal advr., F.M.S., December, 1922; M.F.C.

GIE, S. F. N., Ph.D.—B. 1885; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Amsterdam and Berlin Univ.; teacher and inspr. schls., Cape Prov.; prof. hist. and chmn., senate, Stellenbosch Univ., 1918; under-sec., educn., Union of S. Africa, 1926; sec., ditto, 1927; publications include a history of S. Africa in Afrikaans in two vols.

GILBERT, HUMPHREY RALEIGH.—B. 1899; ent. R. Navy, 1912; served, Great War 1914-18; ret., Jan., 1923; lieut. commdr. (retd.), Feb., 1928; admtn. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1927; aast. dist. offr., Jan., 1929.

GILBERT, JOSEPH TROUNSEY, O.B.E. (1933), M.B.E., B.A. (Oxon.), (1927).—B. 1888; ed. Bedford and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1914; aast. coll. (now aast. dist. comsrr.), Zanzibar, Jan., 1912; ag. mag., Mar. to June, 1915; 2nd aast. sec., Mar., 1915; priv. sec. to res., June, 1915 to Aug., 1921; ag. first aast. sec., June, 1915; ag. aast. chief sec. on various occasions, 1915-1927; offr. examr. in the Swahili language, 1919; Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. IV., 1919; cls. III., 1930; senr. aast. sec., Sept., 1924; aast. ch. sec., Jan., 1928; del., C.O. confcn., 1930; ag. ch. sec. for various periods between 1924 and 1932.

GILCHRIST, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1879; ed. at Brunswick House private schl., B. Guiana; entd. H.M. Customs, B. Guiana, Dec., 1899; copyist, regisr.'s office, Oct., 1900; 6th cls. clk., ditto, Mar., 1902; 5th cls. clk., cust., 1903; aast. commissary, Sept., 1905; clk. to atty.-gen., May, 1906; stip. mag., J.P., coroner, and comsrr. of oaths, 1910; chmn., Rose Hall village pattition of lands coman., 1911; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1914; ag. aast. govt. sec., Mar. to Sep., 1914; censor (war) under defence scheme, 1914-20; recd. thanks of S. of S.; practised at English bar, 1920-21; comsrr. to enquire into disturbance, H.M. penal sttlmt., B. Guiana, 1922; ag. junr. puisne judge, in 1923 and 1925-6; ag. atty-gen. in 1923, 1924 and 1926; ag. puisne judge in 1925, 1926 and 1927; puisne judge, Sept., 1927; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, 1931.

GILKS, J. L., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Medical offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; prin. med. offr., Kenya, Feb., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. councils; dir. med. and army services, 1926.
GILL, EDWIN.—B. 1896; on naval serv., 1915-19; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 12th July, 1926.

GILL, EDWIN LEONARD, D.Sc.—B. 1877; aast. Manchester Museum, June, 1900; curator, Hancock Museum, Newcastle-on-Tyne, July, 1901; aast. R. Scooth Museum, July, 1922; dir., S. African Museum, Cape Town, Jan., 1925.
GILL, HENRY SEWELL CURRIE, M.A.—B. 1903; cadet, provincial adminstr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1927; aast. dist. offr., Aug., 1929.
GILL, OSCAR SAYLE.—B. 1880; operating engrnr., eleo. dept., Ceylon, Nov., 1927; ch. engrnr. and man., eleo. undertakings, June, 1932.

GILL, RONALD GODDARD.—B. 1896; ed. Haberdashers' Askes Schl. (Hampstead); on mily. serv. overseas, 1917-19; commissioned, Nov., 1917; temp. clk., Miny. of Lab., 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., Mar., 1923.

GILLMAN, CLMENT, F.G.S., M.S.A.S.C.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1882; ed. Swiss Federal tech. high schl., Zurich; on rly. constrn. and surveys in E. Africa, 1905-14; served E.A.P. Force, 1916-19; capt., rly. corps; exec. engrnr., E. African mily. rly. (occupied territory), 1916-18; ch. engrnr., ditto, 1918-19; dist. engrnr., Tanganyika Rly., Oct., 1919; senr. dist. engrnr., Apr., 1925; ch. engrnr., Apr., 1928.

GIMSON, FRANKLIN CHARLES.—B. 1890; B.A. Oxon.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1914; aast. office of naval intell. offr., in add. to his own duties; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, in add. to his own duties; on mil. duty, 1918; addtnl. aast. col. sec., July, 1919; offic. aast. to govt. agt., N. C. Prov., Sept., 1919; addtnl. col. sec., Mar., 1920; 4th aast. ditto, Sept., 1920; ag. aast. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1922; landing... survr., cust., Feb., 1924; ag. dep. collr., cust., Mar. to May, 1928; addnl. asst. dir., educn., July, 1931; asst. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Feb., 1932.

GLEN, JAMES ALEXANDER, M.A.—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec. 1915; lieut., July, 1917; ag. capt., Nov., 1918; served in France and Palestine; demob., Jan., 1919; asst. dir. of edn., Iraq, Apr., 1920; ag. adviser, Min. of educn., Apr., 1922.

GLENDAWAY, VINTON G., O.B.E. (1929), M.A. Oxon. (hons. nat. sci.), F.G.S., Diploma, Forestry.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. comsnr., N. Frontier, Jan., 1914 to June, 1919; offr. in charge, Somali disarmament, N. Frontier dist., Mar., 1919 (A.G.S. Med.); seconded as dist. comsnr., Br. Somaliland, Mar., Sept., 1920; asst. dist. comsnr., Oct., 1922; ag. dist. comsnr., Sept., 1922; dist. comsnr., 1926; Abyssinia mission, 1927-28; ag. prov. comsnr., 1930-31.

GLEN DING NING, JAMES, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Queen's Univ., Belfast; asst. dir., surveys, Gold Coast, Oct., 1919; dep. dir., cadastral branch, Jan., 1920; dir., cadastral branch, Dec., 1920; dep. survr.-gen., Apr., 1924; ag. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1921-26; survr.-gen., Oct., 1926.

GODBOLT, S.B.—B. 1884; clk., treas., Cape 1901; Transvaal, 1905; Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treas., Union of S. Africa, 1920; ch. clk., grade II, 1924; ditto, grade I, 1928; comsnr., pensions, July, 1932.
GODDARD, HARRY FREDERICK.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.O., 4th May, 1920.

GODDARD, THOMAS NELSON, M.B.E. (1928).—B. 1889; ed. Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., Falkland Is., clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Apr., 1914; lieut., Falkland Is. vol. force, Aug., 1914; capt., 1917; O.C., 1919; postal censor, 1914; proper offr. of the crown, prize ot., 1914; chief clk., col. sec.'s office, Feb., 1915; ag. comsnr. of currency, 1918; ag. local audr., 1920; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1920; clk., exec. coun., 1921; A.D.C. to ag. gov., 1921 and to gov., 1922-23; census offr., 1921; ag. senr. asst. col. sec. for various periods between 1920 and 1930; ag. col. sec. for various periods 1926-31; ch. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1930; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1930 and 1931; compiler, Sierra Leone, Handbook 1924.

GODDEN, HENRY JAMES, M.B.E. (1929)—B. 1877; telegraphist, Basutoland, 1902; postm., 1907; clk. to asst. comsnr., 1917; ag. asst. comsnr., 1922; passed 1st grade Sesuto, 1922; ch. clk., mast. of ct. and regiar., 1924.

GODFREY, GEORGE CROSBIE.—B. 1880; ed. Waitematai Schl., Timaru, N.Z.; N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1897; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., 1901; asst. ch. clk., pub. wks. dept., 1908; ch. clk., 1914; asst. under-sec., 1920; sec. marine, 1923; sec. of lab. and comsnr. of unemployment, 1932.

GODFREY, SIR JOSEPH ED., KT. BACH. (1914).—B. 1868; M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regiar. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; acted on serv. occasions as surg.-gen., regiar.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., regiar.-gen. of b., d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, and comb. ct., July, 1904; dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., 1905; chmn. of poor law comsnrs., 1906; del. for B. Guiana and Bahamas to Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909.

GODLEY, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Alexander John, K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—B. 1867; lieut., Royal Dublin Fusiliers, 1886; adjutant, 1889; capt., 1895; major, 1897; colonel, 1905; apptd. comdt. New Zealand Forces, 1910, with local rank of major-general; comdg. New Zealand expeditionary force in Egypt and at Dardanelles, 1914-15; gazetted major-general, 1915; temp. lieut.-gen. in command of an Anzac army corps, France, 1916; lieut.-gen., 1918; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1928; foreign decorations: Legion of Honour, Croix de Commandeur (1917); Order of the Crown, Grand Officier (Belgian) (1917); Order of the White Eagle (Serbian), 1917; Croix de Guerre (Belgian), 1918.
