(appts.) to S. of S. for the Cols., Apr., 1928; temp. ag. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930.

JAMISON, ROBERT, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Ireland), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1880; ast. med. offr., Hatikulu, Swaziland, 1910-13; med. offr., Swaziland, 1913; served, S.W. Africa campaign, 1914-15; prin. med. offr., 1918

JANSEN, HON. ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., LL.B., M.L.A.—B. 1881; ed. Ladysmith Govt. Schl. and Durban High Schl.; leader of Nationalist party in Natal; mem., Natal Univ. Coll. Coun.; chmn., Saamwerk Unie van Natalse Vereeniginge (the first body in S.A. to prescribe exams. in Afrikaans) for 7 years; rep. Vryheid in parl. since 1921; foundation mem. of Suid Afrikaanse Akademie vir Taal, lettere en kuns; cldc. speaker, Union H. of A., July, 1924; min. of native affra., June, 1929.

JANSZ, HERBERT ERIC.—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; att. Kandy kach., Sept., 1914; att., Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Batticaloa, in add., Aug., 1915; ag. office ast. to govt. agt., Batticaloa, Sept., 1915; office ast. to ast. govt. agt., Kegalla, Jan., 1917; office ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1918; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1925; addnl. dist. judge, Avissawella, in addn., Oct., 1927; ast. stiml. offr., Sept., 1928; ag. pol. mag., Colombo, Apr. to June, 1930; ag. stiml. offr., Mar., 1931.
JANSZ, REGINALD BELTRAN.—B. 1878; cls. V ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1928; office ast. to ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1928.

JARDINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, C.M.G. (1932). O.B.E. (1888).—B. 1888; ed. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. classical trip., 1910; B.A., 1910; M.A., 1914; chief sec.'s off., Cyprus, 1910; pvt. sec. to ag. high comm., July to Oct., 1911; ast. sec. to govt., 1912; ag. ch. ast. sec. on several occasions, 1912-16; passed in modern Greek, 1912; sec. to the admtn., Somaliland, 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu (3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia), 1917; in charge H.Q. services, Somaliland Expedy. Force, 1920 (A.G.S. med. and ment. in desp.); senr. ast. ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., July to Nov., 1925 and June to Aug., 1926; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1927; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1927 and Apr. to Oct., 1928; ch. sec., Jan., 1929; ag. gov., Jan. to Mar., 1929 and from Feb., to June, 1931; gov's. dep. on several occasions since 1927; accredited rep. to Perm. Mandates Comsn., 1929, 1930 and 1931; joint-editor "Hand-book of Cyprus," 1913 and 1919, and author of "Mad Mullah of Somaliland," 1923.

JARDINE, ROBERT FEIR, C.M.G. (1928), O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1894; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; served European War in Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; polit. offr., Mesopotamia, 1917 ast. divnl. adviser, Mosul prov., under Iraq govt., 1921; admstve. inspr., Mosul prov., 1923; on sp. duty in Europe in connec. with Mosul question, 1925; admstve. inspr., Sulaimani, 1925; do, Mosul prov., 1925; ditto, Basra, 1928; author "Grammar of Bahdinan Kurmanji (Kurdish)," 1922.

JARRETT, JAMES HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Lancing Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; H.A.C., 1914; served European War, 1914-19; ast. dist. commr., Uganda, 1919; seconded, judl. dept. as mag., 1922; mag., 1924; ast. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. solr. gen., May, 1927 to Jan., 1928; crown coun., 1927; atty.-gen., Grenada, 1929; ag. admstr. and ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, from May, 1930.

JARRETT, NORMAN ROWSTONE.—B. 1889; ed. Highgate and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 3rd cls. class. moda., 1910, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1912; B.A., 1912; cadet, F.M.S., 1912; passed cadet, July, 1914; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. II, 1930; ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Perak, May, 1931.

JARVIS, CYRIL CHESTER.—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; Rhodes school., 1904; Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hon. juris.); LL.B., Cape Univ.; sec., ch. just., Cape Colony, 1907; admitted Cape bar, 1910; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.F.S.; prof. ast. to atty.-gen., Cape Prov., 1921; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1926.

JAYARATNE, MERENNA FRANCIS DE SILVA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; astd. Colomba kach., Mar., 1927; office ast., Matale kach., Jan., 1928; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1930.

JEFF, CAPT. JOHN.—B. 1896; cadet, F.M.S., astd Chinese prot., Ipoh, Apr., 1920; passed cadet, Dec., 1922; ast. prot., Chinese, Seremban, Dec., 1923; astd. cust. dept., Jan., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Apr., 1928; ditto, N. Sembilan, Jan., 1930; ditto, Selangor and Pahang, May, 1931; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1932.

JEFF, RICHARD HEDLEY ALLISON.—B. 1889; jr. ast. engr., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1913; dist. engr., grade III, head office, July, 1915; supernum. dist. engr., July, 1919; on sp. duty in S. India, Aug., 1919; dist. engr., grade II, K. Lumpur, Jan., 1920; dist. engr., grade I, Jan., 1924; ag. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1928-Aug., 1929; sr. dist. engr., head office, June, 1929; ditto, Johore Bahru, Feb., 1931.

JEFFERY, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1924), F.S.A.—Scholarship, R. Coll. of Art and Science, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.I.B.A., 1892; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem and the East, 1892; hon. corre. mem. of Imperial Institute of Archology of Russia, 1897; local sec. for Cyprus Society of Antiquaries; inspr. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, 1903; relinquished appt. July, 1919; reapttd. curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, Feb., 1921; author of "Summary of Architectural Monuments of Cyprus," "Cyprus under an English King," "Historic Monuments of Cyprus," and of several papers on the medieval archaeology of Cyprus and Jerusalem, edr., new edition (1929) of "An attempt at a Bibliography of Cyprus."

JEFFREY, PAT COMMDE. A., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1892; ed. Glas. Univ.; R.N., 1914-19; probationer, N. Rhodesia, 1919; J.P., 1920; clk. of ct. and under-sheriff, Bataka dist., 1920; ast. nat. commr., 1921; nat. commr., 1926; passed higher Chinyanga; ast. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory 1927; ast. secy., Jan., 1930.

JEFFREYS, ROBERT SYDNEY.—B. 1888; ed. Cape Town (S. Africa), Bedford Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., LL.B. (1909); Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn, 1910); joined adminstr. of N. Rhodesia, June, 1914; asst. native comsnr., and J.P., July, 1916; resigned to proceed on active serv., Feb., 1918; rejoined N. Rhodesia adminstr., May, 1919; native comsnr., July 1921; seconded to secretariat, Nov., 1921-Dec., 1922; asst. mag., Apr., 1926; d't. offr. (grade II) with powers of mag., Apr., 1928; seconded as asst. to sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1927-Oct., 1928; ag. crown coun., Nov., 1928-Mar., 1929; ag. pol. max., May, 1930.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES J., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1896; ed. Malvern Coll.; class. demy., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1914; 2nd lieut., 3rd Wiltshire Regt., Sept., 1915; served in France, 1916; lieut., 1917; invalided from army, Nov., 1917; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 27th Dec., 1917; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., 16th July, 1919; sec., E. Africa land stltm. selection board, 1919; asst. sec., E. Africa currency board; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; hon. sec., Corona Club, since 1921; sec., comte. on governors' pensions, 1927-28; ag. asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1930.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES WILLIAM, F.R.A.S.—B. 1882; Computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1897; junr. ast., Royal Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1902; 1st ast., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1907; chief ast., ditto, 1912; ag. dir. in 1915, 1920, 1924, 1925 and 1929; dir., 1932.

JEKYLL, LIEUT.-COL. STEPHEN HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1901), R.E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1846; lieut., R.E., Apr., 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870, and destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mining, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph dept., from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the G. Coast; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. defence comte., 1878; sec. to the roy. comen. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to visit and report on the defences of Singapore and Colombo, 1883-4; sec. col. defence comte., 1886; priv. sec. to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comen. for Melbourne centennial exhibn., 1888; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to the roy. comen. for the Paris exhibn., 1900.

JELF, SIR ARTHUR SELBORNE, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.I.; B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exh.); 2nd cls. class. moda., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; held various admstive and exec. posts in Malay States, 1900-10; ast. gen. adviser to Johore govt., Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., Jan., 1912; chmn., san. bd., Kinta, Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lieut. general list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., Dec., 1917; tempy. capt., gen. staff, S. Stltm. command; attchd. gen. staff, W.O. (milit. intell.), Jan., 1918; H.M. Petroleum Exec. (sec. to Lord Harcourt's comte.), June, 1919; offr., cls. II., June, 1919; ag. underscr. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1920; dir., Malayan polit. Intell. bureau, cls. I b., Dec., 1921; mom., Itr. Malaya opium comte., Dec., 1923; pres., estates health coman., Apr., 1924 (reed. thanks of S. of S.); col. sec., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ag. gov., for various periods 1925-32; lieut.-col., local forces, Oct., 1927.

JELLICOE, 1ST VISCOUNT OF SCAPA (GREAT. 1918); SIR JOHN RUSHWORTH JELLIFFE, O.M. (1916), G.C.B. (1915), G.C.V.O. (1916).—B. 1869; entd. navy, 1872; served in Egyptian war, 1882; China expeditionary force, 1900; European war, 1914-16; battle of Jutland; naval ast. to controller, 1901-3; dir. of naval ordnance, 1906-7; A.D.C. to King Edward VII., 1906-7; 3rd sea lord of the Admiralty and controller of the navy, 1908-10; comdr.-in-chief of the Atlantic fleet, 1910-11, and of the Grand Fleet, 1914-16; 2nd sea lord of the Admiralty, 1912-14; 1st sea lord, 1916-17; admiral of the fleet, 1919; governor-general of New Zealand, 1920-24; G.C. Legion of Honour, and Croix de Guerre (France); G.C. Order of Leopold, and Croix de Guerre (Belgium); St. George 3 (Russia); G.C.R.S. with Pavlovna (Japan); G.C. Savoia (Italy).

JENKINS, EDWARD ENOCH, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law (Grays Inn).—B.1896; war service, France, 1914-19; cadet, Nyasaland, Nov., 1925; ast. regis., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1927; crown coun., Apr., 1930; ag. solr.gen. on various occasions, 1931-32; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1931-32.

JENKINS, REGINALD FREDERICK.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 30th Nov., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; seconded to Empire Marketing bd., 1926; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Feb., 1928.

JENKS, J. C. A.—Ed. Bristol Gram. Schl. and Angers, France; awarded Croix-de-Guerre (Palm) 1917; Order of Saint Stanislaus with Swords (2nd cls.), and ment. in despa., 1919; colonial audit serv., 1920; ast. audr., Kenya Colony, Apr., 1920; ast. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; senr. ast. audr. in charge, Jan., 1926; mem., retrenchment and rev. comte., Zanzibar, 1927; Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar (5th cls.), 1928; senr. ast. audr. in charge, Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbrs., 1929.

JENNER, FRANK.—B. 1878; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, Bechuanaland campaign, 1896-97; S. African war, 1899-1901; sub-inpr., Basuto-land Mounted Pol., 1901; inspr., 1910; supt., leper stltm., 1914; comsr., 1920.
JENNINGS, F. G.—Ast. dist. commsr., E.A.P., Apr., 1919.
JENNINGS, PAROVAL HENRY.—B. 1903; ed. Christ's Hosp.; col. audt.dept., 1920; ast. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1927; ast. audr., Mauritius, 1931; ag. audr., 1932.

JEPSON, FRANK PRIOR.—B. 1885; M.A., Pembroke Coll., Camb.; exhib., medallist, and dip. in agri. (hons. in entomology), Wye Agric. Coll., Kent, 1906; trop. disease research fund student in med. entomology, Camb. Univ., 1907; assisted in investig. by L.G.B. on flies as carriers of infection, 1908 and 1909; govt. entomologist, Fiji, 1909; offl. visits to Hawaii, 1911, Samoa, 1912, Java, 1913; ast. entomologist, Ceylon, 1919; ag. entomologist in addn., July, 1923 to Mar., 1924 and Oct. to Nov., 1925; del. to Imp. Agrl. Research Conf., Lond., 1927; on sp. serv., U.S.A. (termite invest.), 1928.
JERRAM, JAMES HAYLOCK.—B. 1879; ed. Thames High Schl. and Auckland Gram. Schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. state fire insurance office, 1905; ast. 1906; dep. gen. man., 1908; gen. man., 1923.

JEROVSE, RICHARD SOMERVILLE.—B. 1887; ed. Bradford Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (hist. demy.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; ast. dist. offr., Jan., 1920; dist. offr., July, 1920; chmn., sany. bd., Larut, Jan. and Oct., 1924; 1st mag. and regtr., sup. ct., Johore Bharu, Oct., 1924; ch. ag. ast. treas., F.M.S., and state treas., Selangor, Sept., 1928; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; mag., Ipoh, Nov., 1929; collr., estate duty, F.M.S. in admn., Apr., 1931.
JEWELL, MONTON, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899; conf. clk., 1911; deptl. ast., 1st Apr., 1922.

JEWELL, NORMAN PARSONS, O.B.E. (1929)—M.C. (1917); M.D.; B.Ch.; B.A.O., Trin. Coll., Dub.; L.M., Rotunda, Dub.; B.A. (Moderator), Trin. Coll., Dub.; late anesthetist, Dun's Hosp., Dub.; ast. med. offr., Seychelles, 1910; J.P. and chmn. local bds. of health, Praslin and La Digue; med. supt., leper asylum; med. offr., E.A.P., 1915; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Feb., 1921; ag. surg. specialist, European hosp., Nairobi, 1925.

JOBLING, GEOFFREY LIONEL.—B. 1899; ed. Bowen Sch., Brisbane; called to bar, Jan., 1926 (Gray's Inn); served German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; R.A.F., 1918-19; law dept., B.S.A. Co., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1915; ast. regir., high ct., N. Rhodesia; ast. offr. recr. in bankcy., ast. admin., gen., ast. regir., lands and deeds, ast. regir., births, deaths and marriages, ast. regir., companies, ast. custodian enemy property, ast. contr. local clearing office, ast. regir., high ct., etc., Uganda, June, 1927; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1929.
JOHNS, BRIAN MAURICE, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), F.R.C.S. (Ed.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1901; med. offr., S.S., Mar., 1928; prof. clin. surgery., King Edward VII Coll. of Med., S'pore, Aug., 1928.

JOHNS, FRID, F.J.I.—B. 1888; ed. Cornwall, England; on Adelaide newspaper literary staff from 1885 to 1914; leader of the first off. Hansard staff, S. Australian parlmt. since July, 1914; author of "John's Notable Australians" (1906 and 1908), and of "Australasia's Prominent People," "Who's Who in Australia" (seven editions), and "A Journalist's Jottings"; hon. sec. of Adelaide branch of Royal Society of St. George, and hon. sec., Matthew Flinders' National Statue, City of Adelaide; also hon. sec., Princess Mary wedding gift fund, S. Australia.

JOHNS, ROBERT.—B. 1903; ed. Monmouthshire Agr. Inst., Usk, and Univ. Coll. of Wales, Aberystwyth; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad; N.D.A., C.D.A., C.D.D., A.I.C.T.A.; stock inspr., Tanganyika, 1926; C.O. agril. research scholar, Trinidad, and Univ. of Reading, 1929-30; agril. offr., Zanzibar, 1931.

JOHNSON, CHARLES WALTER TREVOR.—B. 1893; Fiji serv., 1910; col. sec's office, 1915; 1st cls. clk. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., 1920; ag. pvt. sec. to ag. gov. on three occasions; seconded for serv. in W. Pacific high comm. as dist. offr., Fanning Island, Feb.-Nov., 1922; ag. ast. col. sec., 1924; ch. clk., secretariat, 1927; ag. prov. and dist. commnr., June-Sept., 1928; ag. ast. col. sec., Nov., 1929; 2nd ast. col. sec., 1931.
JOHNSON, D. O.—B. 1873; entd. Bahamas civ. ser., 1904; Out Island commnr. (3rd div.), 1909; 2nd div., 1910; 1st div., 1914; senr. commnr., 1928.
JOHNSON, E. T.—Res. mag., E. Africa Prot., 1915; senr. res. mag., Kenya, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, 1924 and 1925; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1929; ag. ch. just., 1932.
JOHNSON, ELLEN M., M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1873; ast. clk., post office, Sept., 1914; ch. clk., Jan., 1924; ag. postmr. in 1926 and 1930; ast. postmr., June, 1926; ag. postmr., Aug. to Sept., 1932.

JOHNSON, FREDERICK ENGLAND, I.S.O. (1932), A.F.I.A. (Australia).—B. 1878; served in S. African war, 1899-1901 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); inspr. of native labourers, British residency, New Hebrides, 1908; in charge of accts. of British residency, 1909; ag. comdt. of British div. of native constab., 1908, 1910, 1912, and from 1914, in addition to other duties; is also a dep. commr. of the high commr.'s ct. for the W. Pacific, and ag. regir. of high commr.'s ct., in the New Hebrides; treas. and collr. of cust., Br. Solomon Is., Nov., 1919; offl. mem., advisory coun., 1927.

JOHNSON, CAPT. GEORGE BENJAMIN, M.R.S.T.—B. 1884; ed. Ryder's Sch., Devonport and Cheltenham Training Coll. (dipl. educn.); Leicestershire Regt. and gen. staff, 1915-19; res. of offrs., T.A.; ag. vice-consul, Le Havre, 1920; headmnr., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1920; ditto and inspr., schls., 1925; ag. dir., educn. in 1920-23, 1925-26, 1928 and 1930; ch. inspr. schls., 1928.

JOHNSON, GEORGE CUNYNGHAM.—B. 1875; 2nd clk., treasy., St. Kitts, Apr., 1894; 2nd clk. admstr.'s office and clk. of legs. coun., Mar., 1896; clk. to admstr. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1901; treas. and comptlr., cuit., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; ag. admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; offl. mem., exec. and legis. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis and offl. mem., Fed. exec. coun.; censor, St. Kitts during the war; is a J.P. for St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is. and admstr. govt., Antigua, June, 1926; ag. commr., Montserrat, Feb., 1927.
JOHNSON, JOHN, M.I.M. AND C.E.—Ast. engr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, 1913; attd., R.W.A.F.F., Cameroons Exped., 1914-15; senr. exec. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927.

JOHNSON, JOHN COLEMAN DE GRAFT.—B. 1884; ed. Wesleyan Coll. Sch., Cape Coast; 5th grade clk., Ashanti, 1907; 4th grade clk., police dept., 1912; ast. inspr. W. and M., 1914; ast. sec. for native affrs., 23rd Mar., 1920; mem., Accra town coun., Jan., 1928.
JOHNSON, HON. SIR WILLIAM ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1862; speaker of the H. of R. C. of A., 1913-14 and 1917-23; ret. from politics.

JOHNSON, WILLIAM JOSEPH, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1892; serv. in I.E.F., E. Africa, 1914-17; ment. in depts., 1917; transf'd. to depot, 111th Mahars, India, 1917; offr. in charge, clearing house, office of field contr. of mily. accts., E.E.F., Cairo, 1918; major, ch. accts. offr., O.E.T. admstr., Palestine, 1919; treas., Palestine civ. admstr., July, 1920; dep. treas. on abolition of post of finan. sec., May, 1922; ag. treas. and mem., exec. and advisory couns. for various periods, 1923-28; mem., comtee. on pub. security forces, 1923; mem., comtee. on establishment of permanent and pensionable cadre for gen. clerical serv., 1925; mem., road bd., 1926; mem., rly. bd., 1928; currency offr., Jan.-June, 1928; treas., 1932.
JOHNSTON, H. LINDSAY.—Govt. statist, S. Australia, 1916.

JOHNSTON, JOHN ROOKE.—B. 1894; ed. St. Ninians, Moffat and St. Bees Schls. and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, 23rd Dec., 1917; seconded to 2/1st K.A.R., 23rd Dec., 1920; adminis. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Sept., 1923.

JOHNSTON, Sir Reginald Fleming, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.M.G. (1928), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; M.A., Magdalen Coll., Oxford (1901), Gray prizeman, Edin. Univ., 1894; prox. sec. Lord Rector's essay, Edin.; hon. in English lit., mod. hist. and constitutional law, Edin.; exhib., Magd. Coll., Oxford, 1894; ment., hon. causa Stanhope Essay, 1898; B.A., 1898; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; ag. clk. of couns., 1899; pvt. sec. to gov., 1900-01; ag. ast. col. sec., 1900-04; J.P., June, 1902; sec. to govt., Weihsaiwei, 1904; dist. offr. and mag., ditto, 1906; seconded for serv. as tutor to Manchu Emperor (ex-Emperor of China), 1919-26; recd. from Emperor button of highest offl. grade and sable robe, 1922; warden, Summer Palace, Peking, 1924; brought Emperor into safety in legation quarter, Peking, Nov., 1924; sec., Br. China indemnity delegation, 1926; comsnr., Weihsaiwei, 1927-30.

JOHNSTON, William.—B. 1890; ast. clk., G.P.O., May, 1910; offr., cust. and excise, Aug., 1911; dep. collr., cust., Mauritius, Dec., 1920; sec., harbr. bd., 1922-25; chmn., ditto, 1923-24; sec., tariff advisory bd., 1927-31; chmn., ditto on various occasions, 1927-30; comsnr. of excise for tobacco in addn., 1927-31 and ag. supt., inland rev., May to July, 1929; ag. collr., cust. and harbrm., M.L.C., on various occasions, 1923-30; collr.-gen., Jamaica, Jan., 1932.

JOHNSTONE, Angus Colin Duncan.—B. 1889; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and in Germany; passed Army entrance exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1906; higher grade staff, G. Cent. Rly., 1909-12; served, Balkan War, with Br. Red Cross Unit attd. Turkish Forces, 1912-13; lieut., 5th (Res.) Batt. West Yorks Regt. (T.), Nov., 1914 to Apr., 1915; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 10th Sept., 1913; lieut., W.A.F.F., and astid. Gold Coast Regt., Apr., 1915; seconded Br. Forces, Togoland, May, 1916; offr., cls. II, polit. serv., Jan., 1920; dist. comsnr., Ashanti Akim, Sept., 1922; prov. comsnr., Apr., 1928; comsnr., S. Prov., Aug., 1929; ag. ch. comsnr., N. Territories in addn., Dec., 1929 to Jan., 1930.

JOHNSTONE, Banner Carruthers, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1882; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Cantab.); passed R.G.S. survey course, 1908; ast. supt., surveys, Ceylon, Oct., 1909; ast. collr. (afterwards ast. dist. comsnr.), Zanzibar Prot., Apr., 1913; ag. 1st ast. sec. and clk. of coun., Nov., 1914 to June, 1915; ch. land sttlmt. offr., Jan., 1916; seconded for active serv. in German E. Africa, Aug., 1916; on mil. serv., France, Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Pemba, July, 1919 to Apr., 1920; dist. comsnr., Pemba, Nov., 1921; ag. senr. comsnr., Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925; 3rd cls. Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1920; ag. senr. comsnr. from Oct., 1926 to Apr., 1927; mem., leg. coun.; prov. comsnr., 1929.
JOHNSTONE, James Montague Cholmeley.—Ent. prison dept. (England and Wales), Aug., 1921; dep. ast. supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1924; ast. supt., 1927.

JOHNSTONE, John Wilkie.—B. 1884; solr., England, 1906; ast. polit. offr., G.E.A. (Tanganika Territory), Nov., 1918; ag. mag., Apr., 1919 to Feb., 1921; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; res. mag., Apr., 1929.

JOHNSTONE, Sir Robert Stewart, Kt. Bach. (1915).—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in history and political science), 1882; LL.B., 1884; M.A., 1896; Holt scholar, Gray's Inn, 1886; called to the Irish bar 1886; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut. 3rd (Militia) Batt. Manchester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary comsnr., 1890; stip. and circuit mag. and judge of ct. of com. pleas., Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1902; cl. just., Grenada, 1909; ret., 1914.

JOMARON, Adolphe Charles.—B. 1893; ed. at University Coll. Schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (class. schol.)—B.A. 1920; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914; lieut., 1915; capt., 1917; ag. major, 1918; wounded, 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; attd. to ast. adviser's office, Muar, Johore; 2nd ast. adviser, Muar, Johore, Jan., 1922; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., cls. V., June, 1922; ast. state audr., chmn., conservancy bd., Khota Bharu and coroner, June, 1924; offr., cls. IV., June, 1925; 3rd ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; sec. to rivers comsnrs. in addn., Dec., 1927; ag. pub. trustee and offl. admstr., F.M.S., May, 1931.
JONES, ROBERT NOBLE, C.B.E. (1928).—B. 1864; barrister and solr., N.Z., 1890; judge, native land ct. and dist. land regtr., 1903; chief judge, native land ct., 1919; under sec., native dept., 1922.

JONES, SAMUEL BENJAMIN, M.B.E. (1924), M.A. (Durh.), B.A. (Lond.), Inter. LL.B. Examin. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glasg.)—B. 1874; ed. Antigua Gram. Sch.; med. offir., and mag., Anguilla, 1918; thanked by exec. coun. of St. Kitts for checking outbreak of small pox in Anguilla, 1921; med. offir., dist. No. 2, St. Kitts, 1924.

JONES, STANLEY BOBCOWEN.—Ch. acot.'s office, Beira and Mashonaland and Rhodesia Rlys., Bulawayo, 1912-18; 2nd Rhodesia Regt., 1914-17; asst. nativ. commnr., and nativ. commnr., N. Rhodesia, 1918-28; asst. dist. offir., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1928; dist. offir., Oct., 1929.

JONES, STANLEY WILSON.—B. 1888; B.A. (Manchester), 1st cls., hon. hist. (1908); cadet F.M.S., Dec., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offir., Pekan, June, 1912; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; offir., cls. V, Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offir., Pekan, Aug., 1917; do., K. Kangar, June, 1918; offir., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; dist. offir., Jelebu; Jan. 1920; do., K. Lipis, May, 1920; local examr. in law and Malay, Nov., 1923; offir., cls. III, Dec., 1924; attld. to fed. sec. on sp. duty, Aug., 1924; local examr. in Malay, Selangor, Sept., 1924; offir., cls. II, Dec., 1928; ag. legal advr., Johore, Feb., 1931.

JONES, WILLIAM JOHN ANDREW, B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1889; ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Mar., 1913; dist. commnr., Dec., 1918; dep. prov. commnr., Jan., 1924; prov. commnr., July, 1927; sec. nativ. affrs., 1930; ag. col. sec., 1930, 1931 and 1932; gov't. dep., Sept., 1932.

JONES BATEMAN, REGINALD.—B. 1894; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1919; attached to Kurunegala Kach., Dec., 1919; office asst. to supt. of census and dir. of statistic., Oct., 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1924; asst. gov't. agt., Matallivu, Nov., 1924; asst. g. vt. agt., Kandy, Dec., 1926; asst. attnt. offir., Aug., 1927.

JORDAN, ARTHUR BENJAMIN.—B. 1890; ed. Nottingham High Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford (class. exhib.)—B.A., Oxon., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed final exam. in Can onese, Apr., 1916; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offir., cls. V, offirg. as asst. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1916; asst. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1918; lieut., M.V.I., Dec., 1918; asst. dist. offir., Raub (Rentong), Feb., 1919; offir., cls. IV, Nov., 1919; asst. prot. of Chinese, N. Sembilan, Apr., 1921; offir., cls. III, Nov., 1925; prot., Ch. nese, Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1926; prot., Chinese, Se'angor and Pehang, Dec., 1930; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., F.M.S., May, 1931; ditto, S.S., in addn., June, 1931.
JORDAN, A. H.—B. 1903; ent. C.O., 17th Mar., 1919; cler. offir., 14th Oct., 1921.

JOUBERT, M. J. A., B.S.A. (Toronto).—B. 1882; pupil teacher, O.F.S., 1897; asst. to gov't. agronomist, O.F.S., 1905; i-natant instr., agr., 1910; offir.-in-ch. and prin., Glen schl. of agr., 1913; prin., schl. of agr., Glen, 1917; prin., Grootfontein schl. of agr. (Cape), 1927; asst. ch. dir., plant industry, dept., agr., Pretoria, 1929; ch., divn. of agr. educn. and extension, Pretoria, 1932.

JOY, GEORGE ANDREW.—B. 1896; ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Bruges; ent. Arm., 1914; served in Belgium and France, 1915 and 1916; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; staff, gen. headqu., Egypt and Palestine, 1919-20; S.W. Arabia, 1921 to 1923; ment. in desps., lieut., R.A.R.O., Feb., 1919; asst. to res. commnr., New Hebrides, Aug., 1924; dep. commnr., W. Pacific, July, 1926; ag. Br. res. commnr., Aug., 1927; res. commnr., New Hebrides, 1928; temp. attld., staff of col. development advisory comm., C.O., Jan., 1931.
JOYCE, G. F. W. C.—B. 1897; ent. E.I.O., June, 1912; asst. olk., Jan., 1915; on mil'y. serv., Sept., 1914 to Apr., 1919; cler. offir., O.S.O., 1st Jan., 1920.

JOYCE, BREVET LIEUT.-COL. PIERCE CHARLES, C.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Beaumont Coll., Old Windsor; joined 1st Batt. The Connaught Rangers, 1900; serv. S. African War, 1900-02 (severely wounded, Queen's Med. with 3 clasps, King's Med. with 2 clasps); attld., Egyptian Army, 1907-16 (Egyptian Med. with clasp, 4th cls. Order of the Medjidieh, 4th cls. Order of the Nile); European War, 1915 (Gallipoli); Arab campaign against the Turks, 1916-18; mily. advr. to Amir Feisal, 1917 (ment. in desps. five times, brevet of lieut.-col., C.B.E., D.S.O., Croix d'Officier, Legion of Honour, Arabian Order of the Nahliah, 2nd cls.); gov't., E. and S. Desert Prov., Egypt, 1920; mil. adviser to Iraq Gov't., Mar., 1921.

JOYNT, HENRY RAYMOND, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Bradford, and Balliol Coll., Oxon. (math. schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; passed cadet, July, 1914; dist. offir., K. Langat, Nov., 1919; dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Mar., 1921; agt. to food contr., N. Sembilan in addn., Apr. to July, 1921; on sp. duty, rubber census office, Kuala Lumpur, June, 1922; offir., cls. III., Nov., 1923; second sec. to kovt., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; offir., cls. II., Nov., 1928; attld. lab. dept., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; ag. dep. contr., lab., Malaya, Jan.-Aug., 1930; commnr. lands and mines, Joh. rr., Apr., 1931.

KANE, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1910), J.P.—Ed. Mariat schl. and Braun's commn. schl., Wellington, N.Z.; record clk. in immigrn. de., 1880; resigns; joined partly. staff, 1886 and served in various offices; clk., house of reps., 1920; clk. of parlia. and clk. of leg. coun., 1930.
KANNANGARA, CHARLES HENRY, W.—B. 1840; cls. V Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1928; office asst., Matare kach., Oct., 1928.

KANNANGARA, EDWARD WILMOT.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; attld. to Kandy kach., Aug., 1919; attld. Batticaloa Kach., Jan., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, Avissawella, May, 1921; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1923; ditto, Jaffna, Nov., 1924; ditto, Balapitiya, June, 1926; asst. commnr. stamps, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. gov't. agt., Columbo, Mar., 1930; sec. to min., agr. and lands, Nov., 1931.

KANTAWALA, MOHAN HARGOWINDIAN.—B. 1890; M.A., Cantab.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1915; att. Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Anuradhapura, in add., July, 1915; addtl. pol. mag. and addtl. mun. max., Colombo, July, 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1918; office asst. to gov't. agt., E. Prov., Oct., 1919; pol. mag., Negombo, Jan., 1921; ditto, Panadura, June, 1923; ditto, Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1924; ag. dist. judge, Matare, May, 1927; addnl. dist. judge, Jaffna, June, 1927; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Mar., 1928; ag. dist. judge, Negombo, Nov., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; dist. judge, Negombo, Mar., 1930.

KAUFMANN, HILARY PILKINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Jaffna kach., June, 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., May, 1921; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov., 1922; dep. rubber contr., Nov., 1923; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1926; pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; chmn., munic. conn., Kandy, Oct., 1931.

KAUFMANN, JOHN ERNEST.—Forester, Umtata, 1900; clk., conservator of forest's office, Umtata, 1901; senr. clk., ditto, 1902; ast. dist. forest offr., Umtata, 1903; transf. to Kokstad, 1912; dist. forest offr., Kokstad, 1912; transf. to Knyana, 1925; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, eastern conservancy, 1926; conserv. of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1927; ditto, Midland conservancy, 1929; ditto, Eastern conservancy, 1931.
KAUNTEZ, W. H.—Bacteriologist and pathologist, E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1919.

KAY-MOAT, JOHN RICHARD, M.A., Oxon and Hon. Causa Adelaide, M.Sc., M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Bristol.—B. 1881; formerly ast. demonstr., physiology, Oxford Univ.; demonstr., pathology, Bristol Univ. and pathologist to R. Infirmary, Bristol; temp. surg. lieut. to R. Naval Divn., Aug., 1914; ag. surg. lieut. comdr. for anti-malarial work, Sierra Leone, May, 1918; demob. to act temp. as prof. of pathology, Bristol Univ., April, 1919; ast. prin. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, Mar., 1921; ag. prin. in 1921 and 1922 and 1926-28; prof. of physiology, Nov., 1921; lect. in pharmacology, June, 1923; del. to jubilee, Adelaide Univ., Aug., 1928.
KAYSER, GERALD WILLIAM ALLISON.—B. 1906; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; appointed to col. audit. dept. as ast. audr., Nyassaland, May, 1929.

KEANE, G.J., C.M.G. (1930), D.S.O., M.D., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. (Liverpool), major, R.A.M.C., R. of O.—Ent. col. med. serv., 1908 (sp. serv., Uganda); European war, D.S.O., desps.; dep. prin. med. offr. (native services), 1923; dir. med. and sany. services, Uganda, Jan., 1927.
KEARNEY, G.—Ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923.

KEAST, MAJOR SYDNEY BANKS, R.E. (R. of O.), M.C. and Bar, French Croix de Guerre—B. 1884; ed. City of London Schol.; Sundan P.W.D., 1906-13; suptg. sany. engrn. P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1913; Cameroons E.F., 1915-16; B.E.F., 1916-19; senr. pub. health engrn., 1924; ast., D.P.W., 1926; dep. D.P.W., 1928; D.P.W., 1932; lt.-col. commdg. Gold Coast Defence Force, 1931.
KEATES, CAPT. R. H. O., M.C., F.R.G.S.—B. 1896; ed. St. Peter's Schol., York; on mil. serv. France and Belgium, 1915-18; Germany, 1919; admnstr. offr., N. Nigeria, 1921; ast. treas., Nigeria, 1925.

KEATINGE, WILLIAM MAYBURY, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1879; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schol. and Trinity Coll., Dublin (Jellett prizeman); B.A., LL.D. (1900); L.L.D. (1903); solr., 1904; admnstr.-gen., regis. of deeds, and sec., Walfk comm., Zanzibar, Mar., 1915; joint custodian of enemy property and liquidator of enemy firms, Nov., 1915; off., trustee, 1919; contr., local clearing office, Nov., 1920; registr.-gen., pub. trustee and off. recr., Kenya, June, 1924.

KEEGAN, R. J.—B. 1901; ed. R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., Indian Army; served, N.W. Frontier and Iraq; ast. to commdt., pol. (Br. divn.), New Hebrides, Sept., 1927; ag. asct., Jan. to June, 1928 and Mar. to Nov., 1930; ag. dist. agt., Efate and commdt., constab., Nov., 1929 to June, 1930.
KEEN, KENNETH.—B. 1907; ed. King's Schl., Rochester, Guy's Hosp. and King's Coll., London; cadet, Hong Kong, 1930.

KEET, JOHAN DIERDIEK MOHR.—Ast. dist. forest offr., working plans, eastern conservancy, Jan., 1908; ast. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1914; ag. dist. forest offr., Knyana, Nov., 1915; dist. forest offr., Knyana, Apr., 1917; conserv. of forests, Transvaal, Mar., 1923; ch. forest offr. (ch., divn. of forest management), 1932.

KEIR, ALEXANDER, M.A., U.D.A.—B. 1884; ed. Fordyce Acad. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A. (Aberdeen), 1905; diploma, agr., Cambridge Univ., 1913; educ. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., 1906; inspr., schls., Selangor, 1911; head, Malay training coll., Matang, 1913; inspr., schls., Perak, 1920; repd. British Malaya at Imp. conferees, edun., 1923; ag. ch. inspr., English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1923 to Sept., 1924 and June, 1931; inspr., schls., Perak, Sept., 1924; del. for British Malaya to Imp. Educat. Conferees, June to July, 1927; inspr., schls., Perak, Apr., 1929.

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD, O.B.E. (1927), Commdr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd lieut., 7th Lanch. Fusils., Aug., 1914; capt., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; seconded, gen. staff, G.H.Q., E.E.F., July, 1915; Bimbashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. offr., Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Sudan, Aug., 1916; Darfur Expdn. and first inspr., Umkedada, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. and Bar); 2nd cls. mag., 1916; dist. commr., Eastern Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. Arabic exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; pub. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory adminstr., Dec., 1919; lat ast. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1925; ast. dist. commr., N. dist., lat Oct., 1925; dep. dist. commr., Jerusalem divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publications, "Darfur, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," "The Pageant of Jerusalem," joint editor, "Handbook of Palestine, 1922"; edr., "Gen. Regs. of the Govt. of Palestine."

KELLAGHER, GEORGE BANNERMAN.—B. 1889; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919; cadet, S.S., 1913; attd. res. councillor's office, Penang, Dec., 1913; attd. G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1914; passed cadet, Feb., 1917; supernmy. offr., cl. V., Feb., 1917; on mil. serv., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.E., Dec., 1917; B.E.F., France, Apr., 1918; wounded Oct., 1918; lieut., June, 1919; demob., Feb., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; currency commr., Mar., 1927; ag. legal advr., Kedah, Aug., 1930.

KELLIE, GEORGE GORDON.—Seconded from London postal serv. and apptd. 2nd ast. dir. posts and tels., Somaliland, Jan., 1924; postal ast. dir., Somaliland, Apr., 1926; ag. D.P.T., June-Oct., 1927; survr., posts and tels., Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

KELLY, SIR HENRY GREENE, KT. BACH. (1906).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; honorman and prizeman in hist. and political science; B.A. and LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dub., Mich., Term, 1884; apptd. a puisne judge in the Niger Territories, 1891; acted on several occasions as chief justice; chief justice, 1899; chief justice of Southern Nigeria, 1900; ret., 10th Feb., 1908.

KELLY, HON. HUGH THOMAS.—B. 1858; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1908; pres., York Co. Law Assoc., 1910 and 1911; chmn., Toronto pub. lib. bd. for 3 years; a gov. of Toronto Univ., 1906-1912; jud. of high ct. of Ontario, 1911.

KELLY, PECY JAMES, C.B.E. (1932), M.B., Ch. B. (Glasgow).—B. 1876; house surg., W. Kent gen. hosp., Maidstone, 1907-8; certif. Lond. schl. of trop. med., 1908; W. African med. serv., G. Coast, 1908; med. offr., Hong Kong, 1910; med. offr. to Victoria gaol and visiting med. offr. to Tung Wa hospital; med. offr. in charge of infectious diseases hosp., Kennedy Town; regiar. and lecturer on physiology, med. coll., Ceylon, 1912; on military duty, 1915-1919; surg. gen. and regiar.-gen., B. Guiana, 1922; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; inspr. prisons and immigrn. agt. gen., 1932, in addn. to other duties.

KELLY, ROBERT ALBOP, A.S.A.A., J.P.—B. 1881; ed. George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh; clk., inland rev., 1898; S.S. war, 1900-02 (Queen's med., 5 clasps); asst. acct., N. Rhodesia, 1911; assist. audir., 1913; ag. audir., 1914-15 and in 1919; lieut., N.R. Rifles, 1916; ag. adjt., 1917-18; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, 1921; ag. treas., 1922; ag. treas. and mem., exec. coun., 1925, 1927 and 1928; ag. comnr., taxes, 1928; treas. and currency offr., mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gold Coast, 1929.

KELLY, ROBERT WILLIAM CREIGHTON, M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.)—B. 1888; med. offr. i/o schls. and venereal diseases, S.S., Apr., 1923; med. offr., social hygiene, May, 1926; health offr., May, 1927; chief med. offr., social hygiene, S.S. Feb., 1928.

KELSICK, OSCAR RICHARDSON.—Ag. cashier, savings bank, Antigua, Aug., 1906; apptd. as ditto, Jan., 1908; acctnt., savings bank, Sept., 1908; treas., May to Aug., 1909; junr. audit clk., June, 1909; 3rd customs offr., treas., Antigua, Apl., 1915; clk. to treas.; clk. to postmaster, cashier and acctnt., savings bank, clk. to exec. coun., Virgin Is., July, 1915; asst. treas., colir., cust., postmr., etc., Montserrat, Apr., 1924; mem., leg. coun., Montserrat, July, 1924; J.P., Nov., 1924.
KELVIN-STARK, DAVID.—B. 1905; ed. Taunton; B.A. (Lond.), 1928; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1928; 3rd asst. ool. sec., 1931.
KEMP, GEN. THE HON. JAN C. G.—M.L.A., Union of S. Africa since 1920; min. of agr., 30th June, 1924.

KEMP, SIR JOSEPH HORSFORD, KT. BACH. (1927), C.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1918).—B. 1874; B.A., Cape Univ.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; land office work, New Territory, 1899; regir., land court, 1900; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1900 to 1904; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1904; asst. sec., sanitary bd., 1904; deputy-regir. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1904; ag. regir., sup. ct., offi. admistr., offi. trustee, and regir. of companies, 1907; ag. offi. recvr. in bank, Apl., 1907, to Apl., 1908; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1908 to 1909; head of san. dept., 1908; regir., sup. ct., offi. adminitr., offi. trustee, and regir. of comp., 1909; crown solr., 1911; ag. puisne judge, 1913; ag. atty.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., 1915; ag. ch. just., Apr.-July, 1930; ch. just., Aug., 1930.

KEMPE, JOHN ERSKINE.—B. 1888; ed. Radley Coll. and University Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. hon. sch. mod. hist.); B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; asst. dist. offr., Feb., 1916; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Feb., 1919; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Pahang, Apr., 1919; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; relief asst. sec. to gov't. F.M.S., Mar., 1927; clk., fedl. coun. in addn., Apr., 1927; fedl. examr., Malay, Jan., 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Apr., 1931; mem., Perak coun. of state, Jan., 1932.
KENDRICK, EDWARD ROBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1913; senr. exec. engnr., 1927; ag. asst. D.P.W., in 1927-28.

KENMUIR, THEODORE JAMES.—B.1878; apptd. treasy., Natal, Jan., 1896; acctg. offr., Natal treasy., Mar., 1901; acct. July, 1903; senr. clk., July, 1906; ag. ch. acct., July, 1909; prin. clk., May, 1910; transf., Union treasy., Oct., 1910; transf. to head office, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Apr., 1911; recr. of rev., Kimberley, July, 1911; head office I.R. dept., Pretoria, Jan., 1915; sp. grade prin. clk. ditto, Apr., 1918; survr., Jan., 1920; ch. clk., income tax, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Apr., 1920; recr. of rev., Pretoria, Mar., 1921; ch. clk., gen. admins., I.R. dept., Pretoria, Dec., 1923; ch. rev. offr., ditto, Sept., 1929; recr., rev., Cape Town, 1930.
KENNAN, MYLES.—B. 1894; clk., Basutoland, 1913; war serv., 1915-18; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1926.
KENNAN, THOMAS BERETON, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Collet Court, Lond. and Worcester Cathedral Schl.; cler. asst., Basutoland, 1910; sub-inspr., pol., 1913; inspr., 1919; served in European War; comnr., 1929.

KENNEDY, F.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apl., 1917; passed London matric., June, 1917; on mil. service from 17th Sept., 1917, to 19th March, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher gr., 1st June, 1928.

KENNEDY, HON. ROBERT, M.A., LL.M. (N.Z.).—B. 1887; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; admitted barrister and solr., 1909; examr. in law to N.Z. Univ.; pres., N.Z. law soc'y., 1924-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1929.
KENNEDY, W.—Vet. offr., E.A.P., May, 1910; dep. chief vet. offr., Aug., 1914; ch. vet. offr., Kenya, Apr., 1921.

KENNEDY-SKIPTON, GEORGE STACY.—B. 1898; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st class schl., 1919; triple senr. mod., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; asst. head, sany. dept. and sec., sany. bd., 1924; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., 1926; asst. supt., impta. and expts., 1926; asst. treas., 1927; dep. estate duty comnr., 1931.

KENNIFF, VICTOR, B.E. (SYD.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (AUST.), M.I.M. AND CY. E.—B. 1886; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; asst. eng., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1916; dist. eng., P.W.D., Fiji, 1921; ag. asst. comnr. of wks., 1927; ag. comnr. of wks., chmn., cent. road bd., Apl. to Dec., 1927; mem., leg. coun., Fiji, May to Dec., 1927; asst. D.P.W., Cyprus, Mar., 1930; ag. D.P.W., July to Oct., 1930 and Feb.-Apr., 1931.

KENT, HENRY TUFFLEY MONEY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Elstow schl. City and Guilds Coll.; instr., tech. schl., F.M.S., Sept., 1914; ag. timber suptd., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1914; contr., timber supplies, F.M.S. rly., Jan., 1919; ditto, forest dept., S. Stilts, and F.M.S., Jan., 1923; snt to report on timber utilization problems in India and Burma, May, 1924; on spl. duty, Madison, U.S.A., Nov. to Dec., 1926; prin., tech. schl., F.M.S., Dec., 1930.
KENT, HON. JAS. M.—K.C., B.A.; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1904; mem. of exec. coun. and min. of just., 1907; judge, supreme etc., 1916.

KERR, DAVID IRVINE.—B. 1878; clk. to pol. mag., Grenada, 1899; rev. offr. (various grades) 1902-15; 2nd clk., treas., 1918; ch. rev. offr., 1919; ag. ch. of pol., 1920 and 1924; ch. clk., P.W.D., 1921; supt., of prisons and recorder of meteorological obsns., 1922; seconded for war service (Egypt and E. Africa), 1915-19; J.P., 1919.

KERR, ROBERT ASHFORD.—B. 1870; ed. pub. sch., Swellendam; served Matabelo war and rebellion and seriously wounded in formerly mem., dep. ch., and ch. of com. prov. coun., Transvaal and pres. senate; mem., Union senate since 1921.

KERR, ROBERT TAYLOR, A.M.I.Mech.E., M.I., Loco.E.—B. 1888; ed. Dumfries Acad.; N. British Rly., 1903; wks. man., Gold Coast rly., Apr., 1915; title changed to asst. loco. supt., Jan., 1917; seconded to Togoland milv. rly. for periods, 1917-20; dist. loco. supt., Gold Coast rly., Apr., 1923; wks. man., July, 1925; ch. mech. engr., Apr., 1926.

KERR-PEARSE, MAJOR B.A.T., C.M.G. (1929) (formerly Rifle Brigade).—B. 1871; A.D.C. to govt. gen. of Australia (Lord Northcote), 1905-07; priv. sec. to gov. of West Australia (Sir G. Strickland), 1909-12; priv. sec. to gov. of Tasmania (Rt. Hon. Sir W. Ellison-Macartney), 1913-15; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia (Rt. Hon. Sir R. Munro Ferguson), 1915-16; mil. sec., 1916.

KERSHAW, ARTHUR EDWIN POMEROY, M.C., A.I.M.M.—B. 1890; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1912; sec. for milv. serv., Sept., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.F.A. (S.R.), Feb., 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1919; asst. warden, mines, Sept., 1921; senr. inspr., mines, B. Gajah, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, mines, Perak, Apr., 1931.

KESTEVEN, LEOFRIC, F.R.I.B.A., M.R.SAN.I., M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1882; draftsman, govt. architect's office, F.M.S., Feb., 1912; ag. asst. govt. architect, July, 1912-Mar., 1913 and May, 1914; archit.-ct asst. to D.P.W., Mar., 1918; asst. architect, state engrn. office, K. Lumpur, Apr., 1928; ar. asst. architect, Aug., 1930.

KIBBLE, EDMUND DAVID, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Highgate Schl. and City & Guilds (Engng.) Coll., S. Kensington; asst. engrn., pub. wks. dept., F.M.S., Dec., 1907; ex. engrn., pub. wks. dept., F.M.S., 1914; senr. ex. engrn., 1923; senr. ex. engrn., F.M.S., July, 1924; addl., govt. town planning dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1924; state engrn., Pahang, June, 1927; ch. hydraulic engrn., Aug., 1930; ag. state engrn., Perak, Apr., 1931.

KIDD, MAJOR GEORGE MONTGOMERY, B.A., Dublin, M.C.—B. 1889; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; seconded for milv. serv. 1915 to 1919; m.c. to advr., Kedah, Jan., 1920; asst. agt., food contr., S. Kedah, Feb., 1920; agt., food contr., Kedah, Sept., 1920; dep. supt., cens., K. dah., Oct., 1920; sec., sany. bd., Alor Star, Sept., 1922; sec., rubber restn. comtee., Kedah, Oct., 1922; addl., rubber restn. off., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1925; chmn., assess. comtee., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., May, 1927; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1929; ag. asst. advr., Muar, Sept., 1932.
KIDD, CAPT. W. R., M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

KIDSON, ERNEST O.B.E. (1919), M.A., D.Sc., F.Inst.P.—B. 1882; ed. Nelson Coll. and Canterbury Coll., N.Z.; on staff, Australian Commonwealth meteorological bureau, 1921-27; asst. dir., 1923-27; dir., meteorological services, N.Z., 1927.

KIDSON, NORMAN S. B.—B. 1885; served in Connaught Rangers, 1914-19; 2nd asst. mast., Boys' Gram. Schl., Fiji, Mar., 1920; clk. of peace, Rewa, Sept., 1920; ag. dist. commnr., Rewa and ag. prov. commnr., Naitasiri, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. commnr. and prov. commnr., Colo East, Apr., 1921; govt. sec., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., Nov., 1922; on duty as judl. commnr. for W. Pacific, Apr., 1924; has acted as offr.-in-charge at headqrs. on several occasions; ag.-rem. commnr. on various occasions, 1926-1932; offm. mem. advisory coun., 1927; judl. commnr. (temp. and provisional), Nov., 1928.

KILPIN, RALPH.—B. 1887; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondeshoek, S. Africa; clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1905; clk. of papers, Union H. of A., 1910; 2nd clk.-asst., 1917; clk.-asst. and acctnt., 1920; admitted sworn translator (Eng. and Dutch), sup. ct., of S. Africa, 1912; assessor apptd. by admstr., Cape exec. combee, elections and elec. of senators since 1910; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-17; recd. vote of thanks of leg. assebly, S.W. Africa, for preparing standing rules and orders and assisting in inauguration of assembly, 1926; author of "The Old Cape House" and "The Romance of a Colonial Parliament."
KIMBELL, ALFRED HENRY.—B. 1875; ed. at Otago Boys' High Schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. pub. serv., 1896; held various positions in P.W.D., incl. land purch. offr.; und.-sec. of mines, 1920.

KIMBER, GUTHRIE.—B. 1906; ed. Per.-e Schl. and Cambridge; nat. sci. schl., Clare Coll., 1924-27; 1st cls. nat. sci. trip.s, pt. I, 1922; 2nd cls. pt. II, 1927; B.A., 1927; appld. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 19th Oct., 1928; temp. ag. prin., 18th Mar., 1932.

KING, BERNARD FRANCIS, Ph.B. (Louv.).—B. 1896; ed. Clongowes Wood Coll., Ireland and Université Catholique de Louvain, Belgium; served in Coldstream Guards and London Regt., 1914-19 (ment. in desn.); invalided from Army, 1919; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1920; practised at bar, Br. Guiana; asst. to atty.gen., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1926; ag. atty-gen., Aug., 1929 to May, 1930; regisr., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Apr., 1930.

KING, CLEMENT HAMPDEN.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ag. 5th cls. clk., police dept., B. Guiana, June, 1893; gradual prom. to prin. clk.; dist. inspr. of police, 1901; county inspr., Dec., 1908; ag. adjt., July, 1907 to Mar., 1908, Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909, and from Oct., 1911 to Apr., 1912; adjt. and staff offr., local forces, Oct., 1914 to Aug., 1919; capt., B. Guiana Militia, Oct., 1914; major, Aug., 1919; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. and supt. of Georgetown fire brig., July to Oct., 1914; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. on various occasions; dep. inspr.-gen. pol. and supt. fire brigade, Aug., 1919; commnr. pol., Sierra Leone, June, 1921.

KING, SIR FREDERICK TRUBY, Kt. Bach. (1925), C.M.G. (1917), M.B., B.Sc.—Founder of New Zealand Pinneket Soc'y, and famous for his work in connection with infant-life protection; dir. of divn. of child-welfare, N.Z. health dept., 1921; inspr.-gen., mental hosps., 1925-27.

KING, GEORGE HOWARD, I.S.O. (1932).—Ag. clk., treasry., St. Kitts, 1894; ag. rev. offr., Nevis, 1895; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, 1895; ag. clk., regir.'s office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1898; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1905; 1st clk., P.O., and sub-inspr. of schls., St. Kitts, 1906; ag. postmr., St. Kitts, 1914; postmr., May, 1915.

KING, JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1900; entered C.O., Sept., 1916; served with R.N.V.R. from 30th Sept. to 19th Dec., 1918; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler.offr., 4th July, 1928.

KING, SYDNEY NOEL.—B. 1897; ed. Dover Coll. and Bedford Schl.; served in R.F.A., 1916-1920 (coman., Aug., 1916); cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; asst. advr., Kukub, July, 1928; ag. 1st mag., Johore Bahru, Sept., 1928; dep. pres., town bd., in addn., Oct., 1928; dep. pub. pros., Johore, Nov., 1929; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932.

KING, THOS. HENRY.—B. 1883; ed. at Exeter Gram. Schl.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1904; passed in Cantonese, 1907; attchd. to Punjab pol., 1907; passed in Hindustani, 1907; ag. asst. supt. of pol., 1908; J.P., 1908; ag.-supt., impts. and expts., Dec., 1909; asst. emigrn. offr. in addition to pol. duties, 1910; passed in Punjabi, 1910; ag. asst. supt., pol., 1911; mem. bd. of examrs., 1912; asst. supt., pol., 1912; ag. dep. supt., pol. and fire brig., 1913; dep. supt., pol., 1921; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1921 and June-July, 1925; dir., crim. intell., 1923; ag. capt. supt., pol., Mar. to Nov., 1929; dep. insprn-gen., pol., Sept., 1930; ag. insprn-gen., pol., Mar., 1932.

KING, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM LYON MACKENZIE, P.C. (1:12), C.M.G. (1906), M.A., Toronto Univ., LL.B., Toronto Univ., Ph.D., Harvard Univ.—B. 1874; aptd. dep. min. of labour and editor of "Labour Gazette," 1902; regir. of boards of conciliation and investigation, 1907; sworn of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of labour, 1909; defeated at g.e., 1911; special investigator, Rockefeller Foundation, 1914; chosen leader of the opposition, H. of C., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Prince Co., P.E.I., 20th Oct., 1919; re-elec. for N. York, Ont., g.e., Dec., 1921; prime minister, Dominion of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921, also pres. of the coun. and sec. of state for external affairs; represented Canada at Imp. Confce., 1923; prime min., 1926-30; leader of opposition, 1930.

KINGDON, Sir DONALD, Kt. Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A., LL.B.—Ed. at Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tem., July, 1905; legal asst. and inspir. of schls., Gambia, Jan., 1907; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Mar. to July, 1912; atty.-gen., Uganda, Oct., 1912; ag. chief just., Apr., 1917, to July, 1918; atty.-gen., G. Coast, Sept., 1918; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1919; compiler of 1920 revd. edn., G. Coast laws, 1920; atty.-gen., Nigeria, Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; compiler, revd. edn., Nigeria laws, 1923; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Oct., 1928 to Feb., 1929; ch. just., Sept., 1929.

KING-HARMAN, Sir CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Chelt. Col.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1879; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec., Mauritius, 1883; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; admstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high comsnr., Cyprus, 15th Apr., 1904; represented Mediterranean Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

KINGSBURY, ALLAN NEAVE, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—Asst. pathologist, Middlesex Hosp. and bacteriologist, Middlesex Hosp., ven. diseases clinic; pathologist, City of Lond. Mat. Hosp.; lieut., R.F.A.; ag. govt. pathologist, Penang, 1923; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1924; on sp. duty in India and Ceylon, 1924; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research and ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., 1925; professor of bacteriology, King Ed. VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, 1926; bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Research, F.M.S., Apr., 1927; ag. dir., ditto, Aug., 1927; dir., ditto, Oct., 1927; awarded N. Persian Forces memorial med. for re-search, Oct., 1927; del. L. of N. health organization laboratory confce., Copenhagen, May 1928; rep. C.O. at Internat. Cancer Confce., London, July, 1928; rep. F.M.S. at Leonard Wood memorial leprosy confce., Manila, Dec., 1930; author of many medical and scientific publications.

KINNEARD, GEORGE, M.D. (Univ. of Manitoba), M.C.P.S. (Sask.), Certif. L.S.T.M. & H.—B.1894; ed. Regina Collegiate Inst., 1st cls. certif., Regina Normal Schl., 1912; trn. Earl Grey and Govan. Pub. Schla. (Sask.), 1913-17; Leeward Is. med. serv., 1926; D.M.O., dist. D. Dominica; M.O., Bahamas gen. hosp., 1930; ag. M.O. and bacteriologist, Bahamas gen. hosp., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., 1932.
KIPPING, RUPERT HARRY, M.B., Ch.B. (Leed.).—B. 1904; late jun. house surg., Darlington Gunl. Hosp.; locum house surg., Sheffield Roy. Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, Feb. 1929.
KIRBY, A. C.—Asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; 2nd grade res., Nyassaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade res., Mar., 1925.

KIRBY, AUSTIN HENRY, B.A. (Cantab.), O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1879; lecturer in science, Antigua, under the Imperial dept. of agric., 1903 to 1909; scientific asst. in the Imperial dept. of agric., and in charge of publications, 1909 to 1913; asst. director of agric., Southern Provs., Nigeria, 1913; dir. of agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; dir., agr., Sierra Leone, 1930 (on retirement); author of publications on tropical agric., educn. and tropical hygiene.

KIRK, JAMES BALFOUR.—B. 1893; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edin Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Oxford), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), F.Z.S. (Lond. and Scot.), Vans Dunlop scholarship bot. and zool., Edin. Univ., 1911, certif. (with distinc.), L.S.T.M., 1922; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-16; lieut., R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917; capt., 1918; asst. pol. offr., Hai, 1918-19; civ. surg., Arabistan, 1919; asst. M.O., Baghdad, 1919-20; bacteriologist, R. Inst. Pub. Health, 1920-21; demonstr., L.S.T.M., 1921; M.O.I., P. Louis, Mauritius, 1922; ag. dir., med. and health dept., Mar. to Dec., 1927; dir., ditto, Dec., 1927; author of "Pub. Health Practice in the Tropics" (1931), "Hints on Equipment and Health for intending Residents in the Tropics" and several articles on med. and hygienic subjects.

KIRKHAM, G. H.—B. 1881; S. African constab., 1901; served S. African War, 1901-02 (wounded); Transvaal pol., 1908; adjt., 4th S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1914; S. African Rebellion, 1914; capt., 1st S. African Infy. (Overseas Brig.) 1916-18; German W. Africa campaign, 1914-16; ment. in despatches, France, 1916-17 (twice wounded); M.C., Apr., 1917; King's pol. med.; dep. commr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1918; commr., 1929.

KIEKPATRICK, THOMAN WINIFRED, M.A. (Cantab.), Dipl. Agr. (Cantab.), F.E.S.—B. 1896; ed. Marlborough and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; mily. serv., 1915-19; entomologist, miny. agr., Egypt, 1921; do., Kenya Colony, 1925; entomologist, Welcome Trop. Research Labs., Sudan, 1928; entomologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.
KIRSOPP, GEORGE DOUGAL.—Asst. collr. cust., E.A. Prof., Jan., 1914; collr. cust., Kenya Col., Jan., 1920; comptr. cust., Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; ag. treas. for various periods, 1924-32.
KIRWAN, ARTHUR CONINGSBY—Jun. clk., col. sec.'s off., Falkland Is., Jan., 1907.
KIRWAN, SIR JOHN WATERS, Kt. Bach. (1930).—B.1869; M.L.C., S. Prov., W. Australia since 1908; chmn. of comtees., 1923-26; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

KITCHING, A. E.—Ed. Oundle Schol. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; dist. offr., June, 1926; ag. asst. sec., native affrs., Apr., 1929; asst. sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. sec., native affrs., Apr. to Dec., 1931; dep. prov. commr., Apr., 1932.

KITCHING, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; ed. Lancaster and Peterhouse, Cambridge; survey prob., F.M.S., May, 1913; asst. supt., rev. surveys, Negri Sembilan, 1919; Perak, 1921; Selangor, 1924; supt., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., 1930; seconded to Trengganu as supt., surveys, 1928-31; officiating asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., 1932.

KITTERMMASTER, SIR HAROLD BAXTER, K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G. (1926), O.B.K. (1918).—Ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Ch., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1902-07; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. commr., 1915; offr. in charge, N.F.D., 1916; secy. to admnstr., Somaliland, Aug., 1921; gov. and commdr-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 1926; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Br. Honduras, 1931.

KNAPMAN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—S.A. War; Transvaal civ. serv., 1900; ch. clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; office supt., secretariat, E.A.P., 1911; lieut., K.A.R., 1917-19; estab. offr., secretariat, Kenya, 1923; Queen's S.A. med., Br. War and Victory meda.

KNIBBS, STANLEY GEORGE CURTHOYS, F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Sydney Gram. Schol., New South Wales; dipl. in all subjects, Hawkesbury Agrl. Coll., 1908; land survr., Fiji, 1911; land survr., Victoria and N.S.W., 1912; crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1913; ag. supt., pub. wks. in addn., 1918; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands during 1919; regar. of land titles in addn., 1919; dep. commr. for W. Pacific, 1919; dep. for natives in land comm., 1920 and 1921; ag. res. commr. during 1920; commr. of currency (temp.), 1920-24; qualified as civ. engnr. to shires and municipalities of N.S.W., 1923; commr. of lands and crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1924; chmn., mining bd., 1927; attended Imp. Survey Conf., and Internat. Geog. Conf., 1928.

KNIGHT, ALWIN TRIGGS.—B. 1887; statn. mast., Ipoh, July, 1913; ditto, K. Lumpur, Dec., 1913; ditto, Prai, Jan., 1914; traffic inspr., Turnpat, Jan., 1915; sec. to Siamese State Rlys., Sept., 1917; asst. traffic man., F.M.S. Rlys., Jan., 1918; asst. traffic man., S'pore, May, 1931.

KNIGHT, JOHN MARCUS, M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1884; ed. Grenada Grammar Schol.; 5th clk., treasry., Grenada, 1912; 2nd clk., G.P.O., 1913; served in the Great War, 1914-19; Egypt, 1916; Palestine, 1917; twice ment. in despatches; rev. and cust. offr., Grenada, 1919; 2nd clk., secretariat and clk., leg. coun., 1921; ch. clk., gov.'s office, Windward Is. and clk., ex. coun., Grenada, 1925; sec. to govt. of Windward Is., 1928.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS.—Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addtnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan, 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914.

KNIGHT-BRUCE, G. K.—B.1891; ed. Radley Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A. (2nd cls. hona., schl. of jurisprudence), 1913; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ment. in despatches, 1919; ag. dist. commr., 1920; ag. res. mag., Mombasa, 1923; res. mag., Uganda, 1925; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. judge, Zanzibar, 1926; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; ag. judge, Zanzibar, for various periods, 1927-32; ag. atty.-gen., 1930.

KNOBLAUCH, CHARLES TRAPPES.—B. 1871; ed. at pub. schl., Caledon and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk., atty-gen.'s office, Cape, 1890; asst. acct. and bookkeeper, A.G.O., 1901; prin. clk., master's office, Cape, 1922; ch. clk. and asst. mast., sup. ct., O.F.S., 1923; mast., sup. ct., 1926.

KNOLLYS, LANCELOT ERskine.—B. 1885; ed. Dover Coll. and S.E. Agric. Coll., Wye, Kent (dep. agric.); asst. supt., cotton cult., Uganda, 1909; asst. dist. commr., 1914; 1st grade admin. offr., 1925; sen. asst. sec., 1931.

KNOLLYS, WILFRED ERskine.—Ed. King's Schol., Bruton, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1906; clk., colonial audit branch, E. and A. dept., Oct., 1906; asst. aud., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Nov., 1907; asst. aud., Uganda Prot., Aug., 1909; senr. aud. aud., E. Africa Prot., June, 1915; sen. asst. aud.-in-charge, German E. Africa prov. admntr., Apr., 1917; audr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; audr., Nigeria, May, 1931.

KNOWLES, CHARLES HENRY, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1878; ed. Burton-on-Trent gram. schl. and Mason Univ. Coll., Birmingham; res. master, agric. schl., St. Vincent, Jan., 1902; supt. of agric., Fiji, 1905; M.L.C., 1913; chmn., agric. experiments comtee., 1916; chmn. food supply comtee., 1917; ag. govt. entomologist (conjoint), 1917 and 1919-21; J.P., 1917; M.E.C. (temp.), June, 1920; prov. supt., agric., G. Coast, May, 1921; dep. dir., agr., Aug., 1924; dir., agr., Dec., 1924.
KNOWLES, JOHN WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1877; joined Trinidad govt. serv., 1898; warden, counties Nariva-Mayaro, June, 1922; warden, St. Andrew and St. David, Dec., 1925.

KOTZEBUE, HON. SIR JOHN GILBERT, Kt. Bach. (1917), K.C., LL.B. (LOND.).—B. 1849; ed. South African Coll. and Lond. Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1874; sole judge of the high court of the Transvaal, 1877 to 1881; one of the commrs. under the convention of Pretoria to settle claims for losses and injuries suffered during the Boer War of 1880-81; chief justice of the Transvaal, 1881-1898; chmn. of bd. of examrs. in literature and science, Transvaal, 1890-1898; recd. the Portuguese honour of Knight Grand Cross of the Order of the Conception, 1896; atty.-gen. and member of exec. and legis. couns., S. Rhodesia, 1900; ag. admstr. S. Rhodesia, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, 1903, and judge pres., Eastern dists. ct., 1904; chmn. of the coun. of Rhodes Univ. Coll.; examr. in law and jurisprudence to the Univ. of C. of G. H., 1903-1908; puisne judge, C. of G.H. Prov. Div., 1st Apr., 1913; judge, president, 14th June, 1920; judge of appeal, appellate divn., June, 1922; ret., 1932.

KOTZE, PIETER CANZIUS, B.A. (Cape), Dip. For. (Oxon.).—Aat. forest offr., King William's Town, 1911; ditto, Port Elizabeth, 1912; ditto, Concordia, 1914; forest offr., Storms River, 1918; ditto, Eshowe, 1920; ditto, Kokstad, 1927; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1929; conservator, forest, Midland conservancy, 1931.

KOTZE, SIR ROBT. NELSON, KT. (BACH.), B.A.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Schl. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., S.A. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of S.A.; asst. engrnr. and consulting engrnr. to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-1907; govt. mining engrnr., Transvaal, 1st Jan., 1908; ditto, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn., Univ. of Witwatersrand; ret., 1st Nov., 1926.

KRAUSE, FREDERICK EDWARD TRAUGOTT.—B. 1868; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Amsterdam, London, Cambridge Univ.; B.A. (Hons.), Cape, 1888; LL.D., Amsterdam, 1893; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Cape, 1905; advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa and high ct., Rhodesia; state prosecutor and ag. atty.-gen., Witwatersrand, 1896-1900; sp. commdt. and mil. govr., Witwatersrand, 1900; chmn., mining regns. comm., 1907-9; mem., Johannesburg town coun., 1905-9; M.L.A., Vrededorp, 1907-10; chmn., hosp. inquiry comm., Johannesburg; mem., prop. representation comm.; mem., Witwatersrand cent. schl. bd.; K.C. (Union of S. Africa), 1912; ag. judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., Apr., 1923.

KRIGE, HON. CHRISTIAN JOEL, M.L.A.—B. 1868; intern. B.A.; atty.-at-law; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; rep. Calden in parl. since 1904; spkr., H. of A., U. of S. Africa, 1915-24; ch. whip, S.A. party for several years prior to elec. as spkr.

KRUGER, JOHANNES JACOB.—B. 1888; on active serv., E. Africa and overseas, 1915-29; govt. printer, 1927; Union R. of O. with rank of major; mem., Union tender bd. and govt. rep., coun., Pretoria tech. coll.

KUIT, ALBERT.—S. Africa Repub., govt. and Z.A.S.M. rly. serv. till Br. annexation; served Jameson raid and Boer war on Boer side; finan. and estate agt., Amersfoort ('T'vaal) town coun., J.P. and practically all public bodies, 1902-25; rly. commr. since 1925; pub. debt, commr. since 1928; a commr. of oaths for Union.

KUSCHKE, HERMANN F.G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal schl., Stoffberg Gedeng Schl., 1916-24; organising instr., native educn., O.F.S., 1924; ch. instr., native educn., O.F.S., 1926.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, C.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regsr., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., adminstr.'s office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1886; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several commrs. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admstr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. admstr. and col. sec., May to Nov., 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada; for various periods, 1915-20; admstd., govt., Sept.-Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.

LACEY, MAJOR ALFRED TRAVERS.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Schl., Norwich and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripods; hons. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; dir., educn., Nyasaland Prot., 1930.

LEAFRLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., LL.D.—B. 1887; ed. Flores's Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903; capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elem. schls., Malta, Aug., 1920.
LA FONTAINE, SYDNEY HUBERT, O.B.E. (1930), D.S.O., M.C. (1916).—Aat. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1910; district commr., Kenya, Oct., 1918; 2nd grade prov. commr., 1931.

LAGDEN, SIR GODFREY YEATMAN, K.C.M.G. (1897), K.B.E. (1927), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1861; clerk, G.P.O., London, 1869-77, after compet. exam.; clk. to sec. to govt., Transvaal, 1878; priv. sec. to admstr., 1878-81; sec. to exec. coun., 1880-81; priv. sec. to Sir W. Bellairs and Sir E. Wood, while administering govt., 1881; sec to Transvaal sub-comm. for compensation claims, 1881-82; engaged as war correspondent, Egyptian campaign, 1882-3, present at all engagements, including Tel-el-Kebir and capture of Cairo; on special financial duty, G. Coast, 1883; sec. and acctnt., Basutoland, 1884; asst. commr., 1886; established magistracy in Masupha's country, 1886; acted as res. commr., May to Nov., 1890; ag. Br. commr., Swaziland govt. comte., adminstr., Apr., 1892; res. commr., Basutoland, 1893-1901; commr. for native affairs, Transvaal, Aug., 1901; mem. of ex. and leg. couns.; chmn. of S. African intercol. native affairs comm., 1903-4; retired, 1907.

LAING, GORDON DACOMB, B.Sc., M.B. Ch.B. (St. And.), D.P.H. (Johannesburg)—B. 1899; ed. Merchiston Schl., Pietermaritzburg, Madras Coll., St. Andrews, and St. Andrews' Univ.; senr. lect. in anatomy, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1924; asst. pathologist, S. African Inst. for Med. Research, 1927; asst. health offr. for Union of S. Africa, 1928; med. offr., health, Pretoria munici., Nov., 1929; published some papers on anatomical and anthropological subjects in the Journal of the S.African Assocn. for the Advancement of Science.

LAING, MALCOLM BUCHANAN.—B. 1890; ed. Forest Schl., Essex; asst. commissary, B. Guiana, Oct., 1909; seconded as ch. clk., immigrn. dept., Apr. 1913; mem., govt. standing bd. of examrs. in Hindi, etc., Nov., 1913; immigrn. agt., Aug., 1920; capt. B. Guiana Arty. (Militia), Jan., 1921; senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1925; clk. to exec. coun. and leg. coun., July, 1928; priv. sec. to gov., Apr., 1929 to June, 1930; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1932.

LAKE, FREDERICK JAMES.—B.1894; 2nd divn. clk., G.P.O., 1912; B.E.F., France, 1915; K.A.R., German E. Africa, 1917; exec. offr., G.P.O., 1920; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika, 1921; dist. offr., 1923; dist. offr., 1932.

LAKE, MORICE CHALLONER.—B. 1885; ed. Wellington Coll. and Sandhurst; comsdr., unatt. list (attd. 1st Battn. Somerset Light Infy.), Jan., 1904; T.A., 1905; regt. duty, 1905-15; Aden, 1913; served, Aden Field Force, 1915-19; offr. in ch., signals, 1915-16; offr. commdg. signal schi., Lucknow, 1916-17; raised and commanded Aden (Arab) Labour Corps, 1917-18; raised and commanded 1st Yemen Infy., 1918-25; sp. duty, W.O. revising mily. rept., Aden Prot., 1926-27; reverted, mily. duty, India, 1927-28; adviser to C.O. and Air Miny. on raising levies, etc., in Aden, 1928; polit. offr., Aden, Nov., 1928; ag. 2nd asst. res. and Prot. sec., July to Nov., 1930.
LAMACQ, FRANK CONSTANT.—B. 1903; apptd. cler. offr., London telephone service, May, 1921; transferred to C.O., July, 1923.
LAMB, F. M.—Senior staff survr., E.A.P., May, 1907; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1910; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Jan., 1911; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1918.
LAMBERT, CHARLES DOUGLAS, M.I.Mech.E., M.I.Loco. E., M.I.Transport.—B. 1885; ch. mech. engr., Kowloon Canton rly., 1910.

LAMBERT, C. E.—B. 1900; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. clk., cust., Dec., 1919 to Jan., 1920; R.I.C., Feb., 1920 to Feb., 1921; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921 to 7th July, 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1923; seconded as regnr. on staff of high comsnr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Great Britain, 1923-33; returned to C.O., 12th Sept., 1932.

LAMBERT, SIR HENRY CHARLES MILLER, K.C.M.G. (1919); C.B. (1910).—B. 1868; ed. at Eton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; 1st class in classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schls., 1891; apptd. 2nd class clk. in the C.O. after open compet. exam., Feb., 1892; mem. of comtee., emigrts.' information office, 1894; chmn. of the comtee., 1897-1907; acted as priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain for the purposes of the comtee. of the H. of C. on South Africa, 1897; 1st class clk., Nov., 1898; visited Canada, 1903; visited Australia, on behalf of E. I. O., in connection with emigration questions, 1905; prin. clk., Jan., 1907; acting officer, C.O.; asst. under-sec. of state, 4th June, 1916; sec. to the Impl. Confe.; senr. crown agt. for the cols., 1921-32; ag. under sec. of state for the cols., 30th July, 1924 to 4th Aug., 1925.
LAMBERT, H. E.—Tempy. asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

LAMBERT, R. W.—Asst. auditor, E.A.P., Mar., 1912; asst. dist. comsnr., May, 1914; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1925; ag. prin. asst. comsnr. for local govt., lands and stltmt., 1928.

LAMBORN, WILLIAM ALFRED STEWELL, O.B.E. (1930), M.R.T.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1877; ed. Oxford Preparatory Schl., King's Schl., Canterbury, and Middlesex Hosp.; asst. med. offr., St. Mary's Infirmary, London, 1899 to 1903; asst. med. offr., British Guiana, 1905; asst. med. offr., Oxford County Asylum, 1906; med. offr., Nigeria Bitumen Co., 1908; govt. entomologist, S. Nigeria, 1913; entomologist, Imp. Bureau, 1914; temp. med. offr., Tanganyika, 1917; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1919; asst. med. entomologist, F.M.S., 1920; med. entomt., Nyasaland, 2nd Dec., 1921.
LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, creat. 1880).

SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1895).—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903-7.

LAMONT, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1885; vice-prin., Schl. of Agr., Grootfontein, Middelburg (Cape), dep. of agr., South Africa; prin., schl. of agr., Elsenburg, Mulders Vlei, 1920; asst. ch., divn. of agr. educn. and extension, 1925; ch., divn. of economics and markets, 1926; mem. agr. educn. comn., 1921 and fruit export enquiry comtee., 1924; chmn., wine exp. comn., 1927; ag. under-sec. for agr., 1927; seconded for sp. duty, expt. of Union tobacco, 1928.

LANCTOT, CHARLES, K.C.—B. 1864; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Laval Univ.; private tutor; called to the bar 1885; K.C., 1899; special law offr., Quebec, 1891; dep. atty.-gen., Quebec, 1905; mem. of coman. for revision of code of civil proceed., 1893-97; comsnr. for revision of statutes of Quebec, 1909-10; published "Traité théorique et pratique du droit criminel"; "Mannel du juge de paix"; "Annotated Criminal Code."

LANE, CHARLTON ADELBERT (STAVES).—B. 1890; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1911; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 1913; ag. dist. comsnr., 1918; asst., secretariat, 1918; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1919; senr. asst. sec., 1920; dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 1923; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; res. mag., Kenya, 1929.

LANG, ERNEST PHILIP HENRY.—Ed. Monmouth; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1905; pte. prac., Hong Kong, 1905-15; war serv., 1915-19 (capt.); asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, Sept., 1928; dep. regitr., sup. ct., Oct., 1928; ag. offr. recr. and regtr. trade marks and letters patent in aldtn., 1930; ag. regitr., offr. admstr. and regitr., companies, Jan., 1931.

LANG, HON. SIR FREDERIC WILLIAM, KT. R.A.C.H. (1916).—B. 1852; mem. of H. of R., New Zealand, 1893-1905 and 1906-22; chmn. of comtees., Aug., 1912 to 26th June, 1913; speaker from June, 1913 to 1922; M.L.C., 1921-31.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A. of New South Wales; treas. in Storey and Dooley govt.; premier, N.S.W., 1925-27 and 1930-32.

LANGLEY, CARLETON GEORGE.—B. 1885; ed. City of London Schl.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; on milly. serv., Malta, France and Ireland, 1914-21; pte. practice, 1921-30; atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1931; comsnr., sup. ct., J.P.

LANGLEY, EDWARD RALPH.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab); war service, E. Africa 1917-18; passed lower stand. exam. in Temne; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1914; ag. asst. col. sec. in 1916 and 1918; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., May to Oct., 1919; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1922.

LANGSLOW-COCK, E. A., M.Inst.M.M., M.I.MIN.—Ed. Marlborough and Truro Schl. of Mines; I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900; inspr. mines, Pahang, 1903; ditto, Negri Sembilan; ditto, Perak, 1908; inspr., mines, N. Nigeria, 1910; ditto, Nigeria, 1914; title changed to ch. inspr., mines, Nigeria, 1919.

LANKTREE, Charles Joseph Dane, Barrister-at-Law, King's Inn, Dublin.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-19; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1921; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1921; ag office astt. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1922; office astt. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1924; adm. sec. to dir. of med. and san. serv., Oct., 1925; addn. astt. dir., educ., Nov., 1926; adm. sec. to dir. elec. undertakings, July, 1928; astt. sttlmt. offr., Jan., 1932.

LANDOWSON, Charles William Henry, B.A., I.L.B.—Clk., C.G.R., Mar., 1889; clk., C.S.O., Capetown, Oct., 1893; atty.-gen.'s off., Nov., 1894; mag.'s clk., Aliwal N., Feb., 1898; pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Aug., 1898; ag. chf. clk. to sol.-gen., Grahamstown, May, 1901; ag. ast. mag., Port Elizabeth, Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Nov., 1901; addn. legal advdr., July, 1907; prof. astt., atty.-gen.'s off., Capetown, Apr., 1912; law advdr., dep. of justice Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Cape Prov., Dec., 1926; senr. law adviser, 1927; puisne judge, Natal prov. div., Oct., 1931.

LASCELLES, Sir Alfred George, Kt., Bach. (1913), B.A. Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; b. 1857; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. cts. in Cyprus and as puisne judge: pres. of dist. cts. Paphos, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1896; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. cts., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1898; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; chief just., May, 1911; retired, 1914.

LASSALLIE, Charles Francis, M.D., Edin., M.B.C.M. (Hons.), Edin., D.P.H., Oxford.—B. 1871; ed. Univ. of Edinburgh; postgraduate work at London schl. of trop. med., 1902; the Polyclinic, London, 1902; Univ. Coll., London, 1902; Lister inst. of Preventive Medicine, 1912; mem. of B.M.A.; mem. of coun. of med. bd., Trinidad and Tobago; apptd. govt. med. offr., Trinidad, Nov., 1894; port health offr. and med. insur. of immigrants, Trinidad, Jan., 1910; astt. med. offr., inspr. of health, Dec., 1910; has acted as astt. M.O.H., res. surg. at col. hosp., and also as D.M.O. at various centres; dep. surg. gen. and med. inspr. health, 1931; author of "Notes of lectures on Midwifery for Midwives," etc.

LAST, Joseph Spurrer.—B. 1897; on active serv., Egypt and Palestine, 1915-1917; astt. dist. comsrr., Zanzibar, Sept. 1919; ag. dist. comsrr., Pemba, Dec., 1926 to Apr., 1927; ag. astt. ch. sec., clk. to exec. and leg. couns. and edr., off. gazette, July to Sept., 1927; ag. astt. ch. sec., in 1928, 1929, 1930 and 1931; cls. IV, Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1929.

LATCHFORD, Hon. Francis Robert, B.A., K.C.—B. 1856; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; elec. to Ontario legis., 1899; re-elec., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, chancery divn., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908; ch. just., 2nd divn., sup. ct., 1923.
