Galle kach., Aug., 1919; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1922; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Oct., 1924; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1925; commr. requests, Kandy, Oct., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1931.

POUSSETTE, HENRY RIVINGTON.—B. 1872; ed. Sarnia Pub. Sch., Upper Can. Coll., Royal Mily, Coll., Kingston, and Sch. of Prac. Science, Toronto; capt., S. African Constab., 1901, and thereafter in same corps throughout S. African serv.; dist. comdr., and pub. pros., S. Africa; ent. serv. dept. of trade and commerce, Can., as trade comrn., Durban, S. Africa, 1909; trade comrn., Buenos Aires, 1910; enlisted for active serv.; returned, Can., 1919; inspr., trade comrn. serv.; sp. trade comrn., India, 1919; dir., comm. intelligence serv., Ottawa, 1921; trade comrn., Trinidad, 1924.
POWERS, HON. SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1929).—Justice of the high ct. of Australia, 1913; ret.

POYNTON, ARTHUR HILTON.—B. 1905; schol. of Marlborough Coll. (1918-23) and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (1923-7); 1st cls. honos., moda., 1925; 2nd cls. honos., lit. hum., 1927; M.A., 1933; apptd. asst. prin., dept. of scientific and industrial research after compet. exam., 31st Oct., 1927; transf. to C.O., 1st Oct., 1929; joint sec. Col. Development Advisory Comtee., 1st Apr. to 30th Sept., 1930; sec., do., 1st April, 1932; pte. sec. to partly. under sec. for cols. (Sir R. Hamilton), 18th April, 1932 and to (Lord Plymouth), 30th Sept. 1932.

POYSER, KENNETH ELLISTON, D.S.O., K.C. (Barbados).—B. 1882; ed. Shrewsbury and Merton Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1906; served, European War, 1914-20; Yorks. L.I., and Loyal N. Lanc. Regt. (deeps. thrice, D.S.O., 1917); puisne judge, Leeward Islands, Sept., 1920; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1925; atty.-gen., Uganda, 1928.

PRAIN, LT.-COL. SIR DAVID, KT. BACH. (1912); C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S.E., F.L.S., F.R.S.—B. 1867; ed. Aberdeen gram.schl. and Univs. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3, and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-1884; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1898; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1895-1905; director, botanical survey of India, and supt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1905; trustee of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1905; ret., 1922.

PRANKERD, HORACE ARTHUR, B.A.(Cantab.).—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class. tripes), B.A., 1905; baccalaureat (B.L. 1st part) Lyons Univ., France, 1908; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. res., 1911; ag. supt., schla., July, 1912 to Apr., 1913; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Apr., 1921; promoted cls. I., grade I., adm. serv., Feb., 1928.

PRASAD, MUBARI, U.B.—B. 1888; Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; addtl. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914; addtl. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Dec., 1915; pol. mag., Chilaw, Apr., 1917; ditto, Panadure, Apl., 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, May, 1921; pol. mag., Kalutara, May, 1922; ditto, Gampola, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1924; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, Matara, June, 1927; comsnr., stamps, July, 1932.

PRENDERGAST, HON. JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for La Verendrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1885; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puisne judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910; oh. just., Dec., 1929.
PRESCOTT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Apptd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; asst. regar. and deputy clk. of arraigna, 1911.

PRETORIUS, Jan Lodewyk.—B. 1878; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; joined O.F.S. repub. civ. serv., Mar., 1887; asst. mag., Edenburg, 1902; Vrede, 1904; Lindley, 1908; dist. asst. mag., Dewetsdorp, Feb., 1910 to Oct., 1913; Reitz, Nov., 1913; mag., Reitz, 1918; Hoopstad, Feb., 1919; Boshof, Oct., 1919; Frankfort, Oct., 1923; Senekal, June, 1929; Vereeniging, Feb., 1930.

PRETTY, ERIC ERNEST FALK.—B. 1891; ed. Harrow, and Demy of Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ag. collr. of land revenue, Johore Bahru, Sept., 1917; offr., cls. V., S.S., Dec., 1917; 2nd lieut. and asst. adlt., J.V.R., May, 1918; supermy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; Br. rea., Brunei, Mar., 1923; offr., Cls. III., Nov., 1926; dist. offr., Krian, June, 1928; ag. sec., high comsnr., July, 1931; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931.

PRICE, CHARLES ROY.—B. 1893; ed. Wellington Schl., Somerset and University Coll., London; B.A. (war), 1919; unattached list, Territorial Force, Jan. to Aug., 1915; Royal Garrison Arty., Sept., 1915 to Dec., 1918; served in France, June, 1916 to Sept., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for dom. affrs., 6th July, 1927 (ag. from 6th June, 1927); temp. ag. prin., D.O., 22nd July, 1927; pvt. sec. to parly. under sec. for dom. affrs., Jan., 1928; asst. sec., Imp. wireless and cable confce., Jan.-July, 1928; ag. prin., D.O., 15th Sept., 1928; U.K. delegn. to L. of N. assembly, 1928 and 1932; U.K. delegn. to London naval confce., 1930; attd. to sec., Imp. confce., 1930; U.K. delegn., disarmament confce., Geneva, Feb., Dec., 1932.

PRICE, HERBERT HENRY.—2nd clk. to asst. comsnr., Northern Div., Bech. Prot., 1910; 2nd clk., res. comsnr.'s office, 1st May, 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1910; ag. asst. res. mag., 1911-12; regar. of brands, 1913; ch. clk. and regar., 1921; passed 3rd grade Seawana exam., 1927.
PRICE, STANLEY LEWIS, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Lampeter Coll., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18.

PRICK-JONES, CAPT. WILLIAM.—Served during Great War in France; awarded M.C., 1917; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1919; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1924; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1926; pol. mag., 1928.

PRICHARD, HERBERT WILLIAM, K.C. (1923)—B. 1873; ed. at Keble Coll., Oxford (open class. scholar, 1892); called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1902; practised in London, 1902-1910; stip. mag., Trinidad, Aug., 1910; puisne judge, Mauritius, Feb., 1915; 1st puisne judge, Oct., 1916; ag. procureur and advoc.-gen., Oct., 1916 to July, 1917; chrmn., sugar sales comte., Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1920 and Feb. to Apr., 1921; ag. procureur and advoc.-gen., April, 1921; confirmed in appt., Jan., 1922; ag. col. sec. from Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1926; puisne judge, S. Stiltsms., June, 1927.

PRING, HARRY.—B. 1883; ed. Watford Schl. and King's Coll., London; imp. serv., 1898-1903; col. sec.'s office, Capetown, 1903; dept. of interior, Pretoria, 1910; ch. clk., 1922; under sec., 1927; pub. serv. comsnr., 1931.

PRIOR, A. C. VINCENT.—B. 1881; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1906; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Jan., 1915; ag. oh. just., Jan. to June, 1915; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 1918; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Gold Coast, 1925; ag. atty.-gen., May to Nov., 1926; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1927; ag. ch. just., June to July, 1928 and June to Oct., 1929; atty.-gen., Nigeria, Oct., 1929.

PRITCHETT, CECIL JOHN SUCKLING, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civil serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Trincomalee, July, 1913; ag. asst. settlmt. offr., Nov., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1914; asst. collr. of customs and landing survr., Jaffna, Nov., 1914; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Feb., 1916; pol. mag., Avissawella, Oct., 1916; ditto, Panadure, Dec., 1917; ditto, Chilaw, Apl., 1918; ag. ditto, Galic, Sept., 1919; asst. land stlitmt. offr., Nov., 1921; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Feb., 1922; dep. rubber contr., Nov., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Nov., 1928; dist. judge, Jan., 1932.

PROBYN, SIR LESLIE, K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in France and Germany, called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The Jurisdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," and other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1893; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. col. sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; gov. secretary, S. Nigeria, Apr., 1901; gov., Sierra Leone, July, 1904; gov., Barbados, Nov., 1910; gov., Jamaica, May, 1918; ret., 1924.
PROBSTING, HON. W. B., C.M.G. (1918).—M.L.C., Tasmania; atty.-gen. and min. for rly., Apr., 1916; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

PROUT, SIR WILLIAM THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1928), KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1905), O.B.E., M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—Asst. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 1885; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 1887; asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ag. chief mag., Gambia, 1893; med. offr. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1896; title altered to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; fellow of roy. med. soc., Edin., and correspndg. mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect., schl. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. adviser to C.O.; mem. of advisory, med. and sanitary comttee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; senr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; author of article "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm Climates" (Davidson); Jnl. Voloulois (Archives de Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. Leone" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other papers.

PRYER, JOHN HAWKES.—B. 1894; ed. Bedford Harpur Trust; mech. transport, B.E.F., France, 1918-19; cadet pilot, R.A.F.; asst. storekeeper, Gold Coast rly., Sept., 1920; asst. ch., do., Aug., 1929; ch., do., Mar., 1932.

PUCRIDGE, GEOFFREY MARTIN.—B. 1895; ed. Exeter Schl.; 2nd lieut., Devon Regt., T.F., Dec., 1913; capt., Dec., 1914; served in France with 5th West Yorks regt.; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, July, 1917; seconded to Inter Allied Aeronautical Comn. of Control, Germany, 1920; re-signed comn., Sept., 1921; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comsnr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1928.

PUDSEY, FAWCOTT, —B. 1882; Assoo. M.I.M.E.; Assoo. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering asst., dock engr.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hassle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916; D.P.W., Palestine, 1927.

PUGH, S. R., M.B.E. (1928).—B. 1877; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904; conf. elk., Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, 1911; staff elk., 1st grade, 1st Mar., 1916; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1922; on service in Sierra Leone, Oct., 1926 to Feb., 1927; sec., Discovery comttee., Jan.-June, 1928.

PURCELL, SIR GILBERT KENHOLM TREFFRY, KT. BACH. (1916).—Ed. Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1888; M.A. 1893; called to bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1890; mem. of western circ., also practised at cent. crim. ct.; apptd. asst. to atty.-gen. Leeward Islds. (St. Kitts), Mar., 1898; official mem. of exec. and legis. couns. of the presidency of St. Christopher and Nevis; ag. atty.-gen. Leewards, Aug.-Oct., 1900; apptd. ag. 1st puisne judge, June, 1901; puisne judge, G. Coast, 11th June, 1901; chief justice, Sierra Leone, 1911.

PURCELL, HUBERT KENNEDY.—B. 1884; ent. Crown Agents office, Oct., 1901; asst. cashier, July, 1914; head of pay dept., Dec., 1920; dep. head of pay and checking dept., Nov., 1921; dep. head, pay dept., 1924; dep. head, gen. dept. and dep. ch. olk., 1930.

PURILL, FREDERICK JOHN.—B. 1891; conf. shorthand writer, B. of T., Aug., 1909; conf. elk. to gen. man., lab. exchange dept., B. of T., Oct., 1909; conf. shorthand writer to S. of S., H.O., Jan., 1911; cler. offr., 21st Jan., 1925; accompanied the Rt. Hon. L. S. Amery, M.P. (S. of S. for Dominion Affairs) on his tour of the Dominions visiting S. Africa, S. Rhodesia, Australia, New Zealand, Fiji and Canada, Aug., 1927-Feb., 1928; transferred to C.O., June, 1928; accompanied Br. economic mission to Australia as offr. shorthand writer, Feb., 1929.

PUTTICK, LEONARD JAMES.—B. 1885; ed. at High Schl. and Huguenot Coll., Wellington and S. African Coll., Cape Town; served Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; draughtsman, Swaziland, 1910; elk. of lands, 1916; ch. elk., 1919; ag. regisr. and mast., sp. ct. and sheriff, 1919; sec., Swaziland-Portuguese bdry. comn., 1922; sec., undeveloped minerals comn., 1927; ag. dept. asst. comn., 1925 and 1930; beacon inspr., 1927; ag. govt. sec. in 1927 and 1928; asst. govt. sec., 1928; regisr. and mast., sp. ct., sheriff, regist., births, marriages and deaths and comn., protocols, 1932.

QUIGLEY, EUGENE PATRICK, M.C.—B. 1889; served six years R.I.C.; joined army, 1914; 2nd lieut., R. Dublin Fusiliers, 1915; served in France, Egypt and Palestine; organised pol., C.I.D., Palestine, 1919; D.C.P., grade I, 1921; dist. asupt., pol., Apr., 1926; dep. comdt., C.I.D., 1930.

RAE, SIR JAMES STANLEY, KT. BACH. (1933)—B. 1881; articled and conf. elk. to Sir W. R. Davies, Kt., when atty.-gen., Bahamas; called to the bar, Bahamas, Apr., 1904; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; ag. coroner for the Is. of New Providence from 1904, confirmed in 1911; J.P., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., Jan., 1910; ag. stip. and circ. mag., 1910-11, and on other occasions; dist. comsnr., Belize, B. Honduras, 1914; off. recr. in bankruptcy, off. admstr. and J.P. for the col., 1914; regisr.-gen., Nov., 1915, in conjunction with duties as dist. comsnr.; dir. of P.O.G. fund, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Sept., 1916; press censor during war; pvt., B. Hond. Territorial Force; addl. to staff as mily. censor; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Apl., 1919; cross of Chevalier of the Order of the Crown, Belgium, for relief services during war, May, 1919; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, June to Oct., 1919; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, Sept., 1920; ag. ch. just., June, 1921 to Feb., 1922 and from Sept., 1922 to June, 1923; ch. just., St. Vincent, June, 1923; ag. admstr., June, 1926 to Feb., 1927; ch. just., Grenada, 1927; revised and consolidated laws, 1926; ch. just., Leeward Is., Apr., 1931.
RAE, NORMAN.—B. 1892; entd. Crown Agent's office, May, 1911; asst. head of dept., May, 1919; dep. head, stores dept., Oct., 1932.

RAEBURN, COLIN, D.Sc., F.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; lient. Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; Salonika, 1915-18; geologist, Scottish Spitzbergen syndicate, 1919; geologist, geol. survey, Nigeria, 1920-29; ast. dir., 1929.

RAFFLES, MAJOR STAMFORD CRUZ, O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919.—B. 1885; ed. All Saints Sch., Bloxham, Royal Naval Academy, Gosport, and in Germany; 2nd lieut., 4th Suffolk regt., 1903; lieut., 1903; transfd., K.S.L.I., 1906; resig. coman., 1908; supt., chandu monopoly, Perak, 1913; seconded for mily. serv., Jan., 1915; lieut., 3rd R.W.F., Mar., 1915; capt., Sept., 1915; staff offr., 3rd cls., R.F.C., 1917; staff offr., 2nd cls., R.A.F., 1918; tempy. major, 1918; transfd., unemployed list, R.A.F., Apr., 1919; resumed duty as supt., chandu monopoly, Perak, and supervisor, cust., Kinta, Dec., 1919; supervisor, cust., Selangor, Dec., 1920; dep. comsmr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Jan., 1921; cls. Ia, 1922; mem., joint F.M.S. and S.S. comte., to draw up a scheme for formn. of R.N.V.R. in Malaya, Apr., 1926; dep. comsmr., excise, F.M.S. in addn., Apr.-July, 1929; dep. comsmr., trade and cust., Selangor and Pahang, June, 1931.

RAIKES, H. R.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl., Dulwich Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (Williams exhib.), Abbot Schol., 1911, final hon. schl. of nat. science (chemy.), class I, 1914; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1919; served European War, 1st Batt. The Buffs, R.F.C. and R.A.F., 1914-1919 (A.F.C.); fellow, Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1919-27; sub rector, 1924-27; demonstr. and treas., Balliol and Trinity Coll. laboratory, 1920-27; ch. instr., Oxford Univ. Air Squadron, 1925-27: wing commander, R.A.F.O. since 1926; prin., Univ. of Witwatersrand, Johannesburg, 1928; publs.—papers in Jnl.s. of Chem. Soc'y and Faraday Soc'y.
RAINSFORD, HUME FREDERICK.—B. 1902; ed. Dublin Univ.; B.A. and B.A.I. hons., civ. engnr., 1925; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

RAINSFORD, CAPT. RICHARD FREDERICK.—B. 1888; Natal pol., 1904-07, served Zulu Rebellion, 1906, (med.); B.S.A.P., 1907-08; inspr. E.A. Pol., 1910; asst. supt., 1914; commanded No. 1 Column, Turkhank Punitive Expdn., 1915 (deeps., A.G.S. med.) ; capt., 5th K.A.R., 1916; adjt. and staff offr. to O.C. troops, Jubaland, 1917-19 (deeps.); "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory med.; supdt., Kenya pol., 1923.

RAMBAUT, ARTHUR EDMUND, B.A.—B.1885; ed. Magd. Coll. Schl. and B.N.C., Oxford; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1905; 4th cls. bot., 1908; dipl. forestry, 1909; asst. con., forests, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; on mil. duty; 2nd lieut. North. Fus. (T.F.), 1915; lieut., 1916; temp. capt. 1916; served in France; intell. corps., 1917 (France, Belgium, Germany); rtnd. to F.M.S., Aug., 1919; dep. con., forests, Perak, South, Nov., 1926; ag. per. asst. to con., forests, Dec., 1927 and Aug., 1928; comsmr., forests, Jan., 1930.

RANASINHA, ARTHUR GODWIN, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; attd. to Kegalla Rath., Mar., 1921; attd., Jaffna kach., Oct., 1922; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Mar., 1923; ditto, Balapitiya, Nov., 1923; ditto, Jaffna, June, 1926; dist. judge Avisawella, Apr., 1928; ditto, Badulla, Jan., 1930.

RANKINE, SIR RICHARD SIMS DONKIN, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1875; clk. treasury, Fiji, Feb., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir George O'Brien, Sept., 1897 to 1901; acted clk. to ex. coun. Sept., 1899 to Mar., 1900; apptd. acct. native taxes, treas., 1901; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson, July, 1902; priv. sec. to Sir E. im Thurn, Oct., 1904; conf. sec., Jan., 1905; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific; elk. of exec. coun.; ag. sec., W. Pacific high comsmr., June, 1905 to June, 1906, and from Nov., 1907 to Dec., 1908; asst. col. sec. and astt. recvr.-gen. Jan., 1909; recvr.-gen. and comsmr. of stamps, pres. of marine bd., mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1910; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1913 to Nov., 1914; gov'r's dep., in June, 1914; comsmr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. act.-gen. of immigrn. (conjoint), Mar., 1915; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1915 to May, 1916; gov'r's deputy, Aug., Oct., Dec., 1915; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1917-19; on sp. mission to India as personal rep. of the gov'r. in connection with emigrn. of Indians to Fiji, Oct., 1919 to June, 1920; ch. sec., Nyasaland Prot., July, 1920; ag. gov', on various occasions, 1921-24 and in 1926; ch. sec., Uganda, Aug., 1927; admstd. govt., June to Oct., 1928; Br. res., Zanzibar, 1929.

RANSOM, HON. ETHELBERT ALFRED.—B. 1868; ed. lower Hutt state schl., N.Z.; mem., N.Z., H. of R. since 1922; min. of pub. wks., 1928-30; min. of lands and comsmr., state forests, 1930; min., same offices, coalition min'y., 1931.

RANSOM, HERBERT CHARLES, O.B.E. (1923), M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1881; entd. crown ag't's off., May, 1901; asst. cashier, Mar., 1914; ag. head of appts. dept. and asst. sec., Jan., 1917; head of miscellaneous dept., Apr., 1919; dep. head, gen. dept., 14th Nov., 1921; sec., Dead Sea mineral reses comte., 1923; sec., Palestine currency bd., 1926-30; ch. clk. and head, gen. dept., Feb., 1930.
RANSOME, JOHN EDWARD GROFFREY.—B. 1891; ed. Bradfield Coll.; 3rd K.A.R., Oct., 1914-Mar., 1923; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1925.

RAWSON, WALTER EDWARD.—B. 1870; ed. New Plymouth High Schl., N.Z.: barrister and solr.; judge, native land ct. and native appellate ct., N.Z., 1906; pres., Maori land bd., 1910; native trustee and east coast trust comsmr., 1921.

RAYMAN, LAZARUS.—B. 1889; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; Brit. adviser's office, Kedah; asst. dist. offr., Pekan, Pahang, 1914-16; asst. dist. offr., Bentong, Pahang, on various occasions, 1916-19; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1919-21; on sp. duty as collr. of stamp duty, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; collr., estate duty, F.M.S., Oct., 1926; 2nd asst. sec. to gov't., Jan., 1927; ag. comsmr., lands and mines, Trengganu, Dec., 1929; cls. II, Nov., 1930; ag. finan. comsmr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Dec., 1930.

RAYMOND, LEONARD WILLIAM.—B. 1892; ed. Horwell Gr. Schl. and Dunheved Coll., Launceston; Royal Coll. Sci.; B.A. (Lond.), B.Sc. (Lond. 1912, hons. 1915), A.R.C.S. (1915), A.I.C. (1918); chem. offr., R.E., 1916; chem.-bactgts. with forces, Mesopotamia, 1917-20; asst. gov't. chem., agr. dept., Zanzibar, 1926; ag. gov't. chem., 1926; ag. asst. dir., agr., on various occasions, 1926-32; del., Imp. agr. conce., 1927.

RAYNE, MAJOR H., O.B.E. (1920), M.B.E. (Mily.)—B. and ed. N.Z.; N.Z. Contingents and R.F.A. in Anglo-Boer War, 1900-02; E. African pol., June, 1903; transport and polit. offr., Nandi and Sotik Expedns. (ment. in depts.), 1905-06; K.A.R., Sept., 1914 to Apr., 1919; asst. dist. comsmr., Br. E. Africa, Dec., 1914; seconded for mily. serv., German E. Africa, Jubaland and Turkana; offr. commdg. troops and administering Turkana, 1918 (ment. in depts.), M.C.; dist. comsmr., Somaliland, Apr., 1919; senr. supply and transport offr., in charge organisation of Somaliland expdn., 1919-20 (ment. in depts.); dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.
READ, ALEXANDER LLEWELLYN.—B. 1877; ent. pub. serv., S. Australia, 1891; ch. sec.'s office, 1920; under sec., sec. to premier and clk., exec. coun., June, 1931.

READ, SIR HERBERT JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B. (1914), C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1863; M.A., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; Ist class math. mods., and 1st class math. finals; clk., higher div., W.O., Feb., 1887; 2nd class clk., C.O., Jan., 1889; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Mr. (afterwards Sir E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; lst cls. clk., Oct., 1898; principal clk., Apr., 1905; British delegate at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1899; one of the Brit. delegates on boundary comn. at Berlin for the settm. of Anglo-German frontier in E. Africa, 1906; one of the Brit. plenipotentiaries at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comttee. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn. of deptmtl. comttee. to enquire into W. African med. staff, 1909; visited E. Africa, 1911-12; sen. mem., C.O. delegation at Paris Peace Confce., 1919; asst. under-sec. of state, March, 1916; regiar. of the Order of Saint Michael and Saint George; gov., Mauritius, 1924 to 1930; hon. mem., managing comttee., Imp. Inst. of Entomology, 1931; hon. mem., managing comttee., Imp. Mycological Inst., 1932.

REAKES, CHARLES JOHN, C.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.V.S., Eng., Doctor of Vet. Science, Univ. of Melbourne.—B. 1885; vet. offr., N.Z. dept. of agr., 1897; asst. ch. vet., 1901; dir., live-stock divn., and dir. vet. services and remounts, N.Z. Defence Forces with rank of lieut.-col. (since col.), 1909; dir.-gen. agr. dept., 1918.
REAY, CAPT. GEORGE HENRY NORL, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1889; asst. teleg. engr., F.M.S., Jan., 1914; war ser., 1915-19; elec. mpr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; elec. engr., P.W.D., Singapore, July, 1922.

REDELINGHUYS, JOHANNES NICOLAS.—B. 1886; ed. Boys' High Sch., Wellington, C.P.; post and tel. asst., 1901; transf. to sec.'s office, dept. of posts and tels., Cape Town, 1909; senr. clk., 1920; ch. clk., 1924; asst. under sec., 1926; under sec. and asst. P.M.G., 1927.

RECE, C. MANSEL.—B. 1894; ed. Rossall Sch., and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1921; prte. practice, Shanghai, 1921-26; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1927; ag. puisne judge, Apr.-May, 1929; crown coun., Uganda, 1930; ag. solr.-gen., Aug. to Feb., 1931 and May to Dec., 1931.
RECE, GERALD.—B. 1897; ed. Rugby Sch.; served in France with Sherwood Foresters, 1915-18; solr., sup. ct., England, 1921; adm. cadet, Kenya, 1925; dist. offr., 1927.

REED, E. A.—B. 1894; on milly. serv., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1918; temp. paper keeper, C.O. lib., 22nd Nov., 1920-15th Aug., 1924; estab. paper keeper (library attendant) after qual. exam., 16th Aug., 1924; cler. offr., 31st Oct., 1928.

REED, HATHORNE.—B. 1873; M.A., Cantab.; ed. Bath Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; Western Circuit; lieut., S. African Constabulary, 1902-1908; 2nd mag., Zanzibar, Dec., 1908; 1st mag., Dec., 1910; ag. asst. judge, in 1910 and 1915-19; asst. judge, Sept., 1920; ag. chief judge and judge of prize ct., in 1919 and 1931-24; junr. puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, May, 1925; judge, high ct., Nyasaland, 1927; author of "A Commentary on the Moneylenders' Act, 1900," and "A Commentary on the Police Offences Ordinance, 1902, of the Orange River Colony."

REED, HON. JOHN RANKEN, C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Gram. Sch., Auckland, N.Z., Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1921; pres., pri-ons bd., 1928; is judge advoc.-gen., N.Z. Defence Forces, with rank of colonel.

REES, LEWIS CHARLES MORRIS PARKER.—B. 1876; ed. Kingston Coll., Yeovil and Merchant Venturers Tech. Coll., Bristol; art. pupil to Messrs. Foster and La Trobe, archs. and survs., Bristol, 1892; asst. to boro. engrnr., Islington, 1897; ch. asst. borough engrnr., Margate, 1900; priv. land surveyor, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1902; salaried, squatters' bd., 1905; aspt., crown lands and survs., 1922.

REES-DAVIES, SIR WM., KT. BACH. (1913).—B. 1863; ed. Eton and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1888; called to bar (Inner Temp.), 1887; went South Wales circuit; J.P. and dep. lieut. Pembrokeshire; J.P. for Haverfordwest; M.P. for Pembrokeshire, 1892-8; priv. sec. to the late Rt. Hon. Sir W. V. Harcourt when chancellor of exchequer, 1893-5, and afterwards from 1895-8; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1897; administered the govt., 1901; ag. ch. just., 1902; King's advocate, Cyprus, Oct., 1902; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1906; K.C., 1908; ag. ch. just., 1909-10; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1912; chmn. of gen. mily. serv. tribunal, 1918; chmn., pub. serv. comn., Hong Kong, 1919; chmn., exec. comttee. on the visit of H.R.H. The Prince of Wales, 1922; ret., 1924.

REGNARD, MAJOR LOUIS FERDINAND.—B. 1888; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology and McGill Univ. (B.Sc.); 2nd lieut., R.E., Nov., 1914; war. serv., France, Belgium and Palestine, 1914-20; major, R.E., 1918-20; regular army R. O. of asst. D.P.W. and surveys, Mauritius, 1921; D.P.W., 1926; M.L.C.
REID, C. B. B., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—Capt., R.A.M.C., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.
REID, FREDERICK MURRAY.—B. 1875; inspr., schls., Basutoland, Oct., 1914; senr. inspr., ashl., 1927.
REID, HAROLD WALLACE, B.Sc.—B. 1897; dep. inspr., mines, Trinidad, Sept., 1927.

REILLY, ROBERT O'MALLEY.—Sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. police, 1904; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; ag. asst. res. mag., 1912; inspr., Apr., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914; res. mag., 1921; passed 3rd grade Seswana exam., 1922; asst. res. comntr., Oct., 1931; ag. res. comntr., April to Oct., 1932.
REIMERS, EDMUND.—B. 1881; archivist and librarian, Ceylon, Oct., 1921.
REJAS, FELIX.—B. 1856; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, Feb., 1887; warden, July, 1912.

RENNIE, GILBERT MCCALL, M.C., M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1895; on milly. serv., July, 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Anuradhapura Kachcheri, June, 1920; attd. to N'Ella Kachcheri, Nov., 1920; office asst. to govt. agt. E. Prov., Nov., 1921; pol. mag., Dambagamuwa, Dec., 1923; ditto, Chilaw, Mar., 1924; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Dec., 1923; office asst. to dir., med. and sanry. services, Apr., 1926; sec., min. for health, July, 1931.

RENTON, SIR ALEXANDER WOOD, G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1925), KT. BACH. (1915); M.A., LL.B. (honours), Edin., K.C.—Forensic prizeman; Lord Rector's essay prizeman; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1882; bencher, Gray's Inn, 1917; b. 1861; called to the bar, 1886; puisne judge, Mauritius, Aug., 1901; chmn., local comttee. of soc. of comp. legis., 1902; chmn. of mountain reserves comn., 1903-4; ag. procureur and advocato-gen., Aug., 1904; puisne judge, Ceylon, Aug., 1905; received thanks of S. of S. for drafting rules of sup. ct. of Mauritius, 1904; mem. of comttee. apptd. in 1912 to prepare new code of civil procedure, Ceylon; ag. chief ust., Ceylon, May, 1913; chief just., Ceylon, Aug., 1914; chmn., police inquiry comsn., Sept., 1915; chmn. of coman. of inquiry into certain cases of shooting during Ceylon riots, 1915; ret., 25th July, 1918; on special mission to Nigeria and G. Coast as to war bonus, Sept., 1918 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); went to Egypt at request of F.O., Feb., 1919, and prepared draft Code of Criminal Procedure for Unified Tribunals; chmn., comsn. of enquiry, Upper Egypt train murders; vice-pres., riots indemnities coman., Oct., 1919; chmn., Ceylon salaries coman., June, 1921; chmn., compensation (Ireland) coman., Feb., 1923; K.C., 12th Oct., 1923; author of "Wood Renton on Lunacy"; joint author of first edition of "Edmunds on Patents"; editor of Law Journal; editor of "Encyclopedia of the Laws of England"; editor of "The English Reports"; joint editor of 2nd edition of "Burge on Colonial and Foreign Laws."

RENWICK, HENRY LANGDON.—B. 1898; London Rifle Brig., Apr., 1916; attd. gen. staff, W.O., 1919; asst. clk. to H.M.T.C. in E. Africa, Dec., 1919; asst. imigrn. and embarkation offr., Mombasa, July, 1920; asst. cust. dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1920; imigrn., offr., Aug., 1921; ag. asst. compt., cust., for various periods, 1923-32; ag. asst. treas., May, 1932; aviation control offr., Aug., 1932.
RETIEF, D. J., B.A.—B. 1882; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; prin., Boshof, 1913; prin., Winburg, 1921; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1927.

REX, MARCUS.—B. 1886; ed. Highgate and Trin. Coll., Camb. (exhib.), B.A., 1908; cadet, F.M.S., 1910; asst. sec. to res., Perak, 1912 and 1915; asst. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, 1913; supk., chandu monopoly, Sel., N.S. and Pahang, 1917; dist. offr., Raub, 1919; mag., K. Lumpur, June, 1921, sec., retrenchment coman., in addition, Feb., 1922; dep. contr. of rubber expts., in addition, Oct., 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; ag. finan. advr., Johore, 1924; ag. finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Nov., 1927; finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

REY, CHARLES FERNAND, C.M.G. (1932)—B. 1877; W. Africa, 1900; joined B. of T. 1900; sec. to China Tariff coman., 1902; to Imp. Inst. Advisory Comte. and Imp. Inst. Trustees, 1903; to All Red Route Comtes., 1904; Roumanian Treaty Negotiations, 1905; mission to Constantinople and Sofia, 1906; Swiss Treaty negotiations, 1906; gen. man., lab. exchange and unemployment insurance, 1909; asst. gen. sec., min. of munitions 1916; dir., employment dept., B. of T., 1917; dir.-gen., national lab. supply, miny. of national service, 1918; chmn. and Br. rep., Inter-Allied coman., Rotterdam, for food supply to Germany; seconded for serv. in Abyssinia, 1919; further journeys to Abyssinia, 1922-3, 1925-6, 1926-7; unemployment grants comitee, 1920-29; asst. res. coman., Bechuanaland Prot., Oct., 1929; res. coman., June, 1930; F.R.G.S., commdr., Order of the Star of Ethiopia; author of "Unconquered Abyssinia as it is today," 1923; "In the country of the Blue Nile," 1927; "The Romance of the Portuguese in Abyssinia," 1929; "The Lady or the Leopard," 1929; contributions to the Royal Geographical Jnl. and the Jnl. of the African Society.
REYNOLDS, A. E.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1900; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918.
RHODES, ALEX LEOPOLD.—B. 1884; gen. foreman, govt. printing and stationery dept., Trinidad 1921; typography instr., bd. of indus. training, 1922; mem., bd. indus. training, 1923; govt. printer, 1925.

RHODES, HON. E. N., P.C. (Can.), K.C., B.A., LL.B., D.C.L.—B. 1877; ed. Amherst Acad., Acadia Univ. and Dalhousie Univ.; apptd. K.C., Nova Scotia, May, 1916; 1st el. to H. of C. Canada at g.e., 1908; el. dep. speaker, H. of C., Feb., 1916; speaker, 1917; re-el. Mar., 1918; el. to legis., N.S., 1925; prov. premier and prov. sec., 1925; re-el. at g.e., 1928; joined Dominion cabinet of Bennett adminstr., as min. of fisheries, Aug. 1930.

RHODES, HON. SIR ROBERT HEATON, M.L.C. (1925), K.C.V.O. (1927), K.B.E. (1920).—B. 1861; B.A. Oxford; entd. New Zealand parlmt., 1899; served in S. African war (Queen's medal and 4 clasps); lt.-col., 1st Regt., Canterbury Rifles Yeomanry Cavalry; postm-r.-gen., min. of telegraphs, min. of pub. health, 1912-1915; min. of defence, 1920-26; comsnr., state forests, 1922-26; leader of leg. coun. and mem., exec. coun. without portfolio, 1926; dep. leader, leg. coun., 1927; ret. from office, 1928.

RHODES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Oxon.).—B. 1884; ed. Bromsgrove Schol., Cambridge Univ. and St. Thomas' Hosp.; sen. govt. pathologist, Cape Town, and offr.-in-charge, govt. vaccine inst., Cape Town.
RICE, HARRY PATRICK.—B. 1888; S.A. constab., 1906-15; K.A.R., 1916-17; asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1919; supt., 1926; dep. commdt., pol. (C.I.D.), Palestine, 1932.
RICH, HON. SIR GEO. EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1932).—Senr. puiane just. of the high court of Australia, Apr., 1913.
RICHARDS, ARTHUR COURTNEY.—B. 1896; 2nd asst. govt. printer, Ceylon, Sept., 1920; asst. govt. printer, Apr., 1923; govt. printer, Apr., 1932.

RICHARDS, ARTHUR FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1933), B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1886; ed. Clifton Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; offr., Cls. V., May, 1913; asst. dist. offr., Krian, June, 1913; mag. cent. ct., Kota Bharu, and state audr., Aug., 1915; adviser, land office, Kedah, and ag. supt. of mines, June, 1916; offr., Cls. IV., Jan. 1918; offr., Cls. III., Nov., 1920; 1st asst. col. sec., S. Stiltns., and ag. olk. of couns. in addn., Apr., 1921; gen. sec., S. S. retrenchment comitee., in addn., Jan., 1922; on sp. duty, London, May, 1923; sec., opium comitee., Br. Malaya in addn., Nov., 1923; chmn., exec. comitee., Br. Malaya, Br. Empire Exhibn., in addn., 1924; under-sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1927; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Nov., 1929; gov., N. Borneo, Mar., 1930.

RICHARDS, DANIEL.—B. 1886; ed. Foley Coll., Londonderry and Queen's Univ., Belfast; B.A., 1908; cadet, F.M.S., 1910; asst. contr., lab., 1915; mag., Ipoh, Nov., 1915; asst. contr., lab., Dec., 1915; collr., war tax, Singapore, 1918; collr., war tax, Singapore, Oct., 1918; prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, 1922; prot., Chinese, Kedah, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Larut and ag. supt., convict estab. and vagrant ward, Taiping, and inspr., prisons, F.M.S., in addn., Dec., 1928; offr., cls. Ib., Nov., 1930; asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, June, 1932.
RICHARDS, HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, LL.D.—Crown sol., S. Australia, 1916; formerly parliamentary draftsman; ag. judge, sup. ct. 1926; judge, ditto, 1927.
RICHARDS, P. E.—B. 1901; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 26th Dec., 1928.

RICHARDSON, ALEXANDER STEWART, B.Sc. (Agr.).—B. 1897; ed. Burgh and County High Sch., Peebles; mily. serv., R. Arty., 1916-19; B.Soc., Edinburgh Univ., 1923; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1924; senr. agrl. offr., 1930.

RICHARDSON, CECILY.—B. 1889; ed. The Laurels, Rugby and Royal Holloway Coll.; B.A., London, 1912; asst. mistress, govt. girls' schl., Penang, 1914; asst. mistress, Raffles girls' schl., Singapore, 1915; ag. prin., 1920 and 1924; prin., St. George's girl's schl., Penang, Sept., 1928.

RICHARDSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR GEORGE SPAFFORD, K.B.E. (1925), C.B. (1917), C.M.G. (1915), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1867; enlisted in Royal Artillery, Aug., 1886; attached to N.Z. Forces as instructor in gunnery with rank of master gunner, Aug., 1891; capt., N.Z. militia and dir. of artillery services (N.Z.), Apr., 1907; transf'd. to N.Z. staff corps, Jan., 1911; major, 1912; lieut.-col., 1915; col., 1919, and brig.-gen. with seniority as from Dec., 1915; N.Z. representative at War Office, London, and attached Impl. gen. staff, Dec., 1913; attached to Admiralty, staff, Aug., 1914; attached Royal Naval Divn. as asst. adjt. and qrmr., Nov., 1914; active service at Antwerp with Royal Naval Divn.; subsequently served as A.A. and Q.M.G. on Gallipoli; selected as D.A. and Q.M.G. of an Army Corps at Salonika with temporary rank of brig.-gen.; comdt., N.Z.E.F. in U.K., Mar., 1916; G.O. in charge admtn., N.Z. Forces, May, 1919; admstr., W. Samoa, 1923-28; also admstr., Tokelau Is., 1926-28; rep. N.Z. before mandates committee, L. of N., 1928; Legion of Honour (Croix de Chevalier) conferred by Pres. of the French Republic, 1916; ment. in despatches (3).

RICHARDSON, R. B.—B. 1902; ed. Lancing Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1927; cadet, Tanganyika Territory admstr., May, 1924; asst. sec., Dec., 1924; attd. as asst. prin., C.O., Feb. to Aug., 1927; ag. crown coun., Jan. to Mar. and Apr. to July, 1929 and in 1932; ag. asst. admstr. gen. and regsr., companies, July to Sept., 1929.

RICHES, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1885; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut., London Scottish, Sept., 1917; apptd. to army audit staff Eastern command, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 17th July, 1922.

RIDDELL, WALTER ALEXANDER, M.A., Ph.D.—Supt., trades and lab. dept. of pub. wks., Ontario govt., 1916; dep. min. of lab., Ontario, 1919; ch. of agrl. serv., Internat. Lab. Office, L. of N. Jan., 1920; Dominion of Canada advisory offr. to L. of N., Geneva, 1925.

RIDDELL, HON. WILLIAM RENWICK.—B. 1852; ed. Cobourg, Ont., Coll. Inst. and Victoria Univ., Toronto; lecturer in maths., Normal Sch., Ottawa, 4 years; called to bar, 1883; Q.C., 1897; bencher, Law Soc., Upper Can., since 1892; judge, high ct., Ont., King's Bench divn., 1906; judge, appellate divn. (second div. ct.), 1913; hon. mem., American Bar Assoc., and of Bar Assoc. of several states; F.R. Hist. Soc., England; F.R.S.C., Can.; LL.D., Toronto, Yale, McMaster, Lafayette, Rochester, Wesleyan, North Western Univs.; L.H.D., Syracuse Univ.; J.U.D., Trinity Coll. (Toronto); author of several legal and hist. works; has contrib. extensively to hist., scientific and literary juls.

RIDGWAY, THE Rt. HON. SIR J. WEST, G.C.B. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.S.I., P.C. (Ireland).—B. 1844; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-secr., foreign dept., India, 1881-5; commr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1886-7; under-secr. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895-1903; chmn. of comm. sent to S. Africa on Transvaal con. stitution, 1906; mem. of hon. comm. comtee. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn., ct. of dira., Br. N. Borneo Co., 1910.
RIDGWAY, T. R. V.—Asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923.

RIGNELL, JOHN HERBERT.—B. 1886; Scots Guards, 1904-25; mily. prov., staff corps, 1911; warrant offr., cl. I., 1920-25; physical training instr., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1915-19; physical training instr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1925.
RIMINGTON, CAPT. G.B.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

RINFRET, HON. THIBAUDEAU.—B. 1879; ed. St. Mary's Coll. (B.A., 1897), McGill Univ. (B.C.L., 1900); called to Quebec bar, 1901; K.C., 1912; min. of roads in Quebec Govt., 1910-22; prof., munici. law and law of public utilities, McGill Univ.; judge, sup. ct., Canada, Sept., 1924.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1896; lieut., R.A.V.C., Jan., 1917; serv. in Salonica, Macedonia, and with Army of Black Sea; capt., Jan., 1918; demob., Jan., 1921; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1921.

RISLEY, SIR JOHN SHUCKBURGH, K.C.M.G. (1922), K.C. (1921), C.B. (1912)—B. 1867; ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibition); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Inn of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L., 1894; author of "The Founding of St. Stephen's Golf Club," 1893; "The Law of War," 1897; "Notes and Echoes," 1902; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th and 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1901; legal adviser, 19th May, 1911.
RITCHIE, J. D.—Clk., agrl. dept., Kenya, Aug., 1912; head clk. and acct., cust. dept., Sept., 1922.
RITCHIE, T. M.—B. 1896; foreman of wks., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1926; inspr. of wks., 1928; mast., Agro. Tech. Schl., 1930.

ROBERTS, ARTHUR HALLAM, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1888; ed. Aldenham schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1914; cadet, Fiji, Jan., 1916; priv. sec. to gov., Mar., 1916; ag. 1st cls. clk., col. sec's office and clk. to exec. and leg. coun., Jan., 1916; passed cadet, June, 1916; priv. sec., Oct., 1916; 2nd gradelst. commr., Oct., 1916; ag. crown solr., July, 1917; assigned dist. of Savu Savu, Aug., 1917; act. serv., June, 1918; dist. commr., Navua, Dec., 1919; resigned, Oct., 1920; el. mem., leg. coun., 1923-26; regsr., sup. ct., taxing master, curator, pub. trustee and offr. recr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1927; ag. atty. gen. in 1931 and 1939; ch. pol. mag., Aug., 1931.
ROBERTS, GEORGE.—B. 1885; ent. Admy., 1902; seconded as exec. engrnr., Falkland Is., 1925; col. engrnr., 1928; mem., leg. coun., 1929; title of office altered to dir., pub. wks., 1931.

ROBERTS, GEORGE FURSE.—B. 1881; ed. Univ. Coll., London; telegraphist, G.P.O., Sept., 1897; 2nd div. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Apr., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1905; off. astt. to govt. agt., Uva Prov., Apr., 1907; pol. mag., Panadure, Jan., 1909; Jaffna, Dec., 1909; astt. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, and commr. of requests and pol. mag., Nuwara Eliya-Hatton, May, 1912; pol. mag., Galle, Mar., 1915; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1916; tea commr., Nov., 1918; contr., estate supplies, in addition to his own duties, May, 1919; commr., requests, Colombo, Nov., 1921; rubber contr., Oct., 1922; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Nov., 1925; ditto, Negombo, Nov., 1926; regir.-gen. and regir., motor cars, Nov., 1928; addl. dist. judge, Kandy, June, 1931.

ROBERTS, JOHN EDWIN THEOPHILUS.—Ed. Mico Coll., Jamaica; head teacher, St. Mary's Schl., St. Kitts; head teacher, Road Town Wes. Schl., Virgin Is.; clk. to commr., Virgin Is., Mar., 1917; ast. educn. dist. offr., Oct., 1919-Feb., 1920; educn. dist. offr., Mar., 1920; clk. to mag., and bailiff, sup. ct., Apr., 1921; commr. to admstr. oaths, July, 1921.

ROBERTS, JOHN HOLLAND, B.A., M.I.M.E.—B. 1886; ed. Tonbridge Schl., 1900-04 and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (B.A. mech. sciences); astt. engrnr., P.W.D., Perak, 1912; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S. 1914; exec. engrnr., 1921; senr. exec. engrnr., 1930.
ROBERTS, M. A. W., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Trinity Coll.), Dublin), F.R.C.S. (Ireland).—Med. offr., Kenya, July, 1929.

ROBERTS, PERCY HAMER.—Prison offr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1901; served penal settlmt., Massaruni, and at Essequebo and Georgetown county prisons; clerical astt., Georgetown and Essequebo prisons; gaoler, G. Coast, Mar., 1911; keeper, James Fort prison, June, 1911.

ROBERTS, THOMAS WEBB.—B. 1880; ed. Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1902; pol. mag., Matare, Aug., 1905; Panadure, Sept., 1906; dist. judge, Chilaw, Feb., 1909; ast. dist. judge, Batticaloa, May, 1912; commr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1914; ast. commr. of excise, Mar., 1917; excise commr., Aug., 1920; dist. judge, Galle, Sept., 1927.
ROBERTS, W. P.—B. 1893; Out Island commr., grade IV, Bahamas, 1923; grade III, 1926; grade II, 1927.

ROBERTS, WILLIAM RODDICK.—B. 1880; war serv., three years, nine months, R.N. and R.N.A.S.; gaol tech. instr., prisons dept., Gold Coast, Jan., 1921; mast., Accra tech. schl., Oct., 1923; seconded to prisons dept., Oct. to Nov., 1923; housemast., Apr., 1926.

ROBERTS-WRAY, KENNETH OWEN.—B.1899; R. Milly, Acad., Woolwich, 1917-18; comand., R. Arty., Jan., 1918; served in France, 1918; ret., 1920; Merton Coll., Oxford.(lstcls. jurisprudence, 1922); M.A.; certif. of hon., bar final exam., 1923; called to bar, Jan., 1924; S. Eastern circuit; professional legal clk., Miny. of Health, Oct., 1926; ast. ast. ch. clk., Jan., 1929; 2nd ast. legal adviser, C.O., 1st July, 1931; part author of "The Law of Collisions on Land."

ROBERTSON, HON. GIDEON DECKER, P.C., LL.D.—B. 1874; called to senate, Jan., 1917; min. without portfolio, Oct., 1917; min. of lab., Nov., 1918 and re-appld. to same portfolio, July, 1920; served as successful mediator in many industrial disputes; Canadian govt. del. to 1st internat. lab. confce., Washington, 1919; mem., Dominion tariff inquiry comm., 1920; min. of lab. in Bennett adminstr., Aug., 1930.

ROBERTSON, IAN DONALD, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrnr.—B. 1886; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jly., 1914; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., irrigation br., Aug., 1916, Aug., 1919 and Nov., 1921; dir., wks., Kelantan, Jan., 1923; exec. engrnr., Jan., 1925; ast. sr. exec. engrnr., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1930.

ROBERTSON, THOMAS ROBERT.—B. 1875; ed. Glenalmond, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.; formerly house surgeon and physician, Swansea hosp. and Victoria Park hosp. for chest diseases; civil surg., S. African Field Force, 1901-2 (medal and three clasps); astt. commr. and gen. med. offr., Salt Cay, Turks Is., 1905; dist. commr. and gen. med. offr., Caicos Is., 1905; gen. med. offr., Grand Turk, 1909.

ROBERTSON, Sir W. C. F., K.C.M.G.(1927), C.M.G. (1919).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; exhibitioner, 1885 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1889; inspr., schls. G. Coast, 1898; dir., educn., 1899; ast. comptr., cust., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901; ast. sec., native affairs, June to Nov., 1905; prov. commr., Nov., 1905; sec. for native affairs, May, 1907; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1908; chief astt. col. sec., Jan., 1909; ag. col. sec., in 1909, 1910, 1911, 1912, 1913; deputy gov., Jan., 1910; dep. gov., Dec., 1912, and April to May, 1913; ag. gov. May to Aug., 1914; dep. gov., Aug. to Sept., 1914; col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1915; leut.-gov. and chief sec. to govt., Malta, May, 1917; offr. admstg. govt., Malta, for various periods, 1920-25; gov.'s dep., in Oct., 1920; leut. gov. by commn. under new constitution, Apr., 1921; gov.'s dep., for various periods in 1920 and 1922; hon. LL.D., Malta Univ., 1925; gov., Barbados, 1925; attended C.O. confce., 1927 and 1930; ret., 1932.
ROBERTSON, WILLIAM.—B.1875; ed. Palmerston Dist. High Schl., N.Z.; N.Z. dept. of lands and survey, 1892; inspr., 1919; astt. under-sec., 1925; under-sec., 1931.

ROBIN, Major General Sir ALFRED WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1916), C.B. (1900).—B. 1861; ed. privately and at Otago high schl.; N. Zealand volr. offr.; comd. squadn. N. Zealand mtd. rifles, Queen Victoria's diamond jub., 1897; comd. Ist N. Zealand contngt., S. Africa, 1890-1901; comdt., Otago mil. dia., 1902-07 (revr. col.); chief of gen. staff and 1st min. of min. of coun. of defence, 1907-11 (col.); adjt. and qtrmr.-gen., 1911-12; represented N. Zealand on Imperial gen. staff, War Off., 1912-13; comdt. and G.O.C., N. Zealand mil. forces, 1914-19; maj.-gen., 1916.

ROBINSON, Capt. ARTHUR GORDON.—B.1887; ed. St. Asaph grm. schl., N. Wales and Birmingham Univ.; astt. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1912; seconded, Kedah, July, 1912; B.E.F., France, Mar., 1917-Jan., 1919; capt., commanding Indian labr., lieut., R.E.; ex. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1921; sen. ex. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., July, 1928; ch. hydraulic engrnr., 1930; astt. dir., drainage and irrign. and astt. advr., do., Malay States, Jan., 1932.

ROBINSON, Aubrey Charles.—B. 1875; ed. Lancing Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1901; practised at the bar, Jamaica, 1901 to 1911; commr., and judge of grand ct., Cayman Is., Jan., 1913; compd. revised edition of Laws of Cayman Is. (publicn. deferred during the war); regir.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1918; perf. duties of S. and C. mag. in July and Dec., 1918 and from

ROCK, JOHN CHRISTOPHER WILBERFORCE.—B. 1882; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. ast. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., June, 1906; Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1908; pol. mag., Panadure, Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Nov., 1911; ast. dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1912; ditto, Matara, Oct., 1913; ditto, Kalutara, Aug., 1920; dep. commr. of excise, May, 1921; dist. judge, Matara, Apr., 1925; ditto, Galle, Mar., 1926; dep. commr., stamps, July, 1927; dist. judge, Jaffna, Feb., 1928; reg'lar gen. and reg'lar, motor cars, Sept., 1930; excise. commr., June, 1931.

RODD, A. J.—B. 1886; apptd., after exam., ast. library attendant, O.O., 11th Dec., 1906; library attendant, 18th Feb., 1911; supplementary clerk, col. audit dept., Apr., 1914; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug., 1914 to 1919; reg'lar, 1st Apr., 1923.

RODEN, SIR ROBERT BLAIR, KT. BACH. (1918).—B. 1860; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; clk., public library, Antigua, 1880; 2nd clk. to the gov', Leeward Is., 1881; acted on several occasions as ch. clk., and as clk. to the legis. and exec. couns., and as a dist. mag., Antigua; also acted as priv. sec. to different gov's., Leeward Is., in 1883, 1890, 1895 and 1896; mag., Nevis, 1890; mem. St. Kitts and Nevis legislative couns., 1891; mem., fed. leg. coun., Leeward Is., 1898-9; mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1903; visiting justice, Glendairy pris., 1904; ch. just., St. Vincent, 1912; adm'ntd. gov't. of St. Vincent from Oct., 1912 to Feb., 1913, and for short periods in Apr., Aug., and Dec., 1913; also from Mar. to Nov., 1914; ch. just., Br. Honduras, Feb., 1915; chmn. of food control com'tee., Jan., 1918; ret., 1921.

RODGER, ROBERT, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. Mech.E., A.M.Inst.M. & Cy.E., M.I.Struc.E., R.G.S.—B. 1893; ed. Wishaw High Schl., Wishaw Tech. Schl. and Glasgow Tech. Coll. (affiliated); on active serv. with Lanarkshire Yeo. (Lowld. Mtd. Bds., Scot., T.F.) 1914-20; dist. engr., P.W.D., Palestine, 1924; road engr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, on "Tarmet" Scheme 1926; road engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, on road development schemes, 1930; road engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1930; ast. ass't dir., pub. wks., in 1931 and 1932.

RODRIGO, EDMUND.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; att'd., Kurunegala Kachcheri, Mar., 1913; office ast. to gov't. agt., W.Prov., May, 1915; mun. mag., Colombo, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1917; office ast. to gov't. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1918; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Apr., 1920; ditto, Matara, May, 1922; ast. commr., excise, Sept., 1924; dep. commr., excise, Sept., 1927; contr., finance and supplies, July, 1931; ast. gov't. agt., Puttalam, July, 1932.

RODWELL, SIR CECIL HUNTER, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1909).—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; serv. on staff of high commr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904; gov', Fiji, and high commr., W. Pacific, 1918; gov. and commrdr.-in-ch., B. Guiana, 1925; gov. and commrdr.-in-ch., Southern Rhodesia, Sept., 1928.

ROE, ROBERT JESTIN, M.R.C.V.S. (Williams Memorial Prize), D.V.S.M.—B. 1897; ed. Drogheda and R. Vety. Coll., Ireland; war. serv., 1915-16; vety. surg., Cyprus, June, 1921; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Jan., 1923; del. to Imp. Agric. Research Conf., 1927; ag. dir., animal husbandry, July-Oct., 1928; title changed to ch. vety. off., Jan., 1930; rep. gov't. Internat. Dairy Cong., Copenhagen, 1931.

ROGERS, FRANCIS MERVYN.—B.1890; student, R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1915; mily. serv., 1916-19; sub-foreman, Kew, 1919-20; head gardener, Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; supt. plantations, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, 1927.
ROGERS, WALTER ERNEST.—B. 1864; ent. civ. serv., S. Australia, 1882; 2nd clk., audit dept., 1912; audr.-gen., S. Aust., 1923.

ROGERSON, WALTER JOHN LANCASTER, B.A., Cantab.—B.1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attd. Kandy kach., Jan., 1913; addtl. office astt., Anuradhapura, June, 1914; ag. office astt. to gov't. agt., Kandy, June, 1914; office astt. to gov't. agt., Badulla, Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1915; office astt. to gov't. agt., Kandy, May, 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Jan., 1921; ditto, Kandy, Feb., 1921; dist. judge Kegalla, Sept., 1922; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1923; astt. gov't. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1925; addnl. gov't. agt., N.W.P., Feb., 1930; astt. gov't. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1931.

ROLSTON, CECIL MICHELL—M.D., C.M., Univ. of Manitoba, Canada, 1890; L.R.C.P., Edin.; F.R.C.S., Edin.; L.R.F.P. and S., Glasgow, 1898; mem. Coll. of Phys. and Surgeons, 1903; F.R.I., pub. health, London; late civil surg., attached to R.A.M.C. during S.African war, 1900-1902; Queen's medal, 2 clasps; King's medal, 2 clasps; apptd. to Leeward Is., med. serv., 1903; med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, and ag. med. offr. of pub. insttns., 1903; dist. med. offr. of health, Vancouver, B. Columbia, 1908; med. offr., dist. S. Nevis, 1914; ag. mag., Nevis, May to June, 1915; med. offr., dist. 6, Nevis, Sept., 1915.

ROOKE, CHARLES EGUSTA, M.Inst.T., F.R.S.S.—B. 1892; ed. Dover Coll.; astt. traffic man., Uganda Rly., Oct., 1914; served with Indian Expedy. Force, 1915-17; 2nd lieut., Indian Army R. of O.; astt. traffic man., F.M.S. Rly., Oct., 1919; gen. man., rly., Cyprus, Oct., 1929.
ROOS, HON. TIELMAN J. DE V., K.C.—M.L.A. for divn. of Lichtenburg, Union of S. Africa since 1915; min. of just., June, 1924; judge, ct. of appeal, Nov., 1929.

ROOTS, NEVILLE, M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Tonbridge Schi. and City and Guilds Engnrng. Coll., S. Kensington; held various engnrng. appts. in U.K., 1897-1904; inspgr. engnr., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1905; astt. dir., P.W.D., 1914; ag. dir., Apr., 1920 to Sept., 1921; dep. dir., Apr., 1922.

ROPER, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1891; entd. C.A. office, 1907; seconded to Falkland Is., Aug., 1912; ag. priv. sec. to admstn. and clk. to coun., Aug., 1913; chf. clk. to col. sec., Jan., 1914; ag. local audr., Nov., 1914; retnd. to C.A. office, 1915; served European war (M.G.C., France), 1916-1919; 1st cls. clk., Nyassaland, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. clk., July to Dec., 1921; edr., Gazette, Oct., 1921 to Dec., 1922; ag. 2nd astt. sec. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., in 1922, 1925 and 1926; organising sec., Nyassaland Vol. Res., Sept., 1926; ag. ch. transport offr., Dec., 1926 to May, 1927; office astt., secretariat, Apr., 1927; sec., gov't. tender bd., Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1928; ag. staff offr., Nyassaland Vol. Res., Apr. to Nov., 1929; ag. senr. astt. sec., May, 1929 to June, 1930 and Nov., 1930 to Mar. 1931; supt., census, 1930; astt. sec., Apr., 1931.

ROSE, ALAN EDWARD PERCIVAL, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1899; ed. Aldenham Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; temp. 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., B.E.F., 1918-19; 2nd cls. hons., law tripes, Cambridge, 1921; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, Jan., 1923; mem., S.E. circuit; ch. pol. mag., Fiji, July, 1929; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1930 to June, 1931; crown coun., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1931; ag. solr.-gen. in 1931 and 1932.

ROSE, HON. HUGH EDWARD.—B. 1869; ed. at Jarvis St. Collegiate Inst., Univ. of Toronto; (B.A., 1891; LL.B., 1892), and Osgoode Hall; mem. of the firm of Faeken, Cowan, Chadwick and Rose; called to the bar, Ontario, 1894; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., Ontario, Dec., 1916; examr. in law for some years for University of Toronto and the Law Society of Upper Canada.
ROSEDALE, JOHN LEWIS—B. 1889; prof. of biochemistry, King Edward VII Medical Coll., Singapore, Apr., 1927.

ROSEDALE, WILLIAM ORIEL PELLY.—B.1891; astt. dist. offr., Nigeria, Aug., 1914; dist. offr., 1924; astt. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1927; ag. prin. astt. sec., 1930-31; admstive. offr., class I, Oct., 1930.

ROSS, EDWARD ANDREW, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Leigh Gram. Schi. and Manchester Univ.; on active serv., 1914-19; relinquished comm., 1921 with rank of capt.; cadet, F.M.S. May, 1920; passed cadet, Dec., 1921; supernumary offr., cls. V, May, 1923; astt. contrlr., lab., Penang, Mar., 1925; offr., cls. IV, May, 1927; attd. for sp. duty to office of offl. assignee and pub. trustee, regis. of companies and collr., est. duty, F.M.S., May, 1927; astt. contrlr., rubber, and astt. dist. judge, S'pore, Oct., 1927; 2nd astt. advr., B. Patah and mag., Klang, Jan., 1928; astt. advr., Kukup, Sept., 1928; contrlr., lab., Johore, Jan., 1930; offr., cls. III, May, 1932.

ROSS, GEORGE ARCHIBALD PARK, M.D., Ch.M. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Edin.); diploma, trop. diseases, Liverpool.—B. 1879; S. African War, Lovat's Scouts, 1900-02; Anglo-Portuguese bndry. comm., Zambesia, 1904-5; native rebellion, Natal, 1906; trop. disease investig., 1906-10; govt. pathologist, Natal, 1911; astt. health offr., Union of S. Africa, stand. in Natal, 1918; major, S. African med. corps, in ch. sleeping sickness bureau, Durban, and sanitation offr., Natal, 1914-19.

ROSS, J. G. H.—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., Sept., 1919; ag. junr. astt. sec., Kenya, Nov., 1919 to Oct., 1919; ag. clk. of exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1919 to Mar., 1920; editor, offl. Gazette, Feb., 1920 to Mar., 1920.
ROSS, JAMES SANDFORD.—B. 1884; ed. Merchant Taylors Schi.; mily. serv., 1914-19; N.W. Frontier campaign, 1915; Salonica Field Force, 1916-19; astt. treas., Kenya, 1919.

ROSS, OSWALD F.—Ed. Malvern Coll. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; capt., N. Staffs. Regt., European War; served Dardanelles expdn., 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916; passed A.T.S. course, 1920; astt. dist. commr. Gold Coast, 1920.

ROUSE, HAROLD STUART, C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Truro Coll. and Christ's Hosp.; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; engrnr., drainage wks. office, Dec., 1913; transf'd to gen. wks. office, P.W.D., Apr., 1915; ag. exec. engrnr., G.W.O., Sept., 1915-16 and from Feb., 1921 to Oct., 1922; offr. in charge, G.W.O., May, 1924; sec., water emergency comte., May to Aug., 1929; exec. engrnr., roads office, P.W.D., Nov., 1931.

ROUSSELL, PHILIP GEORGE.—B. 1877; ent. N.Z. govt. rly. dept., 1893; ch. clk. to dist. traf. man., Ohakune, 1922; ch. clk., head office, 1924; sec., rlys. dep., 1925; gen. supt., transportation, 1928; gen. man., rlys., 1931.
ROWAN, THOMAS LESLIE.—B. 1908; schol. of Tonbridge (head boy 1925) and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to C.O. as asst. prin. after compet. exam., 3rd Nov., 1930.

ROWAN, WILLIAM ALFRED.—B. and educ. Graaff Reinet, Cape Prov.; civ. serv., lower, 1903; higher, 1907; LL.B., 1921; Rhodesian pol., 1897; O.R.C. pol., 1900; asst. mag., O.F.S., 1905; addnl. mag., Bloemfontein, 1915; overseas mil'y serv., 1916-20; ret. as capt.; ment. in desps.; mag., Senekal, 1920; addnl. mag., Pretoria, 1920; mag., Bethlehem and Kroonstad, 1928; do., Port Elizabeth, 1932.

ROWATT, HUGH HOWARD.—B. 1861; ed., Ottawa Coll. Inst., and St. Catherine's Affiliated Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Can., 1887, as correspondent; ch. clk., mining lands br., Mar., 1907; contrlr., mining lands, 1912; supt., 1920; mem., Dominion lands bd., 1928; dep. min. of interior, 1931.

ROWE, CHARLES HAWKSWELL.—B. 1885; asst. acct., constrn., F.M.S. Railways, Feb., 1912; divi. acct., June, 1913-Jly., 1914; acct. and audr., constrn., head office, Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1920; stores supt., F.M.S. Rlys., Nov., 1926.
ROWE, ERIC GEOFFREY.—B. 1904; ed. Chatham House Schl., Ramsgate and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; B.A. 1926; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1930.

ROWE, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1878; ent. Crown Agent's office, 1896; asst. head, stores dept., 1917; asst. head, pay dept., 1920; cashier, 1921; dep. head, finance dept., 1927; dep. head, stores dept., 1929; dep. head, finance dept., 1932; head, pay dept., 1933.

ROWE, LIMUT.-COL. R. H., D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Wellington Coll. and Royal Mil'y Acad., Woolwich; Royal Arty., 1902-25; Bermuda and Jamaica, 1902-6; served with native infantry, Nigeria, 1907-10; Northern Hinterland exped., 1907-8; Niger Cross River exped., 1908-9; operations against Abini, 1909, and against Mochis, 1910; dep. dir., surveys, Nigeria, 1911; Nigeria-Dahomey bndry. comsrr., 1912; Great war (Cameroons, France and Russia), 1914-18; surv'r. gen., Gold Coast, 1920; comsrr., lands, in addn., 1925; surv'r. gen., Sierra Leone, in addn., 1925; Gold Coast rep. with staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to W. Africa, 1925; chmn., comtees, on pub. wks. acct., rly. constrn., mun. finance and mil'y. defence; mem., leg. coun., 1926; comsrr., lands, Nigeria, 27th Oct., 1926.

ROWELL, THOMAS HUBERT.—C.I.K., loco. dept., I.M.R., Pretoria, Oct., 1900; acctg. brch., ch. loco. supt.'s office, Jan., 1903; acctg. offr., ch. mech. engr., s.dept., July, 1908; inspr., audit dept., S.A.R. and Harbrs., Oct., 1910; inspr., asst. contr. and audr. gen.'s dept., Union of S. Africa, May, 1911; inspr., (rlys. and hbrs.) control and audit dept., July, 1916; senr. inspr., Oct., 1922; ag. ch. inspr., rly. and harbrs., Apr. to Sept., 1924.

ROWEN, F. C. P.—B. 1896; copyist, audit dept., B. Honduras, 1912; 3rd ols. clk., atty. gen.'s off., 1915; 2nd clk. clk., ditto, 1918; dk. to regir.-gen., 1919; sec., riot enquiry coman., 1919; ag. regir.-gen. and cust. enemy ppty. and contr. local clearing office, 1920; sec., yellow fever enquiry coman., 1921; again ag. regir. gen., 1921, 1923 and 1925; regir.-gen., regir., lands titles, regir., sup., vice-adm'y. and bankey. cts., regir., companies and patents and regir., births marriages and deaths, 1927; J.P.; stamp comsrr., dir., pub. offr. guarantee fund; comsrr., income tax, 1927.

RUDDICK, JOHN ARCHIBALD, LL.D.—B.1862, dairy expert; asst. to dairy comsrr., Canada, 1891; prin. dairy schl., Kingston, Ont., 1896; dairy comsrr., New Zealand, 1898; asst. to comsrr. of agr., Can., 1900; dairy comsrr., Can., 1905; dairy and cold storage comsrr., Can., 1906; hon. LL.D. (Queen's Univ.), 1924.

RUNDLE, GEN. SIR HENRY MACLEOD LESLIE, R.A.—B. 1863; G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1914); G.C.V.O. (1911); K.C.B., 1898; K.C.M.G., 1901; D.S.O., 1897; ed. Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; entd. R.A., 1876; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Zulu war, 1879; Boer war, 1881; Egyptian war, 1882; Nile expdn., 1884-5; Soudan Frontier field force, 1885-7; Soudan Frontier, 1889; Dongola expdn. force, 1896; adjt. gen., Egyptian army; Khartoum expdn., 1898; comdng. S.E. Dist., 1898-9; dep. adjt. gen. to the forces, 1899-1900; in comd. of div., Aldershot, 1900; lieut. gen. on staff in comd. of 8th Div., S. African field force, 1900, 1902; lieut.-gen. of Nthn. Comd., 1905-7; col.-comdnt., R.A., 1907; gov. and comdnt.-in-chief-Malta, 1909-1915.

RUSHTON, CHARLES BERKELEY, I.S.O. (1931).—C.I.K., comsrr. of rlys. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to comsrr. of rlys., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901; sec., W. Australian London Agency, 3rd Nov., 1919.

RUSHTON, HERBERT HARVEY.—B.1876; acct., electric light dept., Gibraltar, 1897; asst. sec. and asst. acct., munici. authority, 1902; ch. acct. and asst. sec., 1903; sec. and finan. offr., 1908 (also man., pub. electricity and water supplies, mem., comtee. on exchange and currency); asst. ch. acct., Uganda Rly., E. Africa Prot., 1915; lieut., E.A. Forces, 1915; capt., 1916; ag. dep. ch. acct., Uganda Rly., 1916 to June, 919 (also govt. rep., Nairobi munici. coun., 1918, mem., gen. manager's advisory comtee., 1919); ag. ch. acct., to Uganda Rly., June to Dec., 1919; dep. treas., Kenya Colony, 1920 and 1921; ag. treas., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. couns. (also chmn., currency and other comtees.); col. treas., Fiji, 1922 (with charge of group of depts., cust., post and marine, transport, posts, telegraphs and telephones, govt. stores, and returned soldiers' dairy stiml.); mem., exec. and leg. couns.; comsrr. of currency, mem., educn., mining and other bds.; chmn., many comtees.; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1929; gov.'s dep. for four periods; treas., Kenya, Mar., 1930.

RUSSELL, MAJOR-GEN. SIR ANDREW HAMILTON.—K.C.B. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1868; held coman. as lieut. for 5 yrs. in Imperial Army; vol. offr., New Zealand, 1900; hon. A.D.O. to H.E. the Gov. of N.Z., 1911; brig.-gen., 1914; temp. major-gen., 1915; major-gen. in command of N.Z. Div., 1916.
RUSSELL, WILLIAM, M.C., M.D.—B. 1888; ed. Edin. Univ.; Union of S. Africa mental healp. serv., 1913; comsrr., mental hygiene, 1932.

RUSSELL, SIR WILLIAM ALISON, KT. BACH. (1928), K.C.—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., LL.B., honours, 1897; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1900; equity bar, 1900-6; apptd. Crown advoc., pub. proscr., admtr.-gen., prin. registr. of docmts., registr. of companies, Uganda Prot., May, 1906; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Jan., 1909; ag. chief sec. to govt., Aug., 1909, to Mar., 1910; Crown advoc. and pub. proser., Mar. to May, 1910; ag. chief sec. to gov't., June, 1910 to May, 1911; Crown advoc. and pub. proser., June, 1911 to June, 1912; King's advoc., Cyprus, July, 1912; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1912; joint commr. under Reprint of Statutes Law, 1913; edition publ., Feb., 1914; K.C., Cyprus, 1924; ad. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; published "Legislative and other Forms" (2nd edn. 1928) and "Handbook for Magistrates" (2nd edn. 1928).
RUSSELL, WILLIAM HAYTORTH.—B. 1894; clk., Mankainai, Swaziland, Apr., 1915; clk., treas., 1920; asst. acct. and astt. collr., inc. tax, 1922; acct., 1928; ag. finan. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1931.

RUSSELL, W. J., A.L.M.M.—Ed. Royal Coll. of Science, Ireland and Cornwall Cent. Schl. of Mining; astt. inspr., mines, Nigeria, 1914; inspr., mines, 1919; ag. dep. ch. inspr. in 1926, 1926 and 1928; dep. ch. inspr., mines, 1929; ag. ch. inspr., mines, 1930.

RUTHERFORD, JOHN WINNE CORRIE, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Merton Coll., Oxford; astt. dist. consnr., S. Nigeria, 1911; seconded as astt. reor., 1914; astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Lagos, 1926; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1926; cls. I, grade I, admstr. serv., 1928.

RUTHERFORD, CAPT. PETER WILLIAM, M.C. (1916), F.R.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Brighton Coll.; 2nd lieut. W. Yorks Regt., 1914; lieut., 1915; seconded, Flying Corps, 1916; capt., 1917; ret'd from army, 1919; astt. dist. consnr., G. Coast, Sept., 1921.

RYCKMAN, HON. EDMOND BAIRD, B.A., M.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1866; ed. Brantford Coll., Int.t., Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall; sl. to H. of C., Canada, 1921; re-el., 1925; min. of pub. wks. in Meighen Cabinet, 1926; re-el., g.e., 1926; min. of national rev. in Bennett cabinet, 1930.

RYRIE, MAJ.-GEN. HON. SIR GRANVILLE DE LAUNE, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1916), V.D.—B. 1868; ed. King's Schl., Parramatta; mem., state parl., N.S.W., 3 years, mem., house of reps. C. of A., 1911-27; astt. min. for defence, 1919-22; commanded 3rd Light Horse Regt., N.S.W., for 7 yrs. with rank of lieut.-col.; served S. Africa, 1900-01 (severely wounded (Queen's med. 4 clasps); European War, 1914-18; commanded 2nd Light Horse Brig., A.I.F., Egypt; commanded Australian divn., Syria; G.O.C., Australian Forces, Egypt (wounded twice, five times ment. in despatches); maj.-gen. commdg. 1st Cav. Divn., N.S.W., 1923-27; high commr. for Australia, 1927-32.
SADLER, C.—Astt. gen. man., govt. rly., Trinidad, Dec. 1914; traffic man., Nigerian govt. rly., Aug., 1922; gen. man., Trinidad govt. rly., Jan., 1926.

SAINT, SYDNEY JOHN, B.Sc., A.I.C.—B. 1897; ed. Beaumarister Grammar Schl., Reading Univ.; B.Sc., 1920; served with R.A.F., 1916-19; Salters research fellow, 1920-22; lect. in agrl. chemistry, Leeds Univ., 1922-26; agrl. chemist, Barbados, 1927.

SALE, GILBERT NOEL, B.Sc. (Forestry, Edin.)—B. 1897; ed. King Alfred's Schl., Wantage, Edinburgh Univ. and Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; army serv., 1916-19; lieut., R.A. (S.R.); astt. conserv., forests, Cyprus, Mar., 1922; ag. astt. prin. forest offr., 1924; ag. prin. forest offr., 1926-28; dir., forests, Mauritius, Dec., 1928; conserv., forests, July, 1930.
SALISBURY, THOMAS RONALD FPOULKS.—B. 1882; served in Fiji, and under the condominium govt., New Hebrides; govt. agt., New Hebrides, 18th Mar., 1922.

SALMON, FREDERICK JOHN, M.C., A.C.G.I., F.R.G.S., Lieut.-Col., R. of O., R.E.—B. 1882; ed. Univ. Coll. Schl., Lond., and Cent. Tech. Coll., S. Kensington; empd. on Transvaal Mines, 1904-1907; assoc., inst. of mining and metallurgy; astt. supt., surveys, Ceylon, June, 1908; supt., surveys, 1914; served with field survey units in France, 1915-19; thrice ment. in despatches; lieut.-col., supt., surveys, on staff (appl. surveys), Ceylon, 1924; ag. astt. survr.-gen. 1928; dir., land registr., and surveys, Cyprus, Sept., 1930.
SALOMONE, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. St. Ignatius Coll. and Malta Univ.; elec. for 6th divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-elec., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for industry and commerce, Aug., 1927.

SAMARAKOON, EDWIN RICHARD DE ALWIS.—B. 1877; cls. V. Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1927; extra office astt., Badulla kach., Oct., 1927; office astt. to contr., rev., Feb., 1928; office astt. to land consnr., July, 1931.
SAMARASINGHE, SOLOMON DIAS.—B. 1875; cls. V Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1929; extra office astt., Kurunegala kach., May, 1929.
SAMBIDGE, GERALD CHURCHILL.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham; served Nigeria Regt., Mar., 1915; instr. in arts and crafts, N. Provs., Nigeria, Dec., 1920.

SAMPSON, BASIL FEHRSEN, M.C.—B. 1888; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown; M.B., B.S., Lond. Univ. (Guy's Hosp.), 1917; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1918; temp. surg., R.N. (H.M.S. Benbow), 1915; temp. capt'n., S.A.M.C., France, 1916; M.C., 1918; astt. bacteriologist, Guy's Hosp.; senr. govt. pathologist, Union of S. Africa health dept., Durban, 1931.

SAMPSON, HON. HENRY WILLIAM—B. 1872; mem., Transvaal parlmt., 1907-10; mem., Union parlmt., 1910-29; O.B.E., 1918; joined Union cabinet as min. for posts and tels. and pub. wks., Nov., 1928; pres., S.A. typographical union, 1903-1929.

SAMUEL, Rt. Hon. Sir Herbert Louis, P.C. (1908), G.B.E. (1920), M.A.—B. 1870; ed. University Coll. Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P., Cleveland Divn., N. Riding, Yorks., 1902-18; partly, under sec., home dept., 1906-9; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster (with a seat in the cabinet), 1909-10 and 1915-16; P.M.G., 1910-14 and 1915-16; pres. of the L.G.B., 1914-15; sec. of state for home affairs, 1916; chmn. of the select comtce. of house of commons on national expenditure, 1917-18; British sp. commr. to Belgium, 1919; grand offr. of the Order of Leopold of Belgium, 1919; high commr., Palestine, 1920-25.

SAMUEL, SILVERIO I., M.B.E. (1924).—Ed. at St. Gregory's Rom. Cath. gram. schl., Lagos; meser. and copyist, educ. off., Oct., 1897; clk. to inspr. of schls., Mar., 1899; sec. to comtce. for exhibtn. of schl. wks., 1902 and 1903; clk. and interpreter to trav. commr., Ilesha, interior dept., Feb., 1904; 3rd cls. clk., gov'ts. off., Aug., 1904; conf. clk. to the gov't. of Nigeria.

SANDERS, JOHN OWEN, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1892; ed. Elstow Schi. and Manchester Univ.; mil. ser., 1915-20; 2nd lieut., 1917; ag. capt., 1918; astt. Crewe loco. works, L.M.S.R., 1922; works man., F.M.S. Rlys., 1924; run. asst., 1927; ag. traffic man., Aug., 1930; transportation man., 1932.
SANDERSON, I., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.)—B. 1894; mily. serv., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

SANDFORD, GEORGE RITCHIE, O.B.E. (1924), Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple, 1924.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; priv. sec. to gov't., 1918; junr. astt. sec. and clk. of couns., Sept., 1922; ag. senr. astt. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1923; resig., 1925; clk. to leg. coun., Jan., 1926; edr., official gazette, Mar., 1926; dep. treas., 1931.

SANDFORD, THOMAS FREDERICK, M.B.E., B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; astt. native commr., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1910; native commr., Apr., 1913; served in E. African campaign, 1915-19; ag. dist. commr., 1927; ag. prov. commr., in 1929, 1930 and 1931; prov. commr., Aug., 1932; temp. mem., leg. coun., 1932.

SANDYS, MELVILL KEVERNE TRELAWNY, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1890; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1919; astt., Ratnapura kach., Nov., 1919; office astt. to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1922; ag. astt. gov't. agt., Putalam, Oct., to Dec., 1922; ag. astt. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, July, 1923; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, July, 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Oct., 1926; ag. agt., Hambantota, May, 1927; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, June, 1927; astt. gov't. agt., Uva, July, 1927; ag. astt. gov't. agt., Matare, Aug., 1927; astt. settlmt. officer, Jan., 1929; astt. gov't. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1931.

SANGER-DAVIES, ARTHUR ELPHINSTONE.—B. 1885; ed. Dover Coll., Coopers Hill and Oxford (Diploma of Forestry, 1907); astt. conservator, forests, grade II., F.M.S. forest dept., 1907; grade I., 1912; dep. conservator, forests, 1919; ag. conserv., forests, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1923; dep. conserv., forests, Oct., 1923; title of office changed to conserv., forests, Jan., 1930.

SANSON, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Plymouth and Munnamed Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; pol. probationer, 1905; registr. of criminals, F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; astt. commr. of pol., Ipoh, 1911; adjt., F.M.S. pol., 1913; commr. of pol., Johore, 1921; supt. pol., S'pore but cont. former appt., Nov., 1924; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1929; offr. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Nov., 1930; commr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1931.

SARAVANAMUTTU, P.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., local divn., Aug., 1919; astt., Colombo, kach., Aug., 1919; addtl. to Matale Kach., June, 1920; addnl. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala, June, 1922; addnl. astt. gov't. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1923; addnl. astt. dir., educ., Dec., 1925; astt. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1929; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1932.
SARGENT, CAPT. R. G., M.B.E. (1928), A.I.N.A., F.R.G.S.—Port. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1917; port capt., 1925.
SARGENT, JOHN DENYS, B.A. (Oxon.), Dipl., Forestry, Oxford.—B. 1885; astt. conserv., forests, Ceylon, Dec., 1909; dep. conserv., grade II., Apr., 1917; grade I., May, 1919; conserv., Sept., 1921.

SAUNDERS, SIR CHARLES JAMES RENAULT, K.C.M.G., 1906, C.M.G., 1895.—B. 1887; held various apptmts. in Natal civ. ser., 1876-87, when he ratified treaty with Queen of Amatongaland (desps.); res. mag., Eshowe, and J.P. for Zululand, 1888; defined bndry. between Amatongaland and Zululand, 1889 (desps.); variously employed under Zululand Gov't., 1888-95 (desps.), when he was apptd. spec. commr. for Trans-Pongoland territory, carried out final act of annexation of that territory and declared British prot. for Amatongaland; acted as res. commr. and chief mag. of Zululand, 1896; apptd. chief mag. and civ. commr. for prov. of Zululand its incorporation with Natal, 1897; medal, S. African War, 1899-1902; col. representative on Zululand Lands Delimitation Comm., 1902, and commr. for native affairs, 1905; ret., 1909.

SAUNDERS-JONES, DAVID WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1899; ed. Christ Coll., Brecon; Indian Army, 1917-23; on service in Waziristan, Egypt and Palestine; wounded in Palestine, 1919; astt. dist. commr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; prte. sec. Br. res., June to Dec., 1925; ag. prov. commr., Pemba, June, 1930; ag. pvte. sec. to Br. res., Aug., 1930.

SAUVE, HON. ARTHUR, P.C. (Can.).—B. 1875; ed. Seminary of Ste. Therese and l'Univ. de Montreal; lat. el. to legis. assem., Quebec, g.c., 1908; re-elec., g.e., 1912, 1916, 1919, 1923 and 1927; el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1930; P.M.G., in Bennett Cabinet, 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.

SAVAGE, GEORGE.—B. 1887; supt. of regtn., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1914; supt. of mails, ditto, Jan., 1915; sup. mails, Penang, but cont. ag. senr. supt. mails, July, 1917; sup., grade II., Jan., 1919; sup., grade I., Jan., 1920; supt., posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1920; senr. acct., G.P.O., S'pore, contd. to act as contr., P. and T., Jan., 1929; contr., P. and T., F.M.S., Aug., 1929; ag. P.M.G., Sept., 1929; P.M.G., Johore, July, 1930.

SAVARY, JOHN LEWIS HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1884; col. schol., Trinidad, 1901; Grey's Inn, 1902; prizeman in evidence, procedure and criminal law exam., 1904; barrister-at-law, Nov., 1905; practised at bar, Trinidad, 1906-24; retainer from Trinidad govt. as junr. coun. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1916-Dec., 1923; ag. solr.-gen., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; registr. and marshal and registr. gen. and prin. regiar., West Indian ct. of appeal, Jan., 1925; ag. puiane judge in 1925 and 1926; puiane judge, Br. Guiana, 1928.

SAVI, VICTOR GEORGE.—B. 1884; pol. probationer, S.S., Nov., 1904; astt. supt pol., Jan., 1908; astt. supt. of pol., Penang, Oct., 1917; dir., crim. intell., Jan., 1920; dep. commr., pol., Perak, Mar., 1922; supt., pol., S'pore, 1932.

SAWARD, CAPT. SIDNEY CARSON, M.C. (1916), M.M. (1916).—B. 1889; fellow of surveyors' inst.; served with 509 (1-1) Lond. Field Coy., R.E., 6th divn.; home serv., Aug. to Dec., 1914; France and Germany, Dec., 1914 to June, 1919; Ireland, June to Aug., 1919; commdg., cadre, 12th Field Co., R.E.; command., Sept., 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Oct., 1918; ag. major, Dec., 1918 to Jan., 1919; ment. in despatches, Mar., 1919; survr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; field supervisor Apr., 1924; ag. dep. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1925-30; ag. survr.-gen., Feb.-June, 1927; asst. survr.-gen., Apr., 1929.

SAWREY-COOKSON, SYDNEY SPENCER, B.A.—B. 1876; ed. Uppingham and New Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1903; judicial commissioner, N. Borneo, 1910-20; compiler, revd. edn., N. Borneo laws; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1920; compiler, revd. edn. (1926) Gambia laws; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 11th June, 1925.

SAWTELL, WILLIAM GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. at King Edward VI's Sch., Bath, and H.M.S. "Worcester"; H.M. Transport Serv., 1915-17; Indian govt. tel. ship, 1917-18; Indian Army R. of O., 1918-22; wharf-mast. and pilot, Tanganyika Territory, 1922.

SAXTON, SIDNEY WARING.—Ed. Toulouse (France), and Dean Close Schl., Cheltenham; paymr. cadet, R.N., July, 1906; paymr.-lieut.-comdr., Mar., 1918; 1st cls. French interpreter lent to C.O. and apptd. asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Sept., 1914, but did not take up apptmnt., having resumed duty in H.M. Navy for the period of the war; assumed duties as junr. asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Apr., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Accra, and ag. trav. comsnr. for Accra-Akwapim and Birrim dists., Oct., 1919; invalided, H.M. Navy, 1920; passed Tropical African service course (all subjects, 2nd part); barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1925; promoted pay comdr., ret. list, 1927; dep. prov. comsnr., 1930.

SAYER, GEOFFREY ROBLEY, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Highgate schl. (founder) and Queen's Coll., Oxford; open exhib. in classics and Fitzgerald exhib.; 2nd cls. clas. mods.; 3rd cls. lit. hum. (B.A., 1910); cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1910; passed in Cantonese, Nov., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr.
