Cathedral Schl. and Simon Langton Schl., Canterbury; war serv., 1914-19; India, Aden and Mesopotamia, as pvt., 1/4th Batt., The Buffs and 2nd lieut., The Queen's Own Royal West Kent Regt.; elk. to gov., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; ch. elk., Apr., 1923; ast. sec. to gov., Somaliland, Mar., 1929; ag. dist. comsnr., Burao dist., Aug., 1929.

WELSH, WILLIAM THOMSON.—B. 1873; elk. to mag., Cape Town, Nov., 1892; Bedford, Dec., 1892; Kimberley, Jan., 1894; Klipdam, April, 1896; Barkly West, July, 1896; Beaconsfield, Jan., 1896; Prince Albert, Mar., 1896; Jansenville, May, 1896; ast. regis., high ct., Kimberley, July, 1896; elk. to mag., Victoria East, Aug., 1900; ast. res. mag., Paarl, Aug., 1901; King Williams Town, Aug., 1904; Middeldrift, Aug., 1907; res. mag., Libode, July, 1908; Mqanduli, May, 1910; Kokstad, Feb., 1916; ast. chief mag., Transkeian Territories, and res. mag., Umtata, Apr., 1919; ch. mag., Transkeian Territories, Sept., 1920.
WENTWORTH, M. F. G., B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Haileybury and Univ. Coll., London (teacher's dipl., Lond.); inspr., schls., Aug., 1930.
WERE, MARY JOSEPHINE, L.R.C.P., S.L., D.P.H.—Lady med. offr., dist. hosp., K. Lumpur, May, 1921; do., infant welfare centre, May, 1923.
WERTH, ALBERTUS JOHANNES.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; teacher at Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-19; M.P. for Kroonstad, 1921-26; admstr., S.W. Africa, Apr., 1926.

WESSELS, SIR JOHANNES WILHELMUS, Kt.—Ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town, Downing Coll., Cambridge, Middle Temple; admitted to Cape bar, 1886; Transvaal bar, 1887; legal adviser to Lord Roberts, 1900-01; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902; judge pres., Transvaal prov. divn., 1920; judge of appl., 1923.
WESTON, BERTRAM JOHN, M.Sc. (Agr.) S. Africa, B.A. (Cantab), F.R.H.S.—B. 1907; ed. Ashford Grammar and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; horticulturist, Cyprus, 1931.

WESTON, ERNEST ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1880; B.A. Cantab, 1902; served with R.E. during Great War, 1914-19; capt., 1914; lieut.-col., 1916; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1907 and served in various posts under Nigerian govt. until 1927 when retd. on med. grounds; on staff of Imp. Economic Comm., 1927-31; commr., Cayman Is., Dec., 1931.

WESTON, HARLEY CHARLES, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; ed. Chatham House Sch., Ramsgate, Goldsmiths Training Coll., Univ. of Lond., Glasgow Univ., and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; war serv., 1914-19; ch. asst. engr., Zanzibar, 1921; res. engr., Dar-es-Salaam, 1925; prin., Accra Tech. Sch., May, 1929.

WHEATLEY, MAJOR H. H., C.B.E. (1931), O.B.E., M.C.—Served with Royal Engineers in France, 1914-19 (twice ment. in desps.); asst. to ch. engr. and sec. to govt., public wks. dept., Iraq, 1920; asst. adviser to min. of communications and wks., Jan., 1921; adviser to min. of communications and wks., Apr., 1922.

WHEATLEY, MAUR.—B. 1888; asst. mast., Victoria Inst., Selangor, Jan., 1911; headmast., govt. Eng. Sch., K. Pilah, Apr., 1927; headmast., King George V Sch., Seremban, Jan., 1928; do., Batu Rd. Sch., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1930; ag. headmast., Maxwell Sch., K. Lumpur, in addn., July-Dec., 1930.

WHEBLEY, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; joined Royal North West Mounted Pol., Canada, Dec., 1912; proceeded overseas with 1st Canadian contingent, Sept., 1914; joined Irish Guards, Oct., 1914; transfd. to 1st Guards Brig. Machine Gun Coy., Sept., 1915; served throughout Great War with Guards Divn., 1914-19; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. Meds., Meritorious Serv. Med., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgian), ment. in desps., Apr., 1917; rejoined R.N.W.M. Pol., Canada, May, 1919; asst. commt., pol., Bahamas, 21st Apr., 1921; ag. commt., June, 1921 to Apr., 1923 and from May to Dec., 1924; commt. of pol., inspr. of prisons, provost marshal and ch. of fire dept., 20th Aug., 1926.

WHEELER, LEONARD RICHMOND, B.A., B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1888; science master, Letchworth Sch., 1909-12; Gram. Sch., Antigua, 1912-14; ent. o.d. serv. as science master, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad Dec., 1914; commr. in 1st batt., B.W.I.R., from 1st Trinidad contingent, Sept., 1915; pilot, R.A.F., 1917, served in Egypt and Salonica, 1916-19; returned to Trinidad, 1920; commr. for Boy Scouts, Trinidad, 1920; transfd. to S.S. and F.M.S. as European mast., Nov., 1921; science and maths. mast., Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, Dec., 1921; vice-prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., T. Malim, Aug., 1924; asst. inspr., schla., Perak, July, 1926 and Sept., 1930; inspr., schla., Malacca, Sept., 1926; ditto, Pahang, Mar., 1928; author of "Flora of Antigua," "Scouting in the Tropics," "The Modern Malay," etc.
WHEELER, CAPT. RICHARD CHAMBERLAIN, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., Kenya, July, 1913; senior veterinary officer, Apr., 1920.

WHISKARD, SIR GEOFFREY GRANVILLE, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.B. (1923), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1909; ent. H.O., 1911; asst. pvte. sec. and subsequently priv. pvte. sec. to successive secs. of state, 1915-20; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin Castle, 1920-22; asst. sec., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; vice-chmn., Overseas Settlement C'tee., 4th Sept., 1929; asst. under sec. of State, D.O. in addn., 1st Jan., 1931; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932.

WHITCOMBE, SIDNEY F.—B. 1899; ent. C.O., July, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., ass. clk., C.O., 1917; on mil. serv., Jan., 1918 to Apr., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, Apr., 1927; dep. reg., Nov., 1931.

WHITE, ALEXANDER HAROLD, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1889; asst. colmr. (now asst. dist. commr.), Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; ag. 2nd asst. sec. and clk. of coun., June, 1915; ag. 1st asst. sec., Oct. to Dec., 1915; ag. mag., His Highness' c'ty for Zanzibar, 1918 (Brilliant Star, 4th cls., July, 1919); ag. dist. commr., Feb. to July, 1919; ass. sec., German E. Africa, provisional admtn., July, 1919; dist. political offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. mag., Dar-es-Salaam, Apr. to July, 1922; seconded for sp. serv. as Br. commr., Anglo-Belgian bndy. commr. (Ruanda-Urundi), Aug., 1922 to Aug., 1925; prov. commr., Jan., 1926; seconded for sp. serv. as Br. rep. on Anglo-Belgian bndy. commr., 1931.

WHITE, ARTHUR REGINALD.—B. 1886; ch. draftsman, Kelantan, May, 1912; on mil. serv., 1915-18; asst. supt., surveys, Kelantan, Nov., 1918; supt., surveys, Jan., 1923; supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., July, 1929; supt., surveys, Malacca, Mar., 1931.

WHITE, ARTHUR V.—B. 1871; ed. Woodstock Pub. and High Schls., and Univ. of Toronto (M.E.); engr. with several prominent firms; lect. in mech. drawing and machine design, Toronto Tech. Sch.; later with dept. of pub. wks., Can.; consulting engr., Coman. of Conservation, Can., until its abolition, 1921; consulting engr., representing Can. to the internat. joint commr., Lake of the Woods investigation under the boundary waters treaty, 1911; consulting engr. to Hydro-electric Comm., Ontario, 1922; has written extensively on water power, elec. development and fuel supply.

WHITE, RT. HON. SIR WILLIAM THOMAS, P.C. (1920), K.C.M.G. (1916), B.A.—B. 1866; ed. High Schls, Oakville and Brampton, Ont., Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate of the Ontario law schl.; apptd. manager Naional Trust Co., Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently general manager and vice-pres. of the same institution; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of finance in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1917; ditto in National govt., Oct., 1917; re-elec. at g.e., Dec., 1917, for Leeds-Brockville; resig. portfolio, July, 1919.
WHITE, W. V. C.—B. 1897; on milv. serv., 8th Sept., 1914 to 14th Dec., 1915; discharged through wounds, 17th Feb., 1916; ent. Min. of Food, 18th Mar., 1918; appt. cler. offr., C.O., 24th Apr., 1922.

WHITEHEAD, FRANK EDWARD, O.B.E. (1926), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); certif., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med.—B. 1880; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; late hon. surg. County Hosp., Huntingdon; med. offr., Sierra Leone, July, 1913; Cameroons Exped. Force, Aug., 1914 to Feb., 1916; med. offr., Somaliland, June, 1916; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Nyassaland, July, 1922; av. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1922; dir., med. and army. services, 1923.
WHITEHEAD, JOHN.—B. 1894; ed. High Schl. and Tech. Coll., Greenock; associate mem., of Inst. of Naval Architects; naval architect, Nigerian marine dept., 1928.
WHITEHEAD, LOUIS HAROLD.—B. 1871; chief clerk to atty-gen., Trinidad, 1st Apr., 1905.

WHITEHORN, Roger Herbert, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1911; attd. col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1911; attd. Colombo kach., Aug., 1912; ag. pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1913; office asst., Ratnapura kach., Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Galle, May, 1914; 4th asst. col. sec., Sept., 1914; on military duty as asst. censor, Nov., 1914; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Apr., 1916; 4th asst. col. sec., Aug., 1916; ag. third asst. col. sec., Oct., 1916; priv. sec. to gov. in addn. Nov., 1916; on military duty, 1918; extra asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1920; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1920; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admstg. gov't., 5th-23rd Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Kandy, Sept., 1922; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Kegalla, Mar., 1923; asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1924; ditto, Puttalam and Chilaw, Dec., 1924; ditto, Matara, Oct., 1927; chmn., munici. coun., Kandy, Nov., 1927.

WHITEHOUSE, Bertram Reginald.—B. 1891; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham, and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1913; cadet, S.S., Feb., 1915, and lent to Johore; attd., censor's office, Singapore, till Apr., 1918; granted permission to join H.M. Forces, Apr., 1918; attd., land office, Johore, Sep., 1919; ag. supt. of Indian imigr., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1922; dep. regis. sup. et., Spore, Jan., 1928; ag. regis. do., in addn., May to June, 1929; dist. offr., Oct., 1929; ag. regis., dist. judge and 1st mag., Malacca, Jan., 1931.

WHITFIELD, Hugh.—B. 1885; ed. Royal Acad. Inst., Belfast and Belfast Univ.; B.E. (2nd cls. hons.); ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Perak, Nov., 1912; ast. engrnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1914; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; exec. engrnr., govt. factory, Nov., 1923-Aug., 1927; anti-malaria engrnr., F.M.S., Dec., 1928.

WHITELEY, Gerald Charles.—B. 1891; ed. Bradford and Jesus Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); 2nd cls. hons. mods., 3rd cls. lit. hum.; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; ag. res., Muri prov., 1926; admstv. offr., cls. I., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1931; ag. ch. sec., June, 1931.
WHITLEY, W., B.A. (Oxf.), A.I.C.—B. 1902; analytical chem., Tanganyika Territory 1928.

WHITLEY, Major Norman Henry Pow nell, M.C., B.A., Cantab., Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple.—B. 1883; called to bar; N. Circuit, 1907; on war serv., 7th Manchester Regt., T.F., 1914-18; demob. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; dep. pub. pros., S.S., June, 1921; puisne judge, S.S., Feb., 1930; judge, F.M.S., Feb., 1930; puisne judge, Spore, Nov. 1930; ag. sr. puisne judge, Penang, Apr., 1931.
WHITTING, Charles Edward Jewel.—B. 1900; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 26th July, 1922.

WHITTLE, Reginald Alfred, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. math. mods., 1919; B.A., 1920; 2nd lieut., Manchester Regt., 1916; served in France, 1917; invalided from Army, 1918; ast. dist. comsrs., Uganda, 1921; ast. sec., 1922; senr. ast. sec., 1932.

WHYBRO, Charles, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; ed. Berkhamsted Schl., London Univ., Schl. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1920-23; economic tripes, pt. I., 3rd cls., 1922; law tripes, pt. II., 3rd cls., 1923; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WHYTE-SMITH, Thomas Stoddart.—B. 1883; ed. Merchiston Castle Schl. and Edinburgh Univ.; writer to signet, 1906; 2nd lieut., the Royal Scots, 1915; lieut., 1916; capt., K.A.R., 1918; served in Nyassaland and Portuguese E. Africa, 1917-19; 2nd ast. crown solr., Hong Kong, Dec., 1923; J.P., 1924; ag. ast. land offr., Mar. to Nov., 1924 and Sept., 1928; ast. crown solr., 1926; ag. mag., Kowloon, 1929 and 1930; ast. crown solr., 1930.

WICKHAM, Capt. Denis Henry.—B. 1896; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; Connaught Rangers, Sept., 1914; served B.E.F., France, 1915-18; mentd. in desps.; transfd. to Queen's Royal Regt., 1922; served with 5th K.A.R. Kenya, 1922-26; admstv. offr., Kenya col.; seconded to Somaliland, Apr., 1929.

WICKRAMASINGHA, Sena Pandukabhaya, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1922; attd. to Matara kach., Dec., 1922; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Apr., 1923; extra office ast., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1925; office ast., ditto, June, 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Feb., 1926; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, June, 1927; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1927; ditto, Point Pedro, Apr., 1929; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, Mar., 1930.
WICKRAMASINGHE, Reginald Hugh, B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.), F.R.A.S.—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1930; attd. Badulla kach., Nov., 1930; office ast., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1932.

WICKREMESINGHE, Cyril Leonard.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Apr., 1912; attd. Badulla kach., Apr., 1912; Matara kach., Oct., 1912; office ast. to gov't. agt., Uva, July, 1913; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; addnl. ast. gov't. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; ast. gov't. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1925; ast. gov't. agt., Puttalam, Oct., 1926; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1928; ag. gov't. agt., N.C.P., July, 1931.

WIDDUP, Major Charles Ponsonby, V.D.—B. 1884; clerical ast., treas., B. Guiana, 1902; 6th cls. clk., lands and mines dept., 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of police, 1903; sub-inspr. of police, 1904; qualified as musketry instr., Hythe, 1905; dist. inspr. of police, 1908; passed Hindustani exam., 1909; county inspr. of police, 1914; dep. inspr. gen., and supt. fire brig. B. Guiana, Dec., 1920; internat. prison confce., 1925; ag. inspr.-gen. pol. and commdt., local forces in 1924, 1926, 1927 and 1930.
WIGHTMAN, J. W. E.—Ast. dist. comsrs., Kenya, Apr., 1914; ag. res. comnrs., 1926.

WIGHTON, Alexander Ritchie, Mem., Transvaal Soc'y. of Acctnts.—Joined Impl. military rlys., S. Africa, June, 1902; audit inspr., ch. acctnt.'s dept., June, 1903; acctnt. engrnr.'s dept., Feb., 1904; ch. clk., ditto, July, 1908; inspr., dept. of audr., S. African rlys. and harbs., Apr., 1910; ch. inspr., ast. controller and audr.-gen.'s dept. Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; ch. inspr. (rlys. and harb. audit), cont. and audit office, July, 1916.

WIGLEY, Wilfred Murray, O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Derby schl.; held various clerical appts. under govt. St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1898; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1901; practised at bar of Leeward Is., for some years; ag. admstr., St. Kitts, puisne judge, Leeward Is. and atty-gen., on various occasions; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.
WILCOCKS, C., M.B., Ch.B. (Manch.).—B. 1896; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov. 1927; tuberculosis research offr., 1930.

WILCOCKS, CARL THEODORUS MULLER.—B. 1861; ed. Godbolt's Comm'l Acad., Graaff Reinet; represented Fauresmith including Jagersfontein and Koffyfontein in first Union parl.; M.L.A. for Winburg (O.F.S.), 1915-24; mem., rly. and harbrs. bd., 1924; pub. debt. commnr., 1925; admstr. O.F.S., Mar., 1929.

WILFORD, SIR THOMAS MASON, K.C.M.G. (1930), K.C. (1929).—B. 1870; ed. Christ Coll., Canterbury, N.Z.; M.P. for Wellington Suburbs, 1900 and for Hutt from 1903-29; ohmn., Wellington harbr. bd., 1908-9 (mem. 10 years); min. of just., marine, etc., in national govt., two years; leader of opposition and liberal party, 1920-25; min. of just. and defence, 1928-29; high comsnr. for N.Z. in London, 1929.
WILKIN, B. O., M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H. (Edin.).—B. 1889; mily. serv., 1914-20; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925.

WILKINSON, ALAN FREDERICK LAUDER.—B. 1892; ed. Royal Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; war serv. (Navy), 1914 to 1919; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, July, 1919; dist. comsnr., April, 1924.

WILKINSON, ERNEST EDWARD, M.B.E. (1922).—B. 1874; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to the bd. of agriculture, May, 1949; transf'd. to C.O., June, 1896; minor staff offr., ch. clk.'s dept., 1902; clk. to finance and concessions comtee., 1916; clk. for legal instruments, Sept., 1921; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1922; librarian, 26th Feb., 1923.
WILKINSON, RICHARD CLARY.—B. 1905; ed. Kings Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Dec., 1928.
WILKINSON, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1906; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1929; att., Kandy kach., Jan., 1930; office asst., Jaffna kach., Nov., 1931.
WILLAN, REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Oakham Schol. and Guy's Hospital; med. offr. to Baro-Kano rly., Nigeria, 1909-1911; joined West African med. staff, Jan., 1912.

WILLBOURN, ERIC STEWART, M.A. (Cantab.), F.G.S.—B. 1889; ast. geologist, F.M.S., May, 1914; 2nd. Heut., R.E. (Signal), Apr., 1918; demob., May, 1919; ag. geologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1922-Jly., 1923 and Jly., 1927-Feb., 1928; dir. geology survey, F.M.S., Dec., 1931.

WILLOCHS, MAJOR JAMES LUGARD, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, Feb., 1912; A.D.C. to G.O.C., N. Army, India, July, 1913; A.D.C. to G.O.C. Indian Army Corps, France, Sept., 1914; (twice ment. in desps.); brig. maj., 1916; brig. maj., Bermuda, Mar., 1919; ret., Feb., 1922; placed on R. of O.); A.D.C., Kenya, Feb., 1925; ag. sec., native affrs. dept., May, 1928; asst. comsnr., prison, 1932.
WILLDON, MARGARET ISABEL, M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; matron, Basutoland leper asylum, 1913.
WILLIAMS, A. D. J. R.—Med. offr., K.A.P., Aug., 1912; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dep. dir., med. services, 1926.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, O.B.E. (1918), M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1873; ed. Llandovery Coll.; ast. wks. man., Vulcan loo. works, Lancs.; inspr., N.Z. and W. W.O., 1915; section dir., Min'y. of Munitions, 1916; major, R.E., D.A.D., W.O., 1918; tech. head of engnr. inspection dept., C.A., 1919; visited Nigeria, 1920; Palestine and Cyprus, 1921; ch. inspgr. engr., 1922; pres. elect., Inst. of Loco. Engrs.

WILLIAMS, MAJOR C. T., O.B.E.—B. 1887; ed. St. John's Leatherhead; late 1st Batt. The E. Surrey Regt.; ret. with rank of major, 1925; serv. in France, Belgium, Italy and Russia; twice ment. in desps.; adjt., N. Territories constab., Apr., 1925; ag. comdt., N.T.C., for various periods; comsnr., pol., Gold Coast on disbandment of N.T.C., Apr., 1929; staff offr., Gold Coast pol., June, 1931.
WILLIAMS, D. L. GWYNNE.—B. 1886; solr. sup. ct., Eng., 1914; served European War, 1915-18; ct. martial offr., 1918; staff capt. (legal), 1919; conveyancer and regnr. of titles, Uganda, 1923.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD BRIDGEWATER.—B.1885; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; ast. dist. offr., Mar., 1910; 2nd ast. dist. offr., Larut, Dec., 1914; ast. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1917; ast. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Sept., 1918; mag. Seremban, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Tampin, Mar., 1920; dist. offr., Coast, Nov., 1920; collr., land rev., Seremban, Mar., 1922; ag. Br. res., Negri Semilan, in addn., Aug., 1925; offr., cls. I.B. legal advr. and judicil. commnr., Kelantan, June, 1928; comsnr., lands, F.M.S., Apr., 1932.

WILLIAMS, ERNEST HILLAS, B.A. (1st cls. hons., maths; 1st cls. hons. experimental science), Trinity Coll., Dublin.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1923; passed cadet, Dec., 1925; sec. to govt. trade loan, Jan., 1926; ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., May, 1926; ast. dist. offr., New Territories, Aug., 1928; ag. 3rd ast. col. sec., Aug., 1930; 2nd pol. mag., Sept., 1930; mag. and dist. offr., N. dist., New Territories, Apr., 1931.

WILLIAMS, ERIC TREGEAR.—B. 1890; ed. Bundell's schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1913; attd., col. sec. office, Dec., 1913; dist. offr., Oct., 1917; offr., cls. IV, S.S., Nov., 1920; ag. 1st ast. sec. (A), S.S., Oct., 1929; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1930; ag. comsnr., trade and cust., Johore, June, 1932.

WILLIAMS, ERNEST HILLAS, B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923; passed cadet, Dec., 1925; ast. to sec., for Chinese affrs., 1926; ast. dist. offr., New Territories, Aug., 1928; ag. 3rd ast. col. sec., 1930.

WILLIAMS, FRANK STANLEY.—B. 1894; ed. St. Paul's Schol., London; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; ast. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; senr. ast. audr., July, 1924; ag. audr. on various occasions from 1926; ag. audr., Mar. to Aug., 1930.

WILLIAMS, GILBERT NEVILLE, D.S.O., V.D.—Cape civ. serv., surv. office, 1892; dept. of agr., 1893; prin. clk., 1906; ch. clk., 1909; ch. elk., Union dept. of agr., 1912; ag. under-seer., 1919; confirmed, 1920; served, Bechuanaaland Rebellion, 1897 (med. and clasp); S. A. War, 1899-1900 (med. and three clasps); served with Union troops, S.W. Africa, 1914-15; A.A. and Q.M.G., 3rd divn., E. African Expedy. Force, 1917-19 (D.S.O., 3 times ment. in desps.); sec. for agr., 1st Aug., 1926; del. to Imp. Agrl. Confco., London, 1927.

WILLIAMS, JOHN BASIL.—B. 1906; ed. Marlborough, McGill Univ., Montreal and Cambridge; colonial exhibitioner, Trinity Coll., 1924-26; 2nd cls., nat. sci. tripes, pt. I., 1927; 2nd cls. pt. II., 1928, B.A. 1927; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 17th Oct., 1929.
WILLIAMS, J. H., M.I.C.E., M.Am.Soc.C.E.—B. 1873; res. engr., Singapore govt. rly.; engr.-in-ch., Canton-Hankow rly.; res. engr., Benue Bridge constrn., Nigeria, 1928.

WILLIAMS, O. G. R.—B. 1886; ed. Lancing Coll. and Oxford; exhibitioner, Hertford Coll., 1906; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1909; Aubrey Moore studentship, 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in sec.'s off., In. Rev., Oct., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Maitland, 4th June, 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Hewins, 26th Sept., 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 14th Jan., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 8th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. asst. sec., Feb., 1926.
WILLIAMS, PATRICK EDWARD WILLOWS.—B. 1904; ed. Shrewsbury Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WILLIAMS, PHILIP STANHOPE.—B. 1889; ed. at Harrow (head of the schl., 1907-08) and at Calais Coll., Camb. (schol. and Saver schol.), 1st cls. classical tripes, 1911; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1915; offr., cls. V., Jan., 1916; asst. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1921; 2nd mag., Penang, Mar., 1925; 2nd mag., Sp'ore, July, 1929; offr. cls. II., Dec., 1929; ag. legal advr. and judl. comsnr., Kelantan, Sept., 1930.

WILLIAMS, R. GREGSON.—Ed. at Rossall Schl., Caius Coll., Camb.; B.A. 3rd cls. hons., Science Tripes; Royal Schl. of Mines, A.R.S.M., M. Inst. M.M., 1st cls. diploma; post-graduate course, Mysore goldfields, India; asst. rea., N. Nigeria, Mch., 1911; ag. inspr. of mines, June, 1911; ag. chief survr., Sept., 1911; contr., mines, Tanganyika Territory, 1926; comsnr., of mines, 1929.
WILLIAMS, SIDNEY BRUCE.—B. 1880; S.A. constab., 1901; Swaziland pol., 1908; clk., 1911; R.J.P., 1916; dep. asst. comsnr., 1928; asst. comsnr., 1930.
WILLIAMS, THOMAS ALFRED.—B. 1875; accntg. clk., Basutoland, 1905; accnt., 1913; finan. sec., 1916; transf'd. Bech. Prot. as audr., H.C. Territories, July, 1927.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1880; ed. Carnarvon County Schl., Univ. Coll., N. Wales and Univ. of Liverpool (engnr. dept.); attd. Portsmouth dockyard, 1909; sent to Hong Kong for electrification H.M. naval yd., May 1909; ch. asst. engnr., Shanghai munici. elec. dept., 1911; engnr.-in-ch. and man., Penang munici. electy. supply and tramways undertakings, 1920; engnr. and man., Penang Hill rly., Oct., 1923; dir., elect. dept., F.M.S., Jan., 1927.

WILLIAMSON, E.—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., Transvaal, Mar., 1903; treasy., Transvaal, July, 1904; senr. clk., Jan., 1919; prin. clk., Apr., 1921; sec. cabinet comtes. on expenditure, 1922-24; ch. clk., estimates, June, 1929; under-sec. for finance, July, 1931.
WILLIAMSON, J., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Cert., Lond. S. Hyg. & T.M. (distinc.)—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WILLIMOTT, STANLEY GORDON.—B. 1899; ed. Liverpool Univ. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; war serv., 1917-19; B.Sc., 1st cls., schl. of chemistry, Liv. Univ., 1921; A.L.C., 1921; Ph.D. (Liv.), 1923; research schl., St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; Ph.D. (Cantab.) in agric. science, 1927; research chem., Imp. Chem. Industries, 1928-29; govt. analyst, Cyprus, Nov., 1929; author of numerous papers in scientific and med. journals.

WILLINGDON, 1ST VISCOUNT (cr. 1924); 1st Baron of Ratton (cr. 1910); Rt. Hon. Freeman Freeman-Thomas, P.C. (1931); G.B.E. (1917); G.C.S.I. (1918); G.C.I.E. (1913).—B. 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Brassey when gov. of Victoria, 1895; M.P., Hastings, 1900-06; Bodmin divn. of Cornwall, 1906-10; jun. lord of treas., 1905-12; J.P.; gov. of Bombay, 1913-19; ditto, Madras, 1919-24; major, Sussex Imperial Yeomanry; lord-in-waiting to His Majesty; govr.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1926-31; viceroy of India, 1931.

WILLIS, MAURICE A., M.B.E. (1933)—B. 1900; ent. M.O.D., G.P.O., May, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., Apr., 1918; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd., P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; transf'd., C.O., May, 1924; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932.

WILLIS, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1897; ed. London; clk., treasy. and cust. house, Turks and Caicos Is., Jan., 1925; ag. asst. treas., postmr., collr. of cust. regurl. of shipping and gen. man., savings bank, May, 1928 to Aug., 1928; clk. to comsnr., Aug., 1928.

WILLMOT, ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1881; ed. Lancing Coll. and Aspatria Agric. Coll., Cumberland; asst. survr., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1907; dist. engnr., P.W.D., May, 1908; ag. asst., D.P.W., Apr.-Nov., 1913; engnr.-in-charge, Kampala Port Bell rly. constrn., Dec., 1913; served with 4th K.A.R. in E. African Campaign, May, 1915 to Dec., 1918 (twice ment. in despatches); ag. D.D.P.W., Oct., 1920 to Apr., 1922; D.D.P.W., June, 1923; ag. D.F.W. on various occasions, 1923-24, 1926-27 and 1929.
WILLMOT, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.D., D.P.H., L.R.C.P.—Aast. health offr., dept. of pub. health, Union of S. Africa, 1913.
WILLMOTT, L. A., M.B., B.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S.—B. 1899; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925.

WILLOUGHBY, GODFREY POUNTNEY, M.Sc. Tech., M.Sc., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1888; ed. Taunton Schl. and Victoria Univ., Manchester; B.Sc., 1st cl. hons., elect. engnr., 1909; Schuster Schol., 1909; tech. asst. post office, Union of S. Africa, Mar., 1913; lect., elect. engnrng., phys., maths., Durban Tech. Coll., Apr., 1919; asst. engnr. and tech. educn. offr., post office, Union of S. Africa, Dec., 1920; elect. inspr., Kenya, and ch. teleg. engnr., Kenya and Uganda, Apr., 1926.

WILMOT, AUBREY CHARLTON.—Cape civ. serv., 1900; transf'd., Transvaal, 1902; ch. clk., mast. sup. ct., 1910; ch. clk., land bank, 1912; asst. gen. man., 1921; gen. man., 1924; man. dir., 1930; chmn., farmers' spec. relief bd., 1931.
WILSON, CAPT. ARTHUR OTTENBURN, A.C.A., A.I.S.A.—B. 1888; on milv. serv., 1915-19; asst. audr. ext. audit dept., F.M.S., May, 1920; sec. to P.W.D. as finan. offr., Sept., 1921; asst. audr.,

WILSON, BASIL MAYOR, M.B., Ch.B. (1901), D.P.H. (1911), M.D. (1911).—B. 1878; ed. Malvern Coll. (Faber Exhib.), and Univ. of Manchester; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1904; dist. med. offr., Levuka, 1907; dist. med. offr., Ba., 1915; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1918-19; served with B.E.F. in France; res. med. offr., col. hosp., Suva, M.O.H. and bacteriologist, Fiji, 1922; prin. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, mem., leg. coun., Br. WILSON, CECIL SAMUEL, M.B., Ch.B. (Dub. Univ.).—B. 1897; med. offr., F.M.S., Jly., 1926; surg., F.M.S., Aug., 1928; surg., N. Sembilan, Apr., 1931.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES, Barrister-at-Law (King's Inns).—B. 1889; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (siz., jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. mod., B.A.); cert B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1913; attd. Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Penang, Dec., 1914; passed cadet, Jan., 1915; on milly serv., 2nd lieut., 6th K.S.L.I., July, 1915; France, Feb., 1916; offr., cls. V, May, 1916; lieut., July, 1916; capt., Feb., 1919; demob., Mar., 1919; ment. in dep., dep. contr., lab., Seremban, June, 1919; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., Dec., 1920; 2nd lieut. and adj., M.V.I., Perak, Apr., 1921; emigr. agt. in India, Aug., 1922; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1925; accomp. contr., lab. to Delhi, Feb. to Mar., 1926 and to Simla, Aug. to Sept., 1927; state treas., Perak, Oct., 1928; chmn. sany. bd., Larut and Matang in addn., 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1929; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang in addn., Nov., 1929; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1930; ag. regist., sup. ct., S'pore, Jan., 1931; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., S'pore, July, 1932.

WILSON, C. J., M.C., M.A., B.Ch. (Cantab), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., certif., L.S.T.M. (distinc.).—B. 1879; med. offr., E.A.P., May, 1911; dep. dir., med. serv., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., med. and sany. servs., 1926; prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Dec., 1928; dir., med. and health services, S.S. and advr., do., F.M.S. in addn., 1932.

WILSON, CHARLES LIVINGSTONE.—B. 1874; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; entd. civ. serv., Grenada, as 2nd clk., G.P.O., Aug., 1892; ag. ch. clk., 1893; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; ex. off. sec., gen. bd. of health and bd. of educa., 1897-1903; ag. ch. clk. to gov. of Windward Is., 1907-8; ch. clk., govt. office, St. Vincent, clk. of couns., man. of printing sub-dept., and sec., hurricane loan bd., 1907-11; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk.of couns. and man., printing sub. dept., 1911-19; col. postmstr., Grenada, 1919; a dir. of the pub. offrs. guarantee fund, 1919; sec. of reception comte., visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to Grenada, Sept., 1920.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and elk., Br. Solomon Is., June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, Jan., 1915 and Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, Nov., 1916; ag. collr., cust., May, 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., Apr., 1919 and Dec., 1924; acctnt. and 1st clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1920; visiting dist. offr., Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, Apr., 1923; dep. comanr. for W. Pacific, July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, Dec., 1923; ag. govt. sec., Mar., 1925; ag. res. comanr., Dec., 1925.
WILSON, D. E., M.B., B.Ch. (Aberd.).—B. 1902; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.
WILSON, DONALD BAGSTER, M.A., B.Ch. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1929.
WILSON, ERNEST PIERRE FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed Amersham Gram. Schl.; clk. acctnt-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, 1914-1922; cler. offr., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1922.

WILSON, FRANK KEESHAW, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1891; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; attd. col. secretariat, Dec., 1914; attd., censor's office, S'pore, Jly., 1915; supt., posts and teles. and ast. supt. lands, K. Bahru, Aug., 1917; sheriff and dep. regiar., sup. ct., S'pore, Oct., 1920; chmn., rubber assessment comtee., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Aug., 1929; ag. regist., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1931; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Bath; apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1903; clk., ch. sec's. off., 1904; ag. ast. sec., ch. sec's. off., Jan. to July, 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; collr., cust., Sep., 1919; ag. comanr. in 1923, 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; ch. collr., cust., and excise, July, 1924; regir., Br. ships, regr., Cyprus ships, inspr., fisheries; mem., leg. coun., 1925-31; title altered to comptir., cust. and excise, July, 1927; regist., partnerships, Apr., 1929.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—B. 1887; ed. Chigwell Schl., Essex; ast. dist. comanr., G. Coast, Aug., 1914; dist. comanr., Jan., 1921.
WILSON, G. R. C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. of London S.T.M.—Tempy. capt., R.A.M.C., May, 1915 to Feb., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919; senr. med. offr., 1927.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A., 1885; called to bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cols., June, 1895; legal ast. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal ast. on high comanr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., in 1903, 1904 and 1906; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (rep. O.R.C. on rly. comte.); ret.; was sec. to the Royal Colonial Institute.
WILSON, J., M.A.—B. 1903; mast., educn. dept., N.T., Gold Coast, Sept., 1929; inspr., schls., Apr., 1931.

WILSON, LIEUT.-COL. JOHN WOLSELEY, M.C., G.C.S.I. (1929), G.C.I.E. (1923), P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1900), etc.—B. 1876; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; 2nd lieut., R.M.L.I., 1890; lieut., 1896; capt., 1901; served S. Africa, 1899-01; (severely wounded), despr., Queen's med. 5 clasp, D.S.O.; A.D.C. to Gov., N.S.W., 1903-09; temp. lieut.-col., R.M. and served through Galipoli operns., 1914-15 (despr.); served in France (severely wounded), 1915-16; M.P. (Reading). 1913-22; S. Portsmouth, 1922-23; parly. asst. sec. to war cabinet, 1918; chmn., national maritime bd., 1919; parly. sec. to miny. of shipping, 1919; parly. sec. to treasy. and ch. unionist whip, 1921-23; Gov., Bombay, 1923-28; Gov., Queensland, 1932.

WILSON, CAPT. LOGIE WEIR.—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick Sch., St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, Royal Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Royal Irish Rifles, 1917; asst. dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, July, 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dist. commnr., 1921; dist. commnr., Aug., 1926.

WILSON, REGINALD CHARLES, D.Sc., F.G.S., Assoc.Inst.M.M.—B. 1888; ed. Scotch Coll. and Melbourne Univ.; capt., Royal Welsh Fusiliers and R.E., France, 1915-18; geologist, Portuguese E. Africa, 1911-14; geologist, geol. survey, Nigeria, 1919-27; dir., 1927.

WILLSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, G.C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B., (1927), K.C.M.G. (1929), K.B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.K., 1893; capt., 1904; major 1913; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in S. Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1915; Commandeur de la Couronne (Belgian), 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; French croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comte., 1911-14; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff cffr., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen., gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comte., 19th Nov., 1918; gov., Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, 1st Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the cols., 6th Aug., 1925.

WILSON, WILLIAM ALFRED.—B. 1888; clk., secretariat, Nyasaland Prot., 1912; legal dept., 1912; registr., high ct., 1914-20 (also offr. rec. in banktey., offl. admtr. of intestate estates, registr., patents, designs and trade marks, etc.); sec., bd. of arbitrn., 1917-19; registr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1920 (also registr., bills of sale and deeds of arrangement, dep. registr., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa); A.G.S. med. (Nyasaland 1915).
WILSON, WILLIAM GLASSFORD WALKER, M.I.L.—B. 1894; ed. Kinlossack Acad.; war serv., 1914-19; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1923; wks. man., 1927; ag. ch. mech. engrn., 1929.

WILTSHIRE, ERIC.—B. 1893; ed. Monmouth Grammar Schl.; articled pupil to county surv. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; dist. engrn., Nov., 1929; ag. asst. dir., P.W.D., 1927, 1928, 1930 and 1931.

WILTSHIRE, H. G., M.A., M.B., B.Chir. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; exhibr., Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (coll. prize); L.S.T.M. certif. with distinc. (2nd place); war serv., 1914-19; France, Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine (1914 Star with clasp); bacteriological specialist, R.A.M.C., 1916-19; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1924; do., Zanzibar, 1930; pathologist, 1932.
WINSER, CYRIL LEIGH, C.M.G., (1928), M.V.O. (1920).—Priv. sec. to govts. of S. Australia since May, 1915.

WINSON, VICTOR HANTOR, B.Sc., A.K.C., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1887; ed. Tiffins Schl., Kingston-on-Thames and King's Coll., London (1st cls. hon., elect. engrng.); 2nd cls. engr., G.P.O., engrng. dept., Oct., 1899; asst. engrn., July, 1911; suptd. posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1913; war serv., Aug., 1917-Apr., 1919; suptd. posts and tels., Jan., 1922; senr. engrn., posts and tels., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct., 1926; senr. engrn., posts and tels., Penang, July, 1928; ditto, F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1930.

WINSTEDT, RICHARD OLAF, C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. at Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; passed cadet, 1904; class V., Jan., 1910; sec. to comte., for Malay studies in addn., Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Dec., 1913; federal examr. in Malay in addn., Dec., 1913; class III., asst. dir., educn. S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addn.; cls. II., Nov., 1919; fed. examr. in Malay, S. S'tlimts., and F.M.S., June, 1920; seconded to act as prim., Raffles' Coll., S'pore, June, 1921; offg. as sec. to high commr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addn., Mar., 1923; dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. Ia, July, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comte., in addn., July, 1924; chmn., tech., educn. comte., in addn., July, 1924; rep., F.M.S., govt. at C.O. confce., Apr., to July, 1927; chmn., comte., med. research, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., Aug., 1928; chmn., Malayan delegation to 4th Pacific serv. cong., Batavia, May, 1929; rep. F.M.S. at C.O. confce., June, 1930; gen. advr. Johore, 1931; author of an "English Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Saws and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melayu.
WINSTEDT, SARA, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1888; med. offr., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, 1913; S'pore, 1921.
WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd clk., post office, Dominica, 1920; asst. clk., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. clk. Sept., 1924; ag. audit clk., Jan., Apr., 1927.

WISE, KENBRICK STANTON.—B. 1881; ed. Dover, Cambridge, and London; M.B., B.S., B.S.C. Lond. Univ.; M.R.C.S., England; L.R.C.P., Lond.; D.P.H.; govt. bacteriologist, Br. Guiana, 1906; govt. med. offr. of health, 1912; surg.-gen. and registr.-gen., 1914; surg.-gen., Trinidad, Aug., 1919.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mch., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comte., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917, to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comte., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comte., May, 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, last Apr., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.
WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, M.B.E. (1932), M.B.E. (1918), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; 3rd cls. Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.
WITTEENOOM, HON. SIR K. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia,

WODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Josceline Heywood, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comanr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; commd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in commd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasp); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WOODEMAN, Guy Stanley.—B. 1886; ed. Rosall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office ast. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; ast. comanr. of excise attached to the office of the excise comanr., Apr., 1913; ast. comanr. of excise, Cent. Dir., Jan., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; attd. ch. censor's office, Sept., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1923; dep. oollr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. comanr., excise, Nov., 1926; comanr., excise, Sept., 1927; govt. agt., S.P., Nov., 1930; prin. colls., cust., Nov., 1931.

WOHLMANN, Ward George.—B. 1872; ed. N.Z. state schls.; N.Z. permanent arty., 1894-95; N.Z. pol., 1895; sergt., 1906; senr. sergt., 1914; sub-inspr., 1918; inspr., 1921; seprt., 1926; comanr., 1930; was comanr. of pol., Samoa, 1920-22.

WOLFE, Edward Dudley Corsoaden, C.M.G. (1928).—Tonbridge schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langs., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langs., tripos (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); M.A. (1925); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; sec., P.W.D. enquiry comanr., 1901; regiar., land ot., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1902; ag. ast. regiar.-gen., 1902; regiar., land ot., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and ast. supt. of pol. 1903-5; seconded to Transvaal govt. serv., 1905; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907; ag. regiar.-gen., 1907; ast. regiar.-gen., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907 to 1909; head of sanitary dept., 1909; postmr.-gen., 1913; ag. col. treas., 1914-16; 1st pol. mag., 1917; ag. col. treas., and custodian of enemy property, 1917 and 1918; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1918; inspr.-gen., pol., 1920; ag. col. sec., 1921; attended internat. pol. confce., New York, 1925.

WOLFE, Henry, M.Sc. (Agric.), Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. astt., dept. of agr. S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir. agr., Tanganika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17; dep. dir. agr. (plant industry), Kenya, 1931.

WOLFFE, Phineas Ernest.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow Acad.; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; ag. office supt., 1917; land astt., 1919; regiar., titles, doc. and coast land titles, 1920; liquidator of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.

WOLSELEY-BOURNE, James Frederick, M.C.—B. 1878; 2nd lieut. Royal Berks. Regt.; lieut., S. African Constab.; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's and King's medals); capt., K.A.R.; served in E. Africa, 1914-1918 ("1914-15" Star, War and Victory medals); commanded a column in operations against Aulihan (Somali) tribe, Jubaland, 1917-18 (M.C., African Gen. Serv. medal); astt. comanr., pol., Kenya, Jan., 1927; dep. comanr., do., Jan., 1932.

WOOD, Alan Eustace.—Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; scholar of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; 1st mods., 2nd lit. hum.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrants, 1908; ag. ast. regiar.-gen., 1908-10; passed cadet, 1st Apr., 1910; 2nd ast. regiar.-gen., 1911; ag. ast. dist. offr., South dist., New Territories, 1911 to 1912; passed in Hakka, June, 1912; title of ast. regiar.-gen. altered to 2nd astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. 1st astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. chief astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., on various occasions, 1914-21; ag. dist. offr., 1915; passed in Pekingese, June, 1916; sent to Japan to study Japanese, 1916; passed in Japanese, May, 1918; ag. dist. offr., Taipo, 1919 to 1920; ag. dist. offr., Taipo in addn., 1922; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923 and 1924; ag. dir., educn., Dec., 1925; dir., educn., Jan., 1926; ch. astt. to sec., Chinese affrs., May, 1931; sec. for Chinese affrs. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1932.

WOOD, Bernard Reader.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Schol., Sid. Suss. Coll., Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; astt. conserv. Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conserv., 1918; conserv. of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; reverted to Indian forest serv., Oct., 1927; conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1928; mem., bd. of exams., Hindi and Urdu, Apr., 1928; ag. off. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1928; chmn., local govt. bd., 1929-30; offl. mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1930; do., leg. coun., Jan., 1931; J.P., Aug., 1932.

WOOD, Harry Edwin.—B. 1881; ed. Manchester Grammar Schol. and Owens Coll. Manchester. (B.Sc., 1902, M.Sc., 1905); astt. lect., dept. of physics, Manchester Univ., 1905; ch. astt., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1906; ch. astt., Union observatory, Johannesburg, 1912; served, German E. Africa campaign, 1916; Union astronomer, Jan., 1928.

WOOD, Capt. John Calder, M.C., R. of O., R.E.—Qual. Land Surveyor, S. Africa—B. 1883; ed. Robt. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; astt., R. Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1903; cadastral and trig. survr., F.M.S., 1911; war serv., France and England, 1915-19; offr.-in-ch. cadastral framework traverse, Gold Coast survey, 1923; dep. survr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926; survr.-gen., 1930.

WOOD, John R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1896; 1st cl. class. tripes, 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; called to the bar, 1919; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; regiar. of land ot., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. land ot., 1903-4; astt. land offr. in N. T., Jan., 1905; ag. astt. reg.-gen., 1905; mem. of squatters' bd., 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1908; dep. regiar. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1908; 2nd pol. mag., 1909; ag. dir. of educn., 1911 to 1912; ag. 1st pol. mag., on several occasions; 1st pol. mag., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920-22. and in 1925; chmn., Sui An piracy comm., 1922; puisane judge, 1928; ag. ch. just., 1927 and 1930.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queen's coll., Camb.; lat cls. math. tripos, pt. I.; 2nd cls. math. tripes., pt. 2; B.A., 1912; asst. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1914-20; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. clk. of comm. for various periods, 1922 and 1929-32; senr. asst. col. sec., 1930; ag. prin. asst. col. sec. in 1931 and 1932.

WOOD, CAPT. REGINALD CECIL MERVYN.—B. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; coy. commdr., 1923; asst. treas., Kenya, 1923; estab. offr., secretariat, Mar., 1928.

WOODHOUSE, JOHN LESLIE.—B. 1892; ed. Merchant Taylors Sch.; asst. transport offr., with rank of lieut., Uganda Transport Corps, Nov., 1914; ment. in desps. for service in German E. Africa, Nov., 1916; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, Dec., 1916; 2nd grade adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1920; dist. offr., June, 1926.

WOODMAN, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1872; ed. prte. schl., Winchester Coll. and St. Mary's Hosp.; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.S.A.; attd. to R.A.M.C. at Fort Pitt Hosp., Chatham, during S. African War; London Schl. of Trop. Med. exam., 1909; med. offr. and radiologist and dist. commr., Br. Honduras, 1910-15; med. offr., hosp., gal., etc., Hong Kong; seconded for serv. in war, 1918-19; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.), in charge of sanitary company, Salonica and Constantinople; in charge, Belize hosp., Jan. to June, 1928; Bahamas med. offr., Out Islands, June to Sept., 1928; ag. res. surg. and radiologist, Bahamas, Sept., 1928-Aug., 1929; ag. dir. med. services, Aug. to Nov., 1929.

WOODS, SIR WILFRID WENTWORTH, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G. (1926)—B. 1876; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cls. final hon. schl.; mod. hist., 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Sept., 1901; asst. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; asst. col. auditor, Ceylon, Feb., 1908; ag. col. auditor on several occasions; col. auditor, Aug., 1915; temp. employed at W.O., Dec., 1917 to 1919; ag. P.M.C., Nov., 1920 to Apr., 1921; col. treas., Feb., 1922; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Oct., 1926; finan. sec., July, 1931.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL, M.C.—B. 1893; on milly. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd. Kandy Kach., June, 1921; ag. office asst. to gov't. agt. N.C.Prov., Sept., 1922; 4th ass't. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd ass't. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attd. C.O., 1925 to June, 1926; addnl. ass't. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd ass't. col. sec., Oct., 1926; asst. gov't. agt., N'Eilya, May, 1927; asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Sept., 1928; sec. to gov't., July, 1931.

WOOLLEY, CAPT. HUBERT WORSLEY, M.C., B.A. (Oxon.), Dip. in Forestry (Cantab.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1887; assumed duties in F.M.S., Sept., 1921; ag. dep. conserv., forests, Feb., 1927; dep. conserv., forests, Jly., 1929; title changed to conserv. of forests, Jan., 1930.

WORKMAN, CHARLES RUFUS MARSHALL, C.B.E. (1927).—B. 1874; ed. Christ Ch., Oxford, M.A.; called to bar, Inner Temple; pol. offr., Ocean Is., 1909; dep. commr., Western Pacific, 1911; admistr., Nauru, 1914; ag. res. commr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., May, 1916 to Feb., 1917; ag. res. commr., Solomon Is., June, 1917; res. commr., Apr., 1919; judi. commr., Feb., 1920; col. sec., Gambia, Sept., 1921; ag. gov. on various occasions, 1921-28.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Kamper, July, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Negri Semilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; asst. dir. of educn. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; asst. dir. educn. (Chinese), F.M.S. and S.S., in 1927 and 1930; ag. 1st mag., Ipoh, Nov., 1930; regiir. sup. ct., K. Lumpur, May, 1932.

WORLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1896; ed. St. Paul's Sch.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in desps.; Ireland, 1919; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. asst. audr., Nov., 1925; seconded to Seychelles, Oct., 1925 to June, 1926; on sp. duty, Seychelles, Oct.-Nov., 1929; audr., Br. Guiana, May, 1932.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEABURN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1910; attd. col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office asst. to gov't. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1911; asst. land settm. offr., Jan., 1912; office asst. to gov't. agt., Central Prov., July, 1914; asst. censor, Dec., 1914; on milly. duty, Feb., 1915; asst. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1921; asst. stimt. offr., Jan., 1924; asst. gov't. agt., Hambantota, May, 1924; asst. gov't. agt., Matare, Jan., 1929; cls. I, grade II, 1931; ag. gov't. agt., W.P., Apr., 1932.

WORSLEY, RICHARD ROBERT LE GETT, Ph.D. (Lond.), D.I.C., A.R.C.S., B.Sc.—B.1899; ed. St. Paul's and R. Coll. of Sci.; R.E.O.C.B., 1918-1919; sub. dir., chem. sect., miny. of agr., Egypt, 1926; biochemist, E. African agr. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOCELYN, O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica, govt. lab. and ap. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. socy., 1906; jnr. asst. chem., Jamaica, 1902; asst. chem., 1906; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmar., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; commr. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food commr., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comm. on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyassaland, Dec., 1920; dir. agr., Trinidad, 1930; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases" and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."

WRIGHT, MAJOR ANDREW BARKWORTH, C.B.E. (1932), M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar (1918), R. of O.—B. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (schol.); served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, June, 1922; asst. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; ag. priv. sec. to high commr., and ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1927-32; ch. asst. sec., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1930; ag. commr., Mar.-June, 1932.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR ALBAN, B.A.—B. 1887 ed. St. Edmund's Sch., Canterbury; open exhibn., St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1906; lieut., 3rd Bn., Bedfordshire Regt. (S.R.), 1909; 2nd cls. hons. schl., mod. hist., Oxford, 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, Fiji, 1912; ag. sub-inspr., constab., 1912; ag. clk., ex. coun. and 1st cls. clk., col. sec't. office, 1913; passed cadet, 1914; ag. dist. commr., Rewa and prov. commr., Naitasiri, 1915; 2nd grade dist. commr., 1915; dist. commr., Nadi, 1915; dist. commr., Colo East, 1916; on mily. duty, 1917; 2nd lieut., 6th Bn. Rifle Brigade, 1917; on active serv., France, 1917; dist. commr., Macuata, 1920; ditto (1st grade), Nadroga and prov. commr., Nadroga and Colo West, 1922; ditto, Colo North, 1923; ag. astt. col. sec., Jan., 1927; astt. col. sec., Jan., 1928; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1929; 1st astt. col. sec., Jan., 1931; sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1932; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD.—M.A., F.I.C., F.C.S.; B. 1880; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. So. Tripos), 1902; M.A. 1916; astt. analyst and science master, Trinidad, Apr., 1908; ag. govt. analyst and prof. of chemistry, Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Kadavu, July-Aug., 1918; govt. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agr. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, JAMES ROGERS, I.S.O. (1931).—B. 1878; ed. at C.M.S. Grammar Schl., Sierra Leone; 2nd clk., queen's adv. ofce, June, 1896; transf'd, gov't's office, July, 1895; astt. mast., circuit ct., Oct., 1903; pol. clk. and clk. of request, Nov., 1908; J.P. for Freetown pol. dist., July, 1909; dep. coroner, Freetown pol. dist., Aug., 1909 and in Apr., 1928; ch. clk. master's office, Jan., 1914; commr. of oaths and affidavits, sup. ct., Nov., 1915; dep. mast. and regisr., sup. ct. and regisr., prize ct., May, 1920; commr., ct. of requests, Freetown pol. dist., Apr., 1924; mast. and regisr., sup. ct., regisr., trade marks, curator of intestate estates and adminstr., civ. servants' estates, May, 1921.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, CAPT. RODNEY GEORGE, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Schl., stud. engnr. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E. (S.R.), 1909; engnr. on survey and constrn. of Magadi rly. and water wks., 1911-14; R.E., 1914-22; served Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded, twice ment. in desps., M.C.; div. offr., R.E., Londonderry and Enniskillen Divs., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; supt. pub. wks., Antigua, July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, Aug., 1922; astt. D.P.W., B. Guiana, Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W., Apr. to Sept., 1927; dep. dir., pub. wks., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931.
WROUGHTON, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1877; med. offr., Basutoland, 1908.
WUPPERMAN, RUDOLF CARL, M.B., Ch.B., Edin., B. 1883; entered med. serv., Trinidad, Oct., 1909; astt. res. surg., colonial hosp., Port of Spain, Oct., 1914; res. surg., col. hosp., May, 1923.

WYATT, ALBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1900; ed. Leamington Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., Hampshire Regt., 1919-26; 2nd K.A.R., 1923-26; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926; astt. dist. offr., Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Biharamulo, June, 1930.

WYNNE, MERVYN LLWELYN.—B. 1891; ed. Corrig Schl., Ireland, Ellesmere Coll., Shropshire, and in Hanover, Munich and Edinburgh; Malayan pol. serv., 1911; astt. commr., pol., Selangor, 1910; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1916; served in France 1917-18; comdt., pol. depot, Kuala Lumpur, 1920; commr., Pol., Trengganu, 1923; dir., crim. intell., F.M.S., 1928; commr., pol., Johore, 1929; dir., crim. intell., S.S., June, 1931.

WYNNE-JONES, EDWARD IRVINE.—B. 1896; ed. Oxford and Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1920; ag. dist. offr., South, June, 1922; astt. col. secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; dist. offr., North, May, 1923; ditto, South, Dec., 1926; astt., col. secretariat, July, 1928; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1928; extra astt. col. sec., Nov., 1928; 2nd astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., in 1929; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk., couns., 1929; dist. offr., North, 1930; 2nd pol. mag., Feb., 1932.

YATES, GEO. W.—B. 1872; ed. London pub. and high schls.; ent. civ. serv. of Ont. as priv. sec. to various mins. from Feb., 1899 to Oct., 1911; accompanied Hon. F. Cochrane, Ont. min. of lands, forests and mines, to Ottawa on latter accepting portfolio of min. of rlys. and canals in Borden govt.; cont. priv. sec., min. of rlys. and canals until formation of union govt., 1917, when became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Robt. Borden; astt. deputy minister of railways and canals, 1920.

YATES, JOSEPH MERVYN ST. JOHN, B.A.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1903; northern circuit, 1903-15; H.M. Forces, 1915-19; regi-gen., Bahamas, Feb., 1920; ag. stip. magis., Mar., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1920; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar., 1921; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; seconded for serv. as ag. astt. to atty.-gen., Jamaica, Sept., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, 1923; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1923 to Jan., 1924; ag. judge, Kingston et., Nov., 1924 to Oct., 1925; ag. puline judge, Oct., 1926; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1926; puline judge, Gold Coast, 1928.
YATES, WILLIAM STEWART, B.A.—B. 1905; ed. Coatham Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1928; astt. dist. offr., Apr., 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. regisr., Sept., 1932.
YOUNG, ALFRED ERNEST, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1891; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, SIR ALFRED CARNEY, KT. BACH. (1923), K.C., B.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councila, 1892; priv. sec. to admistr. of the govt., 1895; ag. regi-gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; admistr. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1911, and in Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914; ag. chief just., Jan., 1916 and from Mar., 1919 to May, 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Feb., 1921; chief just., Fiji, 1923; ret. June, 1929.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON, G.C.M.G. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.K. (1918), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskilling; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdt., mil. pol., Kyrena, Aug., 1878; astt. commr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; commr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; oh. sec., 1894; admistr. the govt., July... to Dec., 1896; Jan. to Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connctn. with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec., S. Stiltna., June, 1906; admtd. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Stiltna., Aug., 1911; assumed govt., Sept., 1911; also high commr. for Malay States and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo and Sarawak; ret. Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.T. BACH (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1909; commr. of crown lands and immigr., 1912-14; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915-18; mem. of comte., for stilttn. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906; asst. atty.-gen., 1913; senr. res. mag., 1917; agt. atty.-gen. on various occasions; pol. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1922; puisne and circuit judge, Sierra Leone, 1930.

YOUNG, MAJOR SIR HUBERT WINTHROP, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1923), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; command., R. Garrison Arty., 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; asst. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb.-Dec., 1915 (1914-15 Star); asst. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, Dec., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir., local resources, Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in desps.); gen staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejaz operations, Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., 3rd class Order of Naisha); pres., local resources bd., Damascus, Oct., 1918; seconded to F.O., Feb., 1919 to Mar., 1921; seconded to C.O., Middle East Dept. as asst. sec., Mar., 1921 to Dec., 1926; accompanied Mr. Churchill to Cairo confere., Apr., 1921; visited Palestine and Iraq on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv., Lausanne for Turkish peace treaty., Jan., 1922 and at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier dispute on various occasions during 1925 and 1926; ret. from army and apptd. col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1927; couns. to high commr., Iraq, Sept., 1929; ag. high commr., on four occasions; envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary, Baghdad, Oct., 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Nyasaland Prot., 1932.
YOUNG, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—Mem., N.Z. parl. since 1911; min. of health, 1926-28; min. of industries and commerce, 1928; min. of health, min. of immigr. in coalition miny., 1931.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Univs.; late house surg., Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on resignation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919; asst. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, JOHN MOULD.—B. 1875; Cape civ. serv., June, 1893; clk., G.P.O., July, 1894; clk., native affs. dept., Cape Town, Jan., 1896; asst. res. mag., Tsomo, Oct., 1896; ag. asst. res. mag., Kokstad, Mar., 1898; clk. and asst. res. mag., Tsomo, June, 1898; clk., P.W.D., Cape Town, Feb., 1899; senr. clk., Umtata, May, 1900; clk. and asst. res. mag., Idutywa, Oct., 1900; res. mag., Flagstaff, June, 1906; ldt gde. res. mag., Lusikisiki, Sept., 1920; senr. gde. mag., Umtata, Apr., 1923; sp. O gde. mag., Umtata, Sept., 1928; pres., native appl. ct., Cape and O.F.S., Jan., 1929; ch. native commr., Natal, July, 1931.

YOUNG, MARK ARTHURSON, G.M.G. (1931).—B. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. ast. to govt. agt., N.Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; agt. ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th ast. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and clk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd ast. sec. col., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1915-1919; 3rd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd ast. col. sec., July, 1920; ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Mar., 1923; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. gov. in 1929 and 1930; ch. sec., Palestine, 1930; admtd. govt. for various periods, 1930-32.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.Ch. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhib., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; chn. ast. children and ear depts., St. Thomas's hosp., 1922; R.M.O., Ealing hosp., 1922-23; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923; ag. R.S.O., Zanzibar, 1927 and 1930.

YOUNG, WILLIAM, M.B., Ch.B. ( Glas.).—B. 1892; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; med. offr., K. Lipis, Nov., 1922; ditto, Taiping, Oct., 1924; ditto, Larut, May, 1925; ditto, B. Gajal, Mar., 1927; ditto, B. Padang, May, 1928; ag. sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1928; med. offr., Larut, Aug., 1929; ag. ch. med. offr., soc. hyg., F.M.S., May, 1930.
YOUNG, WILLIAM ARTHUR, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.B., B.S.—B. 1890; prof. of bacteriology, Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, Mar., 1927.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. ast. govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; commr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admstr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. govr., Leeward Is., 1909; admstr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. govr., Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; govr., Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; assumed govt., May, 1915; ret., 1920.
YOUNGMAN, WILLIAM, Ph.D., D.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1880; Indian agrl. serv., 1918-30; dir., agr., Ceylon, 1931.

ZAMMIT, HON. PROFESSOR SIR THEMIS TOCLES, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G. (1911), M.D. (hon.) D.Litt. (Oxon.).—B.1864; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1882; grad. in medicine, 1889; govt. analyst, 1891-1920; curator, Valletta Museum, 1905; prof. of chemistry, Malta Univ., 1905-20; mem. Mediterranean fever comm., 1904-07; rector, Malta Univ., July, 1920; mem., exec. and leg. coun., 1920-21; hon. fellow, Royal Anthropological Inst., 1919; local sec. (Malta) of Soc. of Antiquaries, 1921; medallist (Mary Kingsley), Liverpool Schl. of Trop. Med.; delegate of Malta govt. at Imp. Educn. Conf., London, June to July, 1923.
ZIAI, MUSTAPHA FUAD.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; inspr., Cyprus mily. pol., Nov., 1906; judge, dist. ct., Apr., 1913; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.
